user_comment: Flattering & Fit Well But Color is Not as Depicted I purchased the Size: 6 | Inseam Length: 28 Inches | Color: Cape Town-waterless. They fit true-to-size, I am 5' 3" ~133 lbs and curvy. The fit was snug but flattering with a generous amount of stretch. The 28" length was perfect - right at my ankle. For anyone wondering, these fit very very similar to the pull-on version (which I also purchased) and the sizing is consistent between the two. In my opinion, they are equally as comfortable and you get real functioning front pockets with this version. I absolutely would have kept them if the color was as depicted in the photos. However, the color of the whiskering and fading is much more yellow and dingy in person and not at all white like the pictures. Overall, these are a really great value for a twenty-ish dollar price point especially if you find a color you like. I'm trying to lose the quarantine 15 and not looking to invest in expensive jeans until I do.

Fit comfortably I recently had barriactric weight loss surgery. I wasn't really sure of my size so I got a size 14. I am 5'7" and weigh 185. I still have a bit of a tummy. These fit so good. They move with you without feeling like you have to keep pulling them up. I am buying more because of the stretching I can lose more weight and still wear them.

Color much darker than shown Shopping in this pandemic without being able to try on is a nightmare. Love Amazon wardrobe, but the husband and I have both bought and returned so many jeans because of the color discrepancies in the stock photos vs what arrives. I ordered cape town waterless, but color actually looks like the darker color option. I’m 5’4”, curvy, and 150 lbs and the 6 fit pretty well. I got the mid length inseam and it’s too long so bunches at the bottom. But I’m long waisted, with short legs, so this length may work for others. Returning due to length and color.

Nice stretch! The stretch is fantastic, especially at the waist. My problem with most jeans is that to have them fit well at my booty/hips and thighs, the waist cuts me creating muffin top. These have great stretch at the waist eliminating that problem. My only complaint at this point is that while they stay put in front, I’m often tugging them up in back. I don’t have much booty to hold them up, I guess. They fit much lower in the back than the front, even at that. I’m short waisted, long legged (33in inseam I’m straight leg jeans), and the 14L is a good length for me.

Snags easily and long is not long enough When I first got these jeans I liked the cut and fit around the waist and they were comfortable to sit in and did not gap at the waist. What I did not like when I first got them was the length; I am 5’ 11” and ordered a long size, however the cuff was still a couple inches above my ankle. Not the length I was hoping for but I kept them because I liked the waist fit well enough. Having worn them for a couple weeks I’m really not a fan of these jeans. The material snags easily and little loops have pulled up all over my lap from my cat laying on me. She’s not scratching me or doing anything crazy, but if a claw is the least bit out then it will snag and pull up the jeans. None of my other jeans have ever had this happen to them so I am confident it’s something about the material of these jeans and not a flaw with all jeans.

ALMOST perfect I am 5'5" and about 150 lbs. I grew out of a size 3 a couple months ago but haven't found another size that fits super great, so I went for a 4 with these. They fit pretty much perfect, but were maybe a tad too loose for my liking in the upper thigh/crotch area. I washed and dried before my first full wear. Unfortunately they didnt tighten up at all after the first wash and dry which was a bummer. They seemed to be the type of jeans that get a little bit looser as you wear them throughout the day. Nothing drastic but noticeable to me. That's the only downside. The color is nice and fit is perfect for the most part, thin, flexible material.

Size 6 runs larger than typical Amazon Essentials 26 waist high rise Skinny Jeans I own several pairs of Amazon Essentials Hi-Rise Skinny Jeans jeans in sizes 26 - 27, and these seem to be sized and cut differently than my other AE Skinnies. For reference, I am 5'3", 117 lbs, and size 26 is typically the best fit. I believe that translates to size 2 - 4 in vanity sizing, and the pair I received is a size 6, so they feel quite roomy with a very relaxed fit throughout the legs. The color is beautiful, but they look oversized and baggy throughout the legs and especially the seat. The fabric is made with 3% Elastane, and they feel very stretchy. I prefer a second skin type of fit, and these would have been perfect in size 2, or possibly even 0 (pushing my luck). These are good to have on hand for the holiday feasting season in case my weight takes a dramatic upward turn in the aftermath of all those yummy desserts. These jeans are generously sized and I would recommend sizing down if you prefer a skin-tight fit. If you like having a bit of leeway, go ahead and order your usual size. ** If you found this review to be at all, please take a moment and click the button below to let Amazon know. Thank you so much 😊 **

Il prodotto è arrivato il giorno dopo l'acquisto, all'interno di una busta di plastica ben sigillata, recante il marchio Amazon e all'interno della busta di carta di Amazon. Indosso una taglia 48 e ho acquistato una taglia 48: veste perfettamente. Il jeans è a vita alta, copre l'ombelico, non risulta aderente, né ingolfante, ma accompagna dolcemente le forme. Sono alta 1.73cm, posso, quindi, affermare che il jeans resta aderente lungo le caviglie e le lascia scoperte di due centimetri, senza creare l'effetto "casa allagata". Il colore corrisponde a quanto indicato dalle foto. Unico neo: l'odore di plastica che proprio non se ne va. L'ho notato con tutti i jeans marca Amazon: servono numerosi lavaggi per fare attutire questo odore, che comunque resterà per sempre nel jeans. Ho la necessità di coprirlo con l'igienizzante spray. Questo a causa di materiali non proprio di grande qualità, infatti sono jeans che tendono a deteriorarsi facilmente.

Perfection ,and read for stats I'm a high rise convert, these are perfection! I'm small but have the muscle tone of Wonder bread plus I'm a 62 Y O grandmother. But in these jeans? I don't look it. I'm 5 7 @ 127 lbs - 29" waist and 39" hips and I bought the size 4 with 32" inseam which is More than long enough. I sew so non issue. My problem area is heinous muffin top if jeans not "just right" and many times I wear a Spanx cami to compensate for this. Not comfy in the south east summers lol. No Spanx needed with these. I'd always thought if I avoid my waist all together I'd avoid muffin top hence low waist line pants, I was wrong! These fit excellent in crotch which can also be another issue with me. The dreaded CT lol. heavy enough fabric that these are not see through, wearing black under things in this picture. Not blessed in the back of the house and these make the most of what I've got by being nice and snug without flattening what I have, great back pocket placement. Nice and higher but not too high up. I'd just tried on $200 white jeans yesterday at the mall but they were too sheer not to mention too expensive for what they were offering me. I'd pay if I had to but... Came home and went on Amazon, ordered Joe jeans at big $ and ordered low at this price. Ecstatic to Find these on my door step this morning and put them on to find my happy place in my fitting world. I can't believe the price of these and am ordering another because coffee guzzling me and white anything means I always order two if I love it lol.

The dreaded CT lol. heavy enough fabric that these are not see through, wearing black under things in this picture. Not blessed in the back of the house and these make the most of what I've got by being nice and snug without flattening what I have, great back pocket placement. Nice and higher but not too high up. I'd just tried on $200 white jeans yesterday at the mall but they were too sheer not to mention too expensive for what they were offering me. I'd pay if I had to but... Came home and went on Amazon, ordered Joe jeans at big $ and ordered low at this price. Ecstatic to Find these on my door step this morning and put them on to find my happy place in my fitting world. Leg length is ridiculous. Otherwise nice. This is the straight leg, not a boot cut. I am 5’6” and 122 pounds. I wanted to wear these with dress boots, but I would still be walking on the heel fabric. Otherwise, soft fabric, good fit and nice quality.

Not 32" inseam This denim is like butter. So comfy. Fits great. But i really need a 32" inseam. These are only 30". Ill give them a try in the wash but if they shrink at all I wont be able to wear them.

Overly stretchy mom jeans - buy one size smaller I'm a size L, 30 in waist, 32 in inseam. Sometimes I wear a 12, sometimes a 14. I purchased the Marilyn in size 12. It is too big. I should have purchased the 10. It stretches over 2 in at the waist. That is brand new. It has stretch so that wearing it over the course of the day, this pant will definitely fall off of me. Inseam is fine, a bit long (probably 33 in) but not a big deal because when I wear shoes it won't look long. The instructions on Amazon say to purchase a size smaller. Definitely do that. Weight of the fabric is heavy, winter weight. I don't think it will be comfortable outside in the summer. Honestly I would not purchase these jeans again - I like Madewell High Rise Alley jeans and Jcrew matchstick jeans better, they are more flattering. These are totally mom jeans. Rise is just below the belly button. I like the high waist, but with this much stretch, it kind of defeats the purpose. These have so much stretch that no matter what size you get, they will be baggy and ill fitting by the end of the day. You could wear a belt, but at that point you might as well wear a real pant. At least these are not the leggings that are made to look like jeans. If you buy these jeans, you will be tugging them up all the live long day. A pair of twenty dollar levis from target fit better than this. I give this product two stars. One star for good quality sturdy fabric and stitching, and one star for comfort. Attached are photos of how the jeans fit me. I couldn't photograph the tug at the waist with my phone - the jeans stretch out four inches with minimal effort. Too much!

As happy as I can be Ordered size 14, 5'7" / 202lbs and I don't love the way anything fits me at this weight. That being said, these jeans are not bad. They fit well, not super tight and not baggy. There is lots of stretch in the denim, so they are very comfortable even when sitting down. The denim is not thin so that it does not show every lump or bump. The waist goes to just at my navel, which means there is no muffin top. I posted a picture of the inside of the jeans front panel. I don't know that I look "thinner" but they don't make me look worse and thats a good thing. The jeans are long enough for 3 inch heels (I only had 2.5 inch heels on in the picture.) The denim is thick enough and has enough stretch to cuff them at the bottom to make them ankle length to wear with flats and still look cute. If someone wants the jeans to be tight and form fitting (for the lifting and tucking) I would suggest sizing down one size from usually size.

NYDJ Women's Marilyn Straight Jeans These jeans are crazy-comfortable. I wouldn't say that I suddenly appear to be a size smaller than I actually am, but I do like how they fit pear-me. The back waistline doesn't gap, which is very appreciated. Here's what makes me unhappy -- in several areas the stitching is inferior to several pairs of $25 jeans that are in my closet (hopefully the photo I'm adding actually appears). For the price (not the most costly jeans ever produced, but not cheap), the craftsmanship should be better. They're made in the US, so I like that and again, very comfortable, so that's important. By the way, I'm 5'4 and the jeans are too long for me by a few inches. Easily remedied at the local tailor's shop.

Rocking those 90's vibes! Where can I start? As a shorter person 5'3" I have major difficulties finding jeans that don't hang many inches below my feet. Not these gems from Aeropostale who offer lengths in short, regular and tall! That's something few jean companies do but all should. The color is that perfect medium/dark wash blue! And the quality is off the charts! You can tell these jeans will be a staple in your closet for years to come. These midrise jeans bring me back to my youth in the 90's when big (within reason) was in. They are super comfortable and at the sale price of $23 they are a steal! So order a pair or 3. They are super versatile and can be worn with a hoodie and sneakers or a blazer and heels. The one complaint I have is the slight gap in the back of the waist. But as someone with a naturally smaller waist and a bigger booty, this is an issue I frequently encounter. It can usually be helped with a good quality belt. All in all a great pair of jeans from a great company!

Pretty nice jeans I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

The best of the barrels For reference, I carry all my weight in my midsection so it's very difficult to find jeans that fit comfortably and don't show my apron belly. I typically wear a size 12 or 14 and am 5'1" tall, so length is also always an issue. I first ordered the $98 Free People barrel jeans in all the colors, hoping they would look cute. I tried one on and didn't even bother trying on the rest, I immediately returned them. Unless you're a size 2, the FP jeans were clownishly huge in the legs, but the denim was buttery soft. I am not typically satisfied with the quality of clothing on Amazon, but decided to try several barrel jeans because I wasn't having any luck from name brands. To my surprise, these fit GREAT! The fabric is a denim blend so not the same quality of designer fabric, but they are beyond flattering - comfortable stretch in the waist, slim through the hips, then a reasonable barrel flare with a tapered bottom. They skim the tops of my shoes so I have to cuff them up a little and they're perfect. Might hem them, but I hate losing the original stitching. I ordered 3 colors and love all of them. The Black is perfectly faded, the Deep Blue is a really nice medium blue, and I'm so happy they make the Retro Blue, which is just like the FP jeans with the slightest green tinge. I initially ordered a pair of barrel jeans from another seller, Grapent, and I kept them but the wash is not as nice, the pockets kind of stretch out, and these jeans are a better shape and quality of colors. If you're an apple shape and hard to fit, I really recommend these. I'm so happy to be able to buy into a trend for less than half the cost of the designer brands. What a find!!!

roomy, stretchy, so comfortable these are SO cute and the perfect price for trying this trend. my favorite silhouette for jeans is barrel and i feel it flatters pear shapes beautifully. it's also the most comfortable imo as your thighs/hips have room and the curved shape balances out my lower legs. i love the wash and the generous pockets. i ordered two sizes up but would've been fine with one size up because i'm bottom heavy & tall, but i'll just wash/dry to hopefully shrink them a bit because i love them too much to return! a belt would also work well but i'm actually a fan of how low they sit on my hips (we love body positivity around here) i'm 5'10, fluctuate between 190 and 200 lbs, very full on the bottom (34"ish waist, 47" butt/hips) and these hit just below my calf (perfect cropped length for me)

YES! THEY'RE THAT GOOD!! Just received the pants today, and I'm OBSESSED. These jeans are very close perfection in my opinion! For starters, I love the distressed look without having holes or looking TOO distressed. They look like something that would say Ralph Lauren on the label tbh. The fabric is soft (not a stiff denim), not too thin or too thick, and they have a nice amount of stretch to them. I'm usually a size 10 (but sometimes 12 depending on the brand). For reference, I'm 5'6" and 170 lbs. I ordered a size 12 because I don't like anything tight around my waist and I was concerned with them running small. The 12 fits nicely and I DO think that if I ordered a size 10, they may have pulled around the waist just enough to be able to see some of the white interior of the pockets (I hope that makes sense). The legs have just the right amount of "barrel" to them (not as barrel shaped as FP jeans). These jeans are PERFECT for anyone looking to try out the barrel jeans trend without committing to a full on barrel jean. I'd rate these jeans 10/10 and ended up ordering 2 more in other colors!!! WORTH EVERY PENNY!!!

Cute and comfortable, runs small I had low expectations for the price, but these are surprisingly flattering and comfortable. One caveat is that you need to size up. I usually wear a size 4 jeans so I ordered a size 6. They fit comfortably but are tighter in the waist and hips than my barrel jeans from Anthro. So if you want more of a baggy look at the top, you'll want to go up two sizes. I am 5'2" and the length is just right for me, so for a taller person they might be too short. The fabric is super stretchy and comfortable, color is good. They are cheaper looking than the Antro/Free People versions, but also $125 less. :-) Updated: after a couple of washings and frequent wear, the jeans are pilling. I have never seen denim pill before, so it must be from the quantity of viscose that makes them soft? I'm glad I have a pill shaver but just thought I'd mention it.

Super cute I was actually impressed with how much I like these! I’m 5’5 about 130 for reference and I got a size 6. They fit great and on the comfier/looser side, which is what I wanted. Docked one star because I think the photos make them look more like structured/stiffer jeans but the material is softer, which isn’t bad and prob why they’re so comfy. Definitely recommend!
The jeans are very comfortable with just enough stretch. For reference I’m 5’3” and 130 lbs and got a size 8. I went back and bought them in black.

Ordered a short I was looking forward to receiving these jeans. Overall they are comfortable and stretchy. I was searching for a petite boot cut and made a point of getting an explantion of shot verses petite. My understanding was that "short" was shorter than "petite". Fit well and are very affordable I'm 5'3", 134 lbs, with a 29.5" waist and 40.5" hip measurement. The size 6, 32 inch inseam, feels just right. This is my first time trying Levi's (I got a few different types) and I'm really pleased with the fit on all of them. These fit almost the exact same as the 315 shaping bootcut and they were half the price. If I had to pick between the two, I would probably go with the 315 but the price is right on these and I have no complaints. I see some people saying they are too long but I actually really like the length. I love the color on this pair (Blue Laguna-waterless). One thing I will note is that these came without any tags on them so I suspect that they were returned previously. They don't have signs of use besides that though so I'm comfortable keeping them, especially since they are well priced and fit very well.

As a petite woman standing at 5'3" and weighing 125 lbs, finding jeans that fit well has always been a struggle. But these jeans? They're great, especially for the price point. First and foremost, the fit is good. They hug my curves in all the right places without feeling too tight or restrictive. The stretchy fabric is incredibly comfortable, allowing for easy movement throughout the day. One of my biggest pet peeves with jeans is the dreaded "gap" at the back when I squat down or bend over. But not with these jeans! They stay perfectly in place, leaving no room for any unwanted exposure. If you're a fellow petite woman struggling to find jeans that fit just right, look no further. These jeans are a good choice.

Run big and stretched out a lot I was ready to love these jeans because of the shape and style, but unfortunately I’m stuck with a pair of jeans that stretches out too much and looks too big after just one wear. I’m 5’7, 180lb, 33” waist, 45” hip - based on the size chart first I ordered size 14, they fit somewhat big and didn’t “suck me in” at all, so I exchanged them for a size 12. At first 12 fit pretty well, comfortable, just loose enough but not sloppy or baggy. However after just one wear, they stretched out too much and are not as flattering as I would like them to be. I should have sized down at least to a 10. As far as the quality - these jeans are well made, no complaints. I feel like the color is somewhat different in person, definitely has a “vintage” look to it. Not a deal breaker just a matter of personal preference. Bottom line - if it wasn’t for the sizing, these jeans could have been a good find.

Real jeans; no stretch material (spandex) thin or light weight I have been looking for REAL jeans since 2006 when everyone started adding spandex so they stretch out and fall down after an hour of wearing. These are thin or light weight so they are not stiff like mens jeans tend to be. They sit on the hip, waist line so they are great with a short sweater or t shirt. They are "stove pipe" legs, which I love and Express used to make. 100% great jeans! The medium blue (true blue jeans color) is what I ordered and I just ordered 2 more in case they stop making them. I am 135lbs and 5'3" and I bought medium. MAYBE...an inch too long but I wear heals and when I put them on the jeans just brush the floor at my heal. I saw a shitty review for these but bought them anyway because it said they were thin and I was looking for thin (I HATE thick, stiff jeans) I will probably order a few more and put away for safe keeping in case I never find good jeans again

Done with Gloria Vanderbilt I am 5'10" tall, 150 lbs. My waist is 31, my hips are 39, my upper thigh is 23.5, my knee is 16.5, my calf 14.5, and my inseam is 34.5. I ordered an 8 long. (My legs are large, so these are the closest I can realistically get to a somewhat skinnier, non-baggy look.) This is online shopping, ladies: let's help each other out with stats! My local stores don't carry tall jeans, so I can only order online, and I need all the help I can get choosing! Buyers of this Levi style say the denim fabric is thin - it certainly is - and it is probably the reason it does not cut into my waist like the Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda classic straight jeans, which I wore in my 50's, do now. I am switching to this Levi style now I've entered my 60's; my waist is bigger and more sensitive to pressure from stiff and heavy fabrics than it was in earlier stages of my life. For someone my height, the pair I ordered in 8 long covers my ankle bone, so my socks won't show when I wear shoes. This is all I was looking for in that regard. Bottom line: Yes, the waist runs big - hallelujah! - and there is some stretching out, but, I personally have never ever owned a pair of jeans that hasn't stretched out over a day of wearing, so this is not a deal breaker for me. Yes, the denim is thin, so if I want to wear these and be warm here in Michigan in winter, I will have to wear tights under them if I spend any significant time outside, which, I rarely do. I also wear a very long coat, so I expect to be fine regardless.

Fit Like a Glove So I purchased a 2L because I have long legs. They are long enough that they hit the top of my feet and half of my heel. I have a thick heel though. lol. I weigh 163 right now. Even if a gain or lose a pound or 2…they will still fit fine I’m sure. My waist is usually 28inch but I sometimes go for smaller waist sizes for the nice cinch it can create. Due to what I read in the reviews before I purchased I opted to try a size 2 long. These jeans feel like a super stretch light material. Like a begging but heavier. Not too light that it doesn’t hold but feels good and comfortable. It almost covers most of my belly button and covers my little booty. I love them. I was a little worried when I pulled them out the bag but I’m pleasantly surprised and pleased when I tried them on. They need to make more colors!

Surprisingly impressed! Love the fit. Signature jeans are a little thinner and more generous than normal Levi's. These fit really well. Nice length. I'm usually an 8 in other Levi's but in Signature I easily get away with a 6L. I'm 5ft 9" and generally need a 34" inseam on straight leg jeans. My waist is currently 32-33" and my hips are 43-44". Hopefully that helps someone more than the usual height and weight which tells us nothing about weight distribution! 🩷

Wrong length I ordered an 18 Short (28 inseam by online description) the sticker says 18S W34 L30 How do I get a short inseam jean? I am 5’2”. The jeans are great otherwise It seemed that every Jean I grabbed to try on said L30. Now I’m wondering if the specs sent overseas were not followed correctly. I’ll try regular Levi’s next.

Nice fit and quality jeans, follow size chart These straight leg jeans by Levi's are high-quality and flattering. Great fit, and comfortable too. I am 5'7", size 8-10, 152 lb (37"/31"/40"), and ordered these jeans in a size 8 per the size chart. The size 8 fits me perfectly so, if you are between sizes, you may want to size a little bit down. These jeans are definitely high rise and come up all the way to my belly button. They fit me exactly perfectly all the way down: not too tight anywhere, but not baggy and loose either. The fit around the butt area isn't the most flattering, but it does me OK. The length is also perfect on me, perhaps on the long side. I also appreciate that the fabric is substantial and the pockets are real. The front pockets are not very deep, though the back pockets are sufficiently large. The fabric is quite substantial compared to other jeans, but still soft enough not to be irritating. Overall a really nice pair of jeans from Levi's if you're looking for straight leg.

I’m a dude, and these are the perfect 90’s jeans. I just wanna preface this written review and say that yes, I’m a dude, and believe that this is a great 90’s look jeans purchase regardless of your gender. The men’s ones out there are just horrendous and I’m tired of buying and then returning (Amazon and in-store like Levi’s or GAP), and the ones from Temu never fits me because of the horrible sizing. I’m 5’9 with a size 30 waist and an athletic build (I wear size L or XL depending on brand for men’s shirt). After numerous research, I find that there some dudes out there still that still swears by women’s jeans only. Since why not? I used to wear tight skinny women’s jeans back in my emo-phase days in the early 2000’s anyways, so I decided to check out this baggy version that is in-style nowadays as I really like the style. I ordered a large and they fit okay, I could’ve sized down to a medium though, but the large is still good with a belt on. These are stretchy, lightweight, and comfortable. However, just like the women’s jeans back in the day when I used to wear the skinny ones, I’m still disappointed that women’s jeans doesn’t have a big pocket like the men’s jeans do. Why is that? Oof. I wish that would change as some of the ladies out there have these mega tablet-like size phone, and with my iPhone Pro Max, it was sticking out and when I sit down, or shift to a weird angle, they slide off. Same thing for my wallet. Overall though, these are the exact jeans I wanted and highly recommend.

Versatile jeans, loose legs without being frumpy Great jeans! I’m 5’1” 145lbs so a little stocky, and bought a medium. I thought the waist fit was a tad small but once I wore them a while, it relaxed a little and now fits well - though I probably won’t run them through the drier in case they shrink. I love the high waisted style - the loops are large enough to fit a nice belt and ALL pockets are actually pockets ☺️ I have to hem them a little since jeans are usually long on me which I don’t mind. I like how you can get a more loose flaired end at the bottom or cuff them for a more tapered look (see images). I appreciate the looseness of the legs while still making my behind look good and not frumpy. Just know that these don’t have any stretch to them, and if you have lower belly fat like me, this won’t do a great job of flattening/hiding it.

sizing might be dependent on which color you choose i am 5'11", 145lbs. I wear size 29 pants ~9 in US sizing. if you're tall you know shopping for pants online is always a gamble where more than half might end up as capris or high-waters. I am pleasantly surprised with these. these are the first pair jeans not labeled as TALL that actually make it past my ankles. I got large and they are a bit loose instead of being tight like I was expecting from the reviews. material is okay. it's really thin denim so it's very comfortable but I have my doubts if it'll hold up over time. button feels secure, large pockets w really smooth fabric on the inside.

Baggiest jeans I've worn in a long time, but FUN, received compliments, fairly comfy! I've been wearing skinny jeans and straight leg jeans for a long time, so I decided to 'step out' with these Ripped Baggy Wide Leg Jeans! They are regular denim, non-stretchy. I ordered my usual size 4 and they fit pretty well. For reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#. The length is perfect, the waist is pretty good (very high-waisted) although a little snug, and the butt fits okay (a little loose). They are baggy and wide from the upper thighs all the way down to the wide bottoms. I'd say the legs are super loose and comfy, but the waist is what is snug since it's so high-waisted. The amount of rips is perfect, and I think necessary to off-set the bagginess and amount (lots) of material that these jeans have. I was able to stay in them all day, although would have been more comfortable if the waist was not so snug. I love the way these jeans look in the front, but do not love the way my butt looks. I guess since there is no design on the back pockets and since the butt is loose, it makes my big butt look big :(. I'm giving these jeans 4 stars as I wish the waist was slightly looser to be more comfortable, but I wish the butt was tighter to look better. Glad to own these fun new jeans though! My teenage daughter saw me wearing these jeans and said, "What are those? You are turning into me!" (And she approved!) Thank you!

Teen daughter likes these My teen daughter is 5’4”, 118 lbs, and has 27.5” waist. She wears size small or 4 pants. She got these jeans in size medium and she’s happy with the fit. The waist is snug but it is not too tight. The hips and legs have a wide relaxed cut. The pants are a bit too long for her to wear with flat shoes so she usually folds them up unless she’s wearing heels, platforms, or bulkier sneakers. There is no fabric content tag. The fabric feels like a quality heavier type of denim with a very small spandex addition. These jeans have a little give but they are not stretchy. The stitching is nice on the exterior and has some small issues on the interior but none are bothersome and don’t compromise the durability of stitching. There was also a piece of plastic foil stitched under the exterior waistband, perhaps a leftover of some kind of a tag but I was able to rip it out.

The thighs and legs are a roomy cut, so if you like a sleeker look in the thigh, these aren’t it. Great for thick-waisted, perimenopausal/menopause body changes 49yo, 5'3", 185lbs, Style: Sun Worshiper, size 14. I'm presently in the throws of perimenopause and at my heaviest. These are the only jeans that flatten my tummy area, hiding a small apron depending on underwear with minimal muffin top. I'm self conscious about the weight gain and feel most comfortable in these jeans. The inseam sizing is off as I initially tried on 14S but they had an inseam is 27 so I went with 14. I took photos without a shirt for transparency of how they fit on someone thick waisted like myself. With a shirt you wouldn't be able to detect the waistline. The slimming tummy panel hides any fat rippling. These are jean material and have the same weight and durability as a regular pair of jeans versus jeggings. Once I lose weight I will size down as the tighter my midsection gets then the slimming tummy is more effective.

I got mine just delivered and tried it on and so far, I like it. I was looking for some really comfortable pull up jeans that I could slap on the go. My regular jeans were suffocating me so I was really going for comfort. I was scared to get it because there were some splitting reviews. I got the size 4 and inseam 30. I'm 5'4" (163cm) and 130lbs (~60kgs). They are about the perfect fit and size for me, a tad bit loose on the calves so giving a loose skinny look. (I prefer the slightl loose compared to the skin tight jeggings look) They do give me a bit of a muffin top but I don't really mind as long as they don't either slip down as I walk around or squish me breathless. No slipping noted as I walked around. I had on regular skinny jeans right before (wax jeans size 3 - 26" 27" waist) and they are really similar at the waist. Band feels nice and secure and it didn't seem to give me the weird crotch. I will see how it wears after a wash but so far so good.

Omg in love I ordered according to the size chart, I wear a size 2, usually need long length but went with 30 because skinny need to be ankle usually. I tried them on immediately, and I could not be happier!! The dark red is more plum, even my picture looks red, but they seem more purple to me, and that's what I was hoping for! The green are also more muted, but still pretty. They are higher than many of my high waisted jeans, these hit right below my belly button, but I have a long torso so high is never high enough! I'm 5'7", 125ish, athletic build, with a booty. I get the reviews saying they run small, but I don't think they're quite right, they are a challenge to get on if you have any thighs and a butt, I mean very very hard to get on! But once up they're perfect, and I don't think more stretch would be as good for how these look. So just be ready for a battle every time you have to pee or dress/undress! These are so flattering and nice and a thick material. I'm so glad I took a chance!

OMG THESE ARE THE BEST These just arrived today and OMG these pants fit me so perfectly I could cry!!! I have a hard time finding pants as it is because I am 5'5" with short legs so everything is always too long. Add in that my legs are thick and I've got a butt and we have problems. Well, I just had my first baby 4 months ago and have been in this awkward stage of nothing fitting. I've been wearing my maternity pants and compression leggings still because I couldn't find any jeans that fit. UNTIL NOW. These fit me perfectly... Seriously. They are just at my ankle, and having had a c-section I feel supported by the panel insert in the belly area. These are so comfy. I can't tell you how relieved I am that I found these and that they fit me... These may just be my jeans for the rest of my life, I'm definitely buying more pairs!!! I ordered these in black and the weathered denim looking ones and I love them!! I ordered size 10 with 28 inseam. Buy these!!!! Oh if you are reading this and are also post partum, I'm wearing a Maidenform Shaping thong. These are amazing and offer support for the belly postpartum.

Best jeans I have EVER owned! The fit: there is a meshy band (like heavy duty pantyhose) across the belly that completely eradicates my "Mommy" tummy. I have a little pooch, loose jiggle, and sometimes it buldges if the waist line pinches. The waist band on these secures and FIRMS my entire waist - no jiggles or buldges. Then there's my rear, which isn't so perky anymore. These grab my cheeks and pull them up - my butt isnt flat in these. My inner thighs are smoothed out, and cellulite is hidden everywhere.The fabric: they feel high quality, well made. Soft, but durable like a denim/canvas - and they stretch and give when I bend/move without loosing shape or stretching out of shape when I srand back up. I wore these to 3 childrens birthdays in one day and still felt so confident in them to rock to a friends party that night.Size: I am 5"11, 160, bought the black long in a size 10 and they fit great. ps - the long still barely hits my ankle bone, but at least thats better than capri length.These pants are magical and I am SSOO bummed the other colors are out of stock. I did order their recommended similar jeans (skinny, signature, totally shaping) but they zip - dont have waist band and dont lift my rear the same.

Generally wonderful, but read update I’m 5’10” and 220 pounds. After reading the reviews, my path was clear! Ordered size 18L to avoid looseness up top. BAM! These are BETTER than the reviews! Unsure I’m still trying to decide how I feel about these pants. The 12s were too big, so I purchased the 10s this time. For the most part, they fit well, though a bit snug in the waist (I do carry most weight in my stomach). They have enough stretch to be comfortable though. The back pockets are big, which would be fine (since I keep my phone in my butt pocket), except they’re big enough that it goes below the bottom of my butt and creates an odd look. I’m certainly not bootylicious, but these pants definitely make my booty look flatter than it is. If you have any chub in your stomach, these pants don’t look great with a tight/fitted shirt - you’ll want to pair with something looser. I have examples of two different shirts of reference. I love doing a French tuck with my tops (tucking the front into my pants) - it looks fine from the front, but from the side, the pants definitely accentuate my stomach pooch. Since measurements are when deciding to buy clothes online, mine are 40-33.5-39, 168 lbs. As you can see from the pictures, they’re definitely a longer pant - I’m just shy of 5’6” and they go just below my ankle bone. Super comfy, but I’m not sure yet if I’ll keep them, since I want to feel confident in whatever I’m wearing for work and these definitely accentuate some flaws and are a bit hard to match tops with.

Size chart confusing. A nice fitting pair of jeans, that are comfy are an investment for me. I'm all about quality, fit and comfort with jeans. I'll pay a higher price if need be. I'm somewhat hard to fit and picky when it comes to jeans. These were cute and the reviews were convincing. I'm a true size 4 in jeans and pants. 27 inch waist. I have narrow hips and pants usually creep down on me and I have to crank them as the day goes on, if I don't have a skinny belt on. These have an elastic band, and they feel like the'll stay "put." LOL Pay close attention when you read their sizing chart. Make sure your'e reading the correct chart for sizing. Either skinnys or boot cut, because both charts are different sizing. The first pair I ordered was a size 2 28' waist. Too small! Returned those and ordered a size 4 29" waist and those fit. I received the second pair promptly. I ordered 32" inseam. So, the manufacturer tag is marked 4L/waist 27 L32. Again, this varies from the size chart listed on the add. Other than the confusing sizing, they are cute,fit nice and comfy. The waist is elastic but isn't "overly" stretchy though. Somewhat light weight compared to other jeans, only moderate amount of stretch. Hopefully they won't get too baggy and stretched out the longer the wear time.

I'm somewhat hard to fit and picky when it comes to jeans. These were cute and the reviews were convincing. I'm a true size 4 in jeans and pants. 27 inch waist. I have narrow hips and pants usually creep down on me and I have to crank them as the day goes on, if I don't have a skinny belt on. These have an elastic band, and they feel like the'll stay "put." LOL Pay close attention when you read their sizing chart. Make sure your'e reading the correct chart for sizing. Either skinnys or boot cut, because both charts are different sizing. The first pair I ordered was a size 2 28' waist. Too small! Returned those and ordered a size 4 29" waist and those fit. I received the second pair promptly. I ordered 32" inseam. So, the manufacturer tag is marked 4L/waist 27 L32. Again, this varies from the size chart listed on the add. Other than the confusing sizing, they are cute,fit nice and comfy. The waist is elastic but isn't "overly" stretchy though. Somewhat light weight compared to other jeans, only moderate amount of stretch. Hopefully they won't get too baggy and stretched out the longer the wear time.

Love them, longs are 32 in length I love the color and fit. I’m 5’9 130 lbs, ( slim built) I ordered a size up , to a 6 longs. I’m glad I did because the waist band is tight when pulling up, even a size bigger, also I usually wear a 34 in length, so I expected them to be a little longer. They look so nice, so I’m keeping them.

Will you get what you ordered?? Let me start with I love Levi jeans, so when I saw these jeggings I had to get them. I ordered the sun worshipper and the black colors. The black arrived exactly as I expected, however the sun worshipper were definitely not jeggings. To which I am very disappointed. They fit, so I’ll keep them, but just be mindful of this. To the fit of the jeggings: oh sweet lord! They feel amazing! Everything you need in in a legging that looks like jeans. They slide on and hold up amazingly! And they are light weight, so perfect for work during the hot as heck summer in the south. I’m 5’3” and 118 lbs and the size 2 fits great. I opted for the 28 inseam so that I have a little bit of ankle showing when I wear my sneakers or sandals. I will definitely order more, even with the gamble of if you get jeggings or jeans.....what can I say, I like a little mystery in my life. lol

EVERYTHING THE REVIEWS SAID AND MORE! LOOK, NO MUFFIN TOP :) I bought these Levi's hoping they were half as good as the reviews said and they Really, Really are! Long story short, I went off my diet and I'm feeling blahhh! I had to go to a school function last night and "be seen"... so I pulled these out of the drawer, took them out of the bag and hoped for the best. I was alittle worried because they have a "pull on" waistband, no zipper or buttons and I thought they might look alittle "old". But as long as you wear a shirt, tunic, sweater that comes down long enough, your good. The inside tummy panel really works great. I went with the 12 Long (5'10 185lbs was 180lbs) and the fit & length are perfect. They're were alittle tight going on and it was a questionable fight for a minute, but I won. I'm wearing the Harmony style.. These hide any bit of muffin top you might have. If your a bigger gal like myself, I highly recommend trying these. They are flattering, the style looks good on and the price is right.The Measurements For 12L:17" Across Waistband,10.5" Front Rise,32" Inseam. They are exactly what I needed. I paired these with a layered tunic and ballet flats and I felt good and was comfortable all night. Definitely Buying More.

Happy Post Partum Mommy I bought these jeggings 2 months postpartum in hopes of wearing something other than leggings and pajama pants. Im 5’9” and 165lbs. I purchased a 10 long. Pros: the length was perfect. I like the fit, they we’re not too tight to creat a muffin top yet tight enough to feel supported. The material definitely feels more like jeans vs leggins. Great price! Cons: i wish the waistband went higher than my belly button. I think this would solve a lot of my issues. Because it hits right at my belly button i still have some extra belly that needs supporting and creates a small muffin top when i bend over. Also, minor issue but the top butt area doesn't provide support for me. My butt kind of bulges out and gives an unflattering extra curve. In summary i would definitely recommend these jeans for any mom post partum or Similar problem in the belly area. I definitely think the price is right for what you get. I am a happy customer because i didn’t over spend on jeans that may be a temporary solution to what i need. I plan on losing more of this postpartum weight and improve my diastasis recti so down the road these jeans will look better on me *crossed fingers*

Every short girls dream So I hesitated on purchasing jeans online bc im short and feel like my body is an odd shape, but I am so happy with these pants. This is my first size 10 pant which made me frown, but we can thank Covid to that. The pants feel and fit so amazing. Im 5ft2in and these fall at my ankle. I got the shortest inseam which I think was 28 on the options. I weigh 149lbs and intend on losing some wait but I feel like these pants would still be good if I dropped a size or 2 bc of the stretching of the material. Also, I love the dark denim, it doesn't have a weird scent after washing them like some pants do from dye. I am absolutely going to be purchasing a couple others. I do have muscular legs and tend to have issues with pants fitting my quads and these pants just have am overall comfort to them and I'm not constantly tugging my pants up.great quality.

Comfy/Versatile/Attracts Hair & Dust I love these stretchy, pull-on skinny jeans! They're very comfortable, and the short size is a pretty good length for my petite 5'1" height. If I were 1" or 2" taller, they would fit perfectly, but they work totally fine as is. The supportive wide waistband is a wonderful design for tummy flattening. Dealing with a chronic pain illness makes me want to wear comfortable clothes whenever possible. I'm often at home wearing pajamas, but for being out, these jeans are an awesome choice that mix comfort, fashion and efficiency. These jeans are perfect for just about any occasion, whether it be work, play, a date, or anything in between. They're rugged enough for hiking and soft enough for lounging. The fabric is very smooth and stretchy. The only problem is that the material easily attracts lots of fur and dust if you have pets that shed. These jeans aren't the most butt-lifting like most regular tight denim jeans are. They're also not as tight as jeggings although they're still skinny. But these jeans are still very flattering and form-fitting, and you can't beat the comfort level. I like these a lot! I think you will too.

Comfy and soft I like these jeans. They are soft and have the elastic waist band. The waist is higher, so they don't fall down when I bend over or sit. I also like the shorter length. Buy them!! I can't say enough good things about these jeans! They look and feel like real jeans, not like jeggings. They have a tummy control panel that comes down about 3 inches from the waistband. It completely flattens my fupa and lower stomach pooch!! And I can still comfortably eat in them. I will say, the more booty you have the more difficult it is to pull them up bc of the stomach compression part. I have 42 inch hips and thick, relatively muscular thighs. I got a size 8 in these which is average for me. I'm an 11 in juniors sizes, if that's useful. It takes some effort to get them over my butt, but once I get the compression fabric up over my thighs and butt I only have to do the *jumps while pulling pants up* once to get them the rest of the way up. 😂 The waistband is smooth and invisible under a thin t-shirt, whereas most jeans cause weird lines where the button and zipper are. They're stretchy enough that there's no gap at my lower back. They do have to be pulled up pretty regularly for me when moving around. I think I might also get a pair in size 10 to wear on more active days.

Nice Pants I usually don't leave reviews, but I'm starting to notice that some reviews don't completely help me. So hopefully I can help someone. I am 5'3", 180lbs. I ordered a 10s (short) and it is right below my ankles which is good. The pants itself is soft and comfortable. It does get lint but not too bad. I ordered the 10 because I did gain weight and size 8's have been a little snug, but these are looser than I like but that's not a big deal. I will definitely update the review once I wash these jeans to see if they will shrink. The band rolls down when I sit or bend down but that always happen to me with elastic waisted jeans. The pants sits below my navel, so if your looking for tummy control these are not for you. I personally do like the jeans, I will order more but in a smaller size.

Great Jeans for the Price I'm 5'5" and 150lb. Short torso and prefer high waist jeans. Usually wear around 10 to 12. Sizing chart for these was so confusing that I ordered an 8, 10 and 12. Went with the 10. Here's why: 8 - I knew by looking that they would not fit 10 - tight around belly, too tight at first. But butt and thighs fit good. 12 - Waist was much more comfortable, but butt and thighs had too much extra fabric. Wore the 10 all day and even went skating in them. Never sagged. Never got the "MC Hammer" crotch. The butt is still looser than I'd like, but it's not saggy. The waist was fine, even with sitting most of the day for work. Did not feel like I I had to pull them up after squatting down and standing up, although I still did this. Probably all jeans will do this unless they're jeggings. Have washed and dried (on low) twice and so far, size has not changed. Gave 4 stars instead of 5 because they just do not hug my little to no butt.

Fit Like a Glove These attract sooooo much dog hair and lint FYI but I like them. Tight in a good way. It’s very hard for me to find pants that fit so I’m shocked I got the size right the first time. It was hard to put on because there’s no button and I have a huge booty but once I squeezed them on they’re perfect. The waste band is a little too tight and uncomfortable to sit in all day at work, but I wouldn’t want to go up a size. Im 5’5” and 145lbs but have very large thighs and butt and legs in general, very small waist comparatively, I ordered a size 6

Sizing is inconsistent These feel great: comfortable, stretchy denim with real front and back pockets. I love that they come in long sizes that are long enough for me (5’10” and 170lbs)! I ordered 2 colors of the same size (10L), and the waist band was vastly different causing a completely different fit between the two. Unfortunately, I need something between them so they’re both going back. Otherwise, they’d be exactly what I’m looking for.

Comfortable but don’t get black if you have animals These pants are comfortable and stretchy. I was surprised by the fabric initially but I ended up liking it. I bought the black pair and was going to keep them until I spent two minutes in them and realized how much they attract dog/cat hair. Very good stretchy Jeans 100 stars!! I’ll start by saying I’m 350. I am being forced to wear jeans at work (outside construction) I’m used to leggings and sweats, but I love how much these jeans stretch because I have to do a lot of bending and I bought other jeans that start to fall once I start sweating and y’all know there’s no pulling up jeans in the heat so yeah I needed comfort and room to move. These have both and more. Extremely flattering, especially the color/wash I'm 5'8 with longer legs and I have an impossible time finding jeans in a 10 Long which fit me, since my waist is a full 13" smaller than my hips (30"/43"). And I cannot stand it when skinny jeans fall inches above my ankle, it drives me nuts. These gave me that relieving feeling of when a skinny jean just perfectly hits the top of my foot (without being super tight around the ankle) - perfection. Normally I avoid mid-rise jeans but the fact there was a 10L in stock, as well as the price, convinced me to take a chance. I'm so glad I did - these are incredibly flattering! I've never owned pull-on jeans before but that's changing fast now. Also the wash "Immaculate-Waterless" is the absolute perfect color and wash - not too dark, not too light, with just enough whiskering around the areas you need to create a sort of contoured shapely look. I do wish the rise was just one inch higher, but that's probably because my lower waist is quite drastically bigger than my natural waist so they're a tad tight. But I'm sure they'll relax a bit after a few wears.

Excellent for Tall ladies For starters, I’m 5’11, 125lbs, usually wear a 28 in jeans or a 4-8 depending on the brand. I was skeptical about these, I didn’t love the idea of pulling on jeans, however I have been pleasantly surprised. I ordered a size 6, with 32” inseam and they fit PERFECTLY. They are not soft like athleisure leggings, definitely a stretchy denim. They pull on easily, and provide a small amount of stomach support and flattening (but not uncomfortably tight). I have hips, a decent booty and large thighs & calves for my build. It makes finding skinny jeans difficult because they fit in the waist but are too tight on my thighs and claves, but these were comfortable and fit both thighs and calves well, without being too constricting. The other issue I run into as a fairly tall woman, is jeans length. The 32” inseam hit me EXACTLY where they need to, right at my ankle bone. Overall, I love these, and if they sold these in white, I would buy them immediately and they would likely replace my white Paige jeans.

No es alagador. Acabo de recibirlos. Decidí comprar este color porque se ve blanco en las fotos, aunque dice que es lila, así que me esperaba que no fuera del mismo color de la imagen ; además nadie había hecho una reseña para este color. Es cómodo y definitivamente elástico pero no esperes una elasticidad de leggins, estos son JEANS! Pero definitivamente se estiran más que los típicos Jeans que tienen botonadura, pero que también tienen cierta elasticidad (ver foto) No estaba posando en las fotos y me veo más gorda de lo que estoy dispuesta a admitir, pero, no se ven alagadores. Los voy a conservar porque puedo arreglar lo holgado de las piernas. Lo único bueno que puedo decir, es que de la cintura me queda perfecto, pero la cintura no estira casi nada, así que me costó mucho que pasaran por mis caderas y eso que no tengo caderas anchas ni trasero tan grande. Mis medidas (inch): Cintura: 35.5" Caderas: 41.5 Mi altura es 4ft 9ich (Sí, soy casi un pigmeo lol) Así que me quedan largos como se puede ver en las fotos, pero tiene arreglo.

I'm throwing away all other jeans! These are the BEST jeans I have ever owned in my life. I can never find a pair of jeans that fit right -- too big, too small, too long, too low, always falling down etc... These are perfect. I am 5'2" and 135-140lbs. I carry a bit of bulk in my bottom and thighs. I've had 4 kids so have a mama pouch that I needs tamed. I don't tuck shirts in so the elastic waist doesn't bother me at all. It will occasionally roll down in half if Ive been sitting a while but for the most part is high enough to be slimming but not so high that it acts like the bottom part of your bra. Length? I purchased the 4-Short and for someone with short legs, it's a perfect length. This is unheard of for me! The jeans are soft with a perfect amount of stretch. I have gone from hating to wear jeans to now owning 3 of these pairs. If you are a short, curvy lady..... don't hesitate to purchase!!

Shapes You, Yet Still Reasonably Comfortable These jeans fit pretty well and shape you in a flattering way. I tried both the short and medium length and didn't see a big difference, but I'm 5'3." I weigh 128# and ordered the 6 -- I was wondering if I might need an 8. Other reviewers complained that if they went big, the butt was saggy. In my case, the waist is tight, but reasonably comfortable -- same can be said for hips and thighs and basically all the way down to my ankles. The part that bothers me after a long day is actually the crotch! I wonder if this is because I have a larger behind and thighs (you can see in the photo that they pull in the thighs) and would get more crotch room in the 8 without the sag others complained about. I may try an 8 and see. I also have not washed these yet, so if that changes the fit, I'll update.

Love for postpartum Love these jeans! I was looking for an elastic waist jean for postpartum as my pre pregnancy jeans were still a little tight, but going up a size was too big. These are perfect. They are snug in the hips and waist to stay on, have a thick band, but fit perfectly everywhere else. Now that I’m back down to my pre-pregnancy size, these still fit snug and are my favorite jeans. I am normally a size 4 in American eagle jeans, I am 122 lbs and 5’4”. I bought the Size 4, 28 inseam, standard pull on in Harmony-waterless. I want these in every color!

These pants are INCREDIBLE!!!!!!! I never write reviews, but these are easily the best jeans I have ever owned in my life and I had to share!!! They fit perfectly in every dimension to the point where I can't believe these aren't custom made for me. I'm 5'11" medium build so I ordered the 8 long which is my normal jean size. I have them cuffed in the photos so you can't tell, but the length is ideal for my legs which is so rare for me. They are incredibly comfortable without sacrificing the look of real jeans. The tummy panel is FANTASTIC. I'm bloated right now from travel in these photos, but these jeans would never spill that secret. Plus the lack of buttons/zippers makes for a very sleek silhouette. They also fit beautifully around the butt due to the stretchiness of the pants, but aren't so elastic they flatten it! On top of all of that these are more comfortable than my leggings. I'm in love with these pants and will be purchasing the rest of the colors to replace every other pair of jeans I own.

I have to return this Honestly, I'm a little bitter that I have to return these. I am 6'0" tall, 145lbs and on the slimmer side so I went for the 4L and it said long so I expected....long. The inseam is barely 30 inches, I thought it was 32"; I even brought out my measuring tape to check. I wanted to return and order a longer version but they don't have that so I'm stuck with feeling bummed out. If anyone has similar specs to me but is slightly shorter, I recommend getting a size 6 because although this fit me just right, I have a sneaking suspicion that these jeans are the type to shrink when washed.

Very comfy! But, will give you that mommy tummy look So these are AWESOME... but mostly when standing. Sitting down, the band really crushes into your abdomen and causes some serious FUPA action. I usually wear the Express perfect lift jeans and don't have this kind of issue. The Express jeans have the thick waistband feel on the entire front to hold your mommy tummy together, but on these only the waistband is thick and holds you together, and the rest of the fabric is thinner, thus the mommy tummy getting pushed out under the waistband for some unattractive bulging. But, very cute on the butt, legs and an overall slim look, just wear with a forgiving shirt if you'll be sitting a lot. I’m a size 8, 5’3” and 145lbs.

Most comfortable stretch jean! Beware these do seem to run big. I really love these Levi stretch jeans. I have other Signature by Levi Straus jeans that I have purchased on Amazon. They all fit amazing. I am always a 10 short so that’s the size I ordered. These are definitely comfortable but are a bit longer in length and not tight around the ankle as shown in picture. I think maybe I could wear an 8 short in this style. I am considering buying another pair in the smaller size because I like this style and I am really curious to know if they run slightly big. Overall, It is a great stretch jean.Update on 11/25/19...I ordered the 8 short and am wearing them today. Not truly happy. I thought sizing down from a 10 to an 8 would reduce the bagginess and that the ankles would have the skinny fit as shown on the model. And no there is no “skinny” to these jeans. I attached a picture of the 8 short to let you see where the are baggy in the thighs and ankle. Don’t get me wrong, these are very comfortable but I wanted the look portrayed on the model. ☹️ And I do love the Levi Strauss& Co. brand.

Great if you get the right size I ordered the black 6M. I’m 5’ 5” and 145-150lbs and 6 months post-second-baby. I’m currently wearing a size 8 or 10 jean. But for some crazy reason I ordered a 6 in these. *face palm* These were way too small for me. Had I ordered my real size I probably would be wearing them right now instead of returning them. So they are made to fit tight and suck you in - almost like compression leggings around the waist. So if you order the right size, these are probably a dream. I didn’t get photos of the ankles, but they are slim, but not TIGHT around the ankles. The material is stretchy but thick. They don’t have the feel or look of leggings. I thought they felt and looked more like black khakis instead of denim. I would say more about the rest of the fit, but it was all pretty bad because of my poor ordering. If I reorder in my size, I’ll update the review.

Buy them! Great pant! Comfortable without being sloppy. I am 5'9 and have butt and thighs for DAYS and a little belly. These pants fit perfectly. I am always nervous to order jeans or anything comparable, especially online but I took a chance and I'm glad I did, they look and feel great! I ordered the longer length and they hit right above my ankle. The waist comes up just past my belly button, it could stand to be a little higher but I could also stand to lose a couple pounds and then it wouldn't matter. I've only been wearing them for about an hour but I haven't had to yank them up yet, which is great news if you're a big booty girl like me. I usually have to yank up jeggings constantly. Nice to find pants that fit nice and aren't leggings or classic jeans. Buy them, you won't regret it!

Amazon Size Chart Is Incorrect According to the Amazon Size chart a waist of of 28 inches is a size 2. I am 5'3 and 122 lbs, and I did use a tape measure to determine my size. When I received these pants, the size on them says 26x28. I chose a size 27 length. So obviously the size chart provided isn't correct. Hopefully they will get the size chart corrected. (I do plan on notifying them of it.) If not, I would recommend ordering a size up. Luckily these jeans do still fit me, but they are a little more tight than I would like, and I have a little bit of a muffin top with them. I went with the 27 inch length which I think looks cute, but if you want the jeans to go to your shoes I would go with a longer cut. I'm short so it's rare for me to find pants that don't sag to the ground. When wearing a shirt that isn't tucked in, the muffin top isn't visible. I ordered 2 pair of these jeans in different colors, but I might return one for a size up.

Beware....Size chart in the description on website does not match the true size. The sizing chart is wildly off from the actual sizing you receive. 18 Long according to the size chart should be Waist: 39 1/4" - 40 1/4" Length: 34" However, you can see that the 18 Long that I received is W 34" and L 32" which is more in line with a size 12 Medium Length on the size chart. That's a big discrepancy. If it was only one size down, I would just exchange them but I don't have the energy to keep ordering until I find the correct size. I'll buy jeans somewhere where I can try them on. What a disappointment!

Love the material & style but sizing is way off To begin with my measurements: hips 47 inches and waist is 37.5 inches. 5'5 feet tall at a whopping 190 lbs. I am boldly sharing my photos of my muffin top with you all. I hope the visual helps xD I ordered size 16 with inseam 30 (no option for short, regular, or long, but would have bought short if prompted). So these clearly ended up far too small. Getting them on was a battle. I had to do a combination of jumping and wiggling to pull them up. Even harder getting them off. I've got size 12 short Maurices jeans that fit better than these (although those are too small as well). Last year I bought size 14 short pull on jeans from Belks that are now too small. I thought maybe I am a size 16 now since I gained weight. I was so hopeful for these. My fiancée doesn't think they're worth returning, since I'm on a weight loss journey, but I wanted jeans that fit now rather than later. I wish I knew what size I need to order, sizing chart appears to be no help. I will say though, the material is very soft and stretchy (I did still get them on haha). I think these could be great with the proper size. I'm not sure in my case if one size higher is best or even two sizes higher.

Good fitting jeggings. Look like real jeans. My jeans are all a bit tight after I gained 10 pounds so I have been on the look out for jean-like leggings that didn't look like too much like fake jeans. Not an easy thing to find. These No Nonsense Women's Denim Legging actually look like jeans. They are not thick and stiff like jeans but they have good texture and coloring. I like that, if I roll them up, they have jean-like coloring on the inside so they look very much like a real pair of jeans. I am 5"5", 139 pounds and got size medium. I also tried the size small and they were just too tight around my gut to be comfortable. The mediums fit really well around my stomach, hips, butt, and legs. I do have thin legs so these don't cling super tightly like a pair of high Spandex leggings but that's fine. That makes them look even more like real skinny jeans. The length is good and they seem to be made well. I will probably buy a pair or two more. They're not warm but definitely not see through or flimsy. If they were just a bit thicker for cooler weather, they would be just about perfect. They are the most realistic looking jean leggings I have found so far.

Tears easily I was actually surprised this was my fav pair to try of the 3 different brands I ordered. They’re super comfy. I’m muscular in the legs and am 5’0 tall. I do NOT put my hands in my back pockets much with an occasional phone, which is in my right back pocket. The left has started to tear at the corner already, and I’ve worn these a handful of times, mainly at work for casual Fridays. I can sew them back with reinforcement, though it’s only been 6 weeks since I bought them. I’ll be ordering another pair. The hole is the only reason it’s a 4 star.

Better than expected! So first off, I weigh 180, 5’6” and usually wear a10/12 in jeans. I ordered a large and they fit perfectly. I was concerned when I read the waist is below the belly button- that was not the case. Waist hit right where it was supposed to. Super comfortable! Color is close to jean material, my hubby thought they were jeans. Only complaint would be I wish they were longer, but I do have long legs so it could just be me. Haven’t washed them repeatedly yet so can’t remark on durability. Bottom line I will be ordering more!

Not bad I’m 11 weeks post partum and struggling to find clothes I feel comfortable in. Pre-pregnancy I was 115 lbs and a pretty standard size 4 in bottoms and small in tops. I’m right at 135 now and unable to fit in most of my clothes, but have been hesitant to spend too much knowing ultimately I’d like to lose some weight. I’m probably between sizes 6 and 8 now depending on the brand and material. I am 5’4” for reference. Best guess on my current bra size is a D. Sharing all this just to make it clear, I don’t really have a “normal” to use as a size comparison right now. Also offering up having a newborn as an excuse as to why my mirror and room are such a mess lol. After reading reviews I ordered a medium, and I’m glad I did. I think I would have struggled to squeeze into a small, and a large would have been too big. I know not to expect top of the line quality from most Amazon clothes, especially in this price range. The quality was about what I expected, not terrible but obviously not top of the line.

The small is too loose for xsmall wearers I'm 5'2" & at the time of the photos, I was 26 waist 36 hips & around 107 lb. I bought this pair in 2019 & wore it for about 3 years before it became very faded. It is very stretchy, it's flattering except the pockets are kind of narrow & longish? makes your butt look like it is slimmer but longer lool. It's not durable, don't expect to last more than a couple of years, depending on how much you wear & wash it. Back then when I wore this pair the most, I was around 115 - 125 lb & it became my go to pair for everything. I would say, I only recommend this pair for people over 115 pounds, otherwise it would be too lose so if you wear xs, the small will probably be too loose. I hope it helps.

Here's the scoop on these jeggings I'm 5' and 127 lbs, waist 33" and hips 38", and I ordered both medium and small. The small was way too small, but the medium fits very well. The waist of the jeggings comes up to my belly button, with no muffin top. Even when I lose a few pounds, they'll still be nice and snug. I just got them, so I don't know how they'll wash, but I'm not worried about it, even if they end up shrinking a little. I'm planning to dry them on medium heat, because I don't want to weaken the spandex. Unless they end up being flimsy, or have other unexpected issues, I recommend these jeggings. To answer some questions that other shoppers asked: - The blue jeggings look like jeans and have the orange stitching in the seams to look like regular jeans. The black jeggings have black stitching. - They both have rear pockets, with false front pockets. - Yes, they are long on me by several inches but, so what? It won't matter if I'm wearing boots, and I'll just tuck the bottom under with other shoes. - Both colors have decent fabric thickness (not real thick, but good enough), and it feels soft and comfortable. They are not see-through (don't know about the white jeggings). - I tried them on with different types of underwear, and I think that if you wear properly fitted panties (not too tight), there shouldn't be a panty line.

Sturdy, nice dark jegging denim I liked the dark denim. True to size. I’m 5’4, 200+ lbs. This waist band is sturdy but comfy & goes up enough. I liked I could fold at bottom for that casual look. Thick enough not to be flimsy, but thin enough to be comfy. I was cautious & bought 1 for now. I’d buy another👍🏼

Perfect affordable pregnancy pants! These are seriously the best "pants" ever. I usually only wear dresses and leggings (blue jeans are SO uncomfortable), but I wanted something to wear tshirts and sweaters with. I have these in a medium prepregnancy and they fit perfectly (for reference I was between 135-140 pounds, 5'5", and a size 6). I wanted comfortable pants to wear during my pregnancy and even with maternity pants the crotch area would be too tight and cut into me, and with how much my hips spread my thighs felt too restricted (not to mention that maternity clothes cost a small fortune). So I took a gamble and ordered these 2 sizes larger and the XL are PERFECT! (Hoping I can get a little more wear out of them postpartum until I can get my body back) For reference, I am 36 weeks pregnant, 5'5", size Medium in Motherhood Maternity/Old Navy Maternity, and 190 pounds. Most of my pregnancy weight is in my belly and hips. P.S. The length is good too, fits about the same as the mediums. There is a little bunching around my ankles but nothing bothersome, I've worn both tennis shoes and boots comfortably with these.

NOW THIS... Is a home run buy!!! LOVE LOVE LOVE!!!! THESE ARE FANTASTIC!!!! Totally different from the"classic" pair I bought and gave away ,, in the light denim. There is a HUGE difference in the CLASSIC and the this one that just says " no nonsense woman's legging" . Beautiful stretch denim material. Light and nice texture... They sit a tad below my belly button ( I am short waisted). FIT IS PERFECT...Hug you in a all the right places. No uncomfortable wad of fabric In the crotch. Ladies you know what I mean! No rubbing. I am 5' 4 106 lbs. , I got the smallest. I was SO HAPPY right out of the box. :):) The length is perfect, and if you want to roll the bottom up a bit with a casual shoe it looks like real jeans, except you won't want to take them off the first chance you get. LOVE THEM> The waist band is the perfect size too... doesn't dig in . Very smooth silhouette. These can be casual or dressed up . They have a deep and generous back pocket. My IPhone 7s fits nicely without it popping out. Compared to the 'classic" ones...these have no brass button ( which i prefer) and I still can't believe the difference in the fit. I LOVE THEM>>>>going to get more as they have a great selection of colors and patterns! I also bought a pair of the capri's in pink and they too are awesome. Same great fit and no button.Nobody would know these are 'jeggings"... they look like jeans , and I am sick of spending big bucks on uncomfortable jeans!SO CUTE!!! *addendum I wore this today in the car , 90 degree heat , runner no around doing errands etc. I am happy to NO SAGGING OR BAGGING ! awesome!☺️

Get the sizing right! I ordered a large and should have ordered a medium. I am returning these but for nothing that is wrong with the legging itself, just a misjudgment in sizing on my part. I am 5'5 154lbs and usually wear 30/31 or size 10 pants. I have belly and hips but not really any booty or leg meat SO! that being said, I can't speak for other woman in the same height/weight class as I, but for me, these fit loosely with a bit of sag that just didn't look appealing. I think getting the size right makes all the difference in this particular brand of legging, so hopefully this short description and pics will help. No other issues - nice material, very comfortable, the waist didn't pinch the skin, seemed well constructed and I may just order these again except in the right size. ;-)

Great Quality and Looks Like Real Jeans I have 41" hips and a 37" waist. Ordered the Large in black. The fit well. A little baggy around the front fly area and the knee and lower leg, so I might have even been able to go MD but didn't want to risk that "too stretched out" look - where you can see the glimmer of spandex - not a good look. These are very very long. As you know, all women are supposed to have the same length legs, so that's why we don't have different length pants, but I unfortunately have very short legs. I'll have to have about a good 6" chopped off of these, but that's nothing new. These are nice quality, thick, REAL BACK POCKETS!, nice stitching that looks like real jeans (deep yellow thread). 100x better than ones I had purchased at Walmart (Faded Glory brand maybe?) where they almost always have that stretched out look where you can totally tell they're not jeans.The black is borderline black - could almost pass for just a really dark wash denim, which is fine by me.Absolutely worth the price!

Happy! I'm happy with this purchase. These are medium weight and don't feel thin or flimsy. Color is classy dark denim. Comfortable! I'm very happy with the value for the price. I am buying these because I need more stretchy clothing for my post partum body for the summer. I think the only thing I might not like is that the waist is rolling down, and then feels a little tight, but I think that's mostly because of my baby belly. I"m 5'-10", 190 lbs, and measure 44" around my butt, and the Large fits perfectly. They are also nice and long! I rolled them up for more of a capri look, but they roll down very long. With no time to shop, I'm turned to amazon for my summer wardrobe, and I'm excited to wear these with some of the cute tops I've purchased on amazon!

Called my sister immediately! As soon as I got these, I tried them on and as soon as I tried them on I called my sister to rave about how great these are! I got a medium. I got 1 black 1 dark denim (that one has not arrived yet, black is in picture). For reference, I am 5'4", 140lbs (and have a bubble butt). They come up to just below my belly button. These are so comfortable. They feel and look just like jeans on the outside but ohh baby they are leggings on the inside. Perfect amount of stretch without being see through. Hugs your curves in all the right places. I can sit, stand, squat, all with ease and comfort! I have longer legs and they just start to bunch near the ankle, but just like jeans do, so if you are shorter, they may need to be folded over. I would absolutely recommend these to anyone who loves jeans looks, but leggings comfort!

They're okay, not the best, but okay These are not as I expected. I have a pair from another retailer that look like actual jeans, but feel like leggings (and of course they don't sell them anymore), and I was hoping these would be the same. These are clearly leggings, they have a white cloth cast underneath. The pair I received had a pinhole kind of... run? You can see that in the picture. But they are very comfortable! I won't ever wear these out for an event or day out, to work or anything like that, but I will wear them around the house or for a quick run to a store or something, and look somewhat put together. And so for that I'll probably order another pair. They're also very long on me. I'm about 5'6 and they would go all the way to my toes if I didn't pull them up. I'm a 12/14/16 (depending on the pants) and ordered a an XL. They're slightly loose, but I would be afraid to do a L and risk them being too tight.

They're ok. I thought I ordered a large but evidently I ordered a medium. It fits though! I'm 5'5 and about 180lbs. I typically wear a size large at like Walmart or Target and a size 10 in stretchy jeans. I'm at that weird point where a 12 is too big and a 10 is kind of snug. I will say I do like these but but honestly $18 is too much. I will keep these because I am satisfied enough. They just feel like cheap $8 leggings from Walmart. They are flattering though and they do have pockets in the back. It is a little misleading to just say they have pockets though. I wish they had a wider waistband.
