Really nice but not a great fit for postpartum These are surprisingly thick for a legging. I wouldn’t be concerned at all about them being transparent. Also I feel like they would be pretty sturdy and warm (once again considering that they are leggings). However I used the size chart and other reviews to determine that I needed a medium. Clearly they are too small for me. I’m 9 weeks postpartum. So a lot of my weight is in my belly. This isn’t high enough or low enough to accommodate my belly. It hits at a terrible spot, I don’t really feel like these are a high rise. Maybe a midrise. They are also extremely tight in the belly and crotch area. I was going to return them but I decided to keep them in hopes they fit when I’m thinner. Currently I am 145 pounds. I’m 5 foot 4 inches. I would say a majority of my excess weight is in my belly. So maybe a large would have been better. I’m really disappointed that they don’t fit now.

SIZE & MATERIAL ARE PERFECT! Love these! Love the fit. Love these, I hope they don’t ever stop carrying them. I could only find this one pair. Walmart usually carries, but they have been out of mid rise. I have had to buy them one at a time from 3 different sites. What I buy…. “Signature by Levi Strauss” -Size12/MID Rise/Straight Leg/Reg Length- -Also Fabric is awesome-Fit is awesome- **True to size** **Mid Rise/waist line, is the perfect Height for me. No muffin top, and no nipple huggers. **Straight leg is an awesome width. Love that I could find a straight leg that doesn’t flair too much, or drag. **waist =31” **Regular length = 32” ** GREAT FIT** I’m a size 12 weight 167 lbs. 5’6” I am a med bone size, so that is where some of my weight is. I have narrow hips, and a big butt. (which I hate, but that’s genetic. Why in the WORLD would anyone want a large Ass is crazy! But whatever.) **GREAT FABRIC/MATERIAL** Soft but still jeans, and not too tight, loose enough to still wear while hangin’ at home. Usually when I get home, the first thing I take off are my jeans. All my jeans are too, stretchy. Stretchy is great, until they fall down a few inches, after a few hours, like tights do, and I end up having to wear a tight belt. And my regular cotton jeans have no stretch, are a hard & stiff fabric. They fit great until I sit down once, or twice, because reg, non stretch “hard/stuff fabric” , actually just stretch out, and never stretch back. **Stretch But with these Signature jeans the fabric has the perfect amount of stretch, a bit stretchy, and a bit reg jean. Just enough to wear 2x before a wash. Hope you try them. Happy jean hunting.

These do not fit like "skinny jeans". They have a little extra fabric at the knee and ankles. I got a size 7 and maybe could have even sized down, because they have a little stretch. Comfortable Pair of Jeans, Washes Well, Doesn't Fade The Levi Strauss Women's Modern Straight Jeans is a comfortable pair of jeans that fit true to size and looks attractive on most body types. I like how the jeans are high-waisted and doesn't pinch anywhere. When I crouch down, I don't have issues with the jeans crouching down with me and exposing my derriere, and I like how it doesn't suck the life out of me. Although the jeans are cut short, at 5' tall, I still found I needed to alter the jeans to fit my leg length. As far as the fit goes, it's a bit more fitted than "mom jeans", but not quite as fitted as bootcut jeans. It's not quite as snug on the thigh and butt area and it has a nice "forgiving" feel. The jeans wash well and there was no fading or fraying and the material seems thick enough to last a while before the inevitable knee holes start showing up. That said, the jean material is not as thick as other jeans I own, but it's still fairly decent quality for the price. Overall, they're comfortable and casual jeans to wear and something I would purchase again.

So comfortable!! Love these. I’ve been gaining weight and I’m 5’7 137 pounds (3 kids). I got the size 6 long. there is room for my c-section tummy and my growing booty! Long enough to wear with cute fall boots and tennis shoes 🙌 best of all room to grow into them!!

Nice. Update: they relax over time Size 8. 5'6" 155lb, (being modest) I have a tiny waist, broad hips, moderate butt. I carry my weight in muscular upper body, and dense lower body these days. I usually wear pretty curvy jeans, because the waste usually gaps in EVERYTHING I own. I wear a belt with ALL my jeans, because of the gap. These jeans fit well, enough that I might consider a size 6 next, with the expectation of a tighter fit. Not perfect fit in the waist, but they probably are a decent "curvy" fit. I liked Levi 529, this is a close next best. I wear a belt so I dont have to keep hiking jeans up, but find I did pull these up several times a day. The legs are mostly straight through the thigh, a tiny bit narrower at the knee, but not much taper after that. I have solid thighs, and large calves (boot buying is never fun) and own slim ankles... these jeans do not cling, but wearing a neoprene knee brace underneath leaves no spare room. I might have considered a 6, but was hoping for space for the brace. The fabric feels like traditional modern denim, not 1920s canvas-denim, and not leggings style stretch-denim...., just modern denim with a tiny bit of stretch... which I hoped meant 2-3 days wear, without looking /feeling worn out. But they just seem to relax over the first day, and bummer, feel like "5th day wear" on the second day... so to me, that's 1 day wear jeans. Color was good. Update: ive had them a few months, they RELAX and dont gently hug curves like they used to. The knees are stretched out. The fabric fibers are starting to give in the knees and thighs giving them a bit of a fuzzy "needs to shave" look. Still confortable as my "slightly looser" pair of jeans, but I have to belt them. Photos attached. I wash/dry them in the machine like everything else, using cold/warm water and timed dry, w name brand soap and dryer sheets... but they dont hold up like my other brand jeans of similar value.

Unflattering Pros: Fabric is nice and thick, flattering for the booty, fits around my wide calves. Comfortable and cute I'm 5'4, about 130lbs, 26in waist, 34in hips, and I got the long inseam. I ordered a size 3 Long in the color Amy. They were very cute. The fabric is a thin cotton denim, feels comfortable and has a little flex in it. The belt loops were sewn in sturdy, and there is no gap in the waist. The size 3 on me is a little tight right out of the box, but I feel like it's the material fits your body better as you move around in it. I decided to return because this color blue isn't my favorite, it looks like a deep navy blue dye. I loved the stitching on the pockets. Overall, the fit is good, the pants are sturdy, and the stitching is done well with good quality thread. 10/10 would get again. Great bang for your buck.

Dressy Jeans and very stylish I normally wear a size 9 in most jeans, but when I purchased a size 9 in these, they were extremely roomy for my taste and I do not wear jeans skin tight by any means. I love the look of these jeans though, and the material is not too thick or too think. The length is slightly longer than I would like. I weigh about 116 lbs. and my height is only 5'3", so I would've had to hem these slightly, if I kept them. When the size 9 arrived and I tried them on, I realized I'd need to go down a size, so I re ordered a size 7 and they fit me perfectly, even the length is perfect. I may send the size 9 back or I may see if someone I know would like to buy them first. I love the jeans, but you never know with sizing, every jean is made from different material, so you really have to consider that when ordering. These jeans are 81% cotton, 18% polyester and 1% spandex, so they have some stretch to them with the lower cotton content. For the price and free shipping, I consider theses jeans a most reasonable deal. I would definitely buy these WallFlower jeans again.

5 Star for fit; 3 for decorative stitching I'm 5'10 and 160 lbs and these jeans are 14L. The fit is absolutely perfect, snug without being tight. (I'm considered apple shape with little discernable waist). The only downside is that there were multiple skipped and cut white decorative stitches that is unfortunately very obvious. Maybe it was intended to look "distressed"? As a sewist, it looked like threads weren't trimmed but just allowed to "hang". Actual construction appears to be satisfactory with flat fell seams & overlocked.

Length runs a big long I ordered these for my daughter who looked them over (before ordering) and she approved. Which was a shocker to me. Teens and pre-teens are so hard to buy for. It's like a game, that I seldom win. We ordered them, and they came pretty quick. These are cotton jeans, which means they are going to shrink unless you hang them to dry. Which turns out is a good thing for us. I ordered a size 2 and should have gone with a 0. She is 5' and weighs about 94#. So heres to shrinking! The material is soft and feels as though they have been worn and washed several times. The color is good too. There won't be any of the breaking in, as the designer worked that into these jeans. She is happy with them, (gasp). She did say if the size would have been correct, she would have worn them straight out of the package, which is big for her. Without her throwing a fit, I am taking these jeans as a win, or as a recommendation.

Great Jeans, Wrong Size. Beware “Amazon Size Recommendation!” Update: Got the right size (25), attached a new image of the fit above. Original review: I couldn’t decide between getting a size 27 or 28, and Amazon’s recommended size was 28. I got the 27 because it would arrive in time for my outing, and it still ended up being way too big (I prefer skinny/bootcut jeans to fit tightly), so the Amazon recc would’ve been way off. The Amazon size recc is based on just your height and weight, not all your measurements, and it messed up another order of Levi’s jeans I got, so I would trust the size guide instead! Other than that, the jeans are amazing. They’re comfortable levels of stretch while still being thick and giving quality, the deep blue color in combination with the contrasting thread in the “Casting Shadows” color is just 🧑‍🍳💋 chefs kiss perfect. Also, in the last image you can see it has the Levis stamp and tag so it’s definitely official, at least my variant! It also comes from the official Levis store and Amazon is strict against impersonators so I don’t think this is fake like that one reviewer claimed. Some Levi’s jeans have a tag that’s just red, without the logo, I own some of those. So yes! Buy it, but make sure to order the right size! My measurements: Height: 5’7 Weight: 120lbs Natural waist: 27 in Belly button: 29 in Hip: 33 in Butt: 37 in

Flare bottoms are perfect, not too big, just enough to fit nicely over a healed boot or wedge. Great denim feel with a little bit of stretch. I highly recommend!

Runs big I had a very hard time determining how these would fit me based off of reviews as there weren’t many photo reviews or reviews in general that mentioned size ordered versus body size/weight. I ordered a 14 thinking maybe they’d run small, but it ended up being the opposite. These were very loose and I will need to return and order a smaller size. They are very stretchy which I honestly like a more structured jean, but if you like stretch then these would be great. They’re soft and comfy material. The buttons worked well. They’re not a thick, structured jean so if you lean more towards liking jeggings or like room for movement/ comfortable sitting, etc then you would like these. For reference I am 30 years old, 5’7” and 225lbs. I carry weight in my stomach, arms and back the most. I feel with how stretchy these fit, I could have gotten away with a size 12 or maybe even a 10. Other jeans I own and fit into are 12-14.

Nice pair of distressed boot cuts for a good price. Not perfect though The first thing I noticed about these jeans (Bb002-blue) were how comfortable they are and how good the rips at the bottom hem will look with heels or boots. Also, how darn baggy they are on me, especially in the thighs. My thighs are only 18” around so I’m used to this. Drying in the machine helped to shrink them a bit and make them wearable for me, although now they’re kinda short. I should have gotten a size 2, but there’s none. For reference, I’m 5’7”, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, usually a 1 or 3 in AE jeans. A comfy fit, the waistband isn’t too tight. But the back pockets are a bit on the large size, making my butt look even smaller. That being said, I’ll mention some other flaws I noticed. Nothing major. The “creases” where the thigh bends are clearly very fake looking. Not like they’re genuinely used and distressed. I was also disappointed to find the style isn’t quite flared. It’s more boot-cut, the flare being overly exaggerated in their pictures. Still, they look okay. They don’t tend to keep their shape either and loosen while wearing (as did all the ae denim). I may need to keep dry-shrinking these, as they do loosen with wear, but they’re coming to fit nicely and I think I’ll add them to my regular jean cycle. Chances are, you’ve got more curves to fill these jeans out, so they’ll fit and look much better than they do on me. That being the case, I’d recommend these for sure, especially at this value.

Jeans that fit! Finally a pair of jeans that don't create a muffin top or cut me in half at the waist! I've always loved Lee jeans and these fit my 72 year old body great. Thus grandma isn't ready for pull on jeans yet, lol. There is just enough stretch in the fabric to be comfortable but not saggy after wearing for a few hours. What's up with the cost difference? I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

Sizing is confusing? I read several reviews to make sure I sized myself right. I normally wear a size 10 so I went with a size 12 and at 5’9” chose the 32” inseam option. The tag said 12L, W31, L32… I have a 33” waist so the W31 may be the accurate reading and the 12 may be irrelevant? Length is great but for sizing up they are still super tight. I’ll keep them as goal pants since I can still wear a baggy sweater over them but if I knew they would be this tight I wish they would list them based off of waist inch size not the numerical sizing that comes with a surprise waist size. my favorite pair of jeans! i am so grateful to have found these jeans! i’ve struggled with jeans shopping all my life. i am 5’3 and 145lb. I got size 6 with 27in length. they fit perfect! super soft! they fit my short legs and thick thighs. plus they make my booty look great - with no sagging. i have to pull them up above my double belly button piercing when i sit down, so they do the weird “i have a d!ck” thing (see photo) but most jeans do. no complaints there really. they stay form fitting all day - even after 3 hours at the skatepark. i don’t find myself having to pull them up or adjust them at all but still feel stretchy and comfy 🙌🏼 i usually cuff my jeans but i don’t HAVE to with these - they’re the perfect length and tightness. but the ankle still has enough give to cuff them if i’d like. the waist is perfect- not too low but not too high [you can still see my top belly piercing :)] overall, recommend - i actually have these in 3 colors now

These are made of denim, magic, and sorcery! I recently lost the vast majority of my clothes in due to Hurricane Florence, and buying pants is always difficult for me, so I wasn't really looking forward to replacing them. I'm 5' 7.5" and I've always had a small waist relative to my hips and butt, and when you add that combo to long legs?! Fuhgeddaboudit when it comes to finding pants and jeans with the right fit...or so I thought until I tried these babies on. To terribly exaggerate, once I easily fastened them, colors were brighter, lights shone down from the heavens while denim-tuxedo clad angels sang Ginuwine's "In those Jeans!" In the real world, I made my husband stare at my hips and butt as we both marveled at how amazingly these jeans fit. I even had jeans left at the ankle, and that is HUGE for me. I got 2 pair to try on through Prime Wardrobe and I'll be getting all available colors now that I'm aware of this magic. Thank you to Levi's and Prime Wardrobe for changing my life! And get yourself a pair of these jeans if any part of my story sounds like your life.

I’m 5’8”, 153 lb., and have thick calves and butt, so bought size 8, after reading reviews. Normally I’d never buy an 8. I’ll be wearing a tailed top to cover the butt because the pockets don’t flatter mine at all. Pockets are too far from the seam, which bugs me. Otherwise I’m excited to wear these with many different style shoes, boots and sandals.

Reliable Levi's, though the Amazon size listing is confusing I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

Garment Shrank 4 inches after washing! These are Misses size 16 Short. I am 5'6" 170lbs. I ordered two pair. I tried one of them on and they fit, except were too big in the back waist (as expected). I usually order short, since that is exactly the right length because I never wear heels with jeans. These were the right length and fit in the leg and all around. I planned to have the waist taken in, but decided to wash one pair, to see what the shrinkage was and whether or not I should wash the other pair before having the alteration done. I thought they might shrink some, but was really floored to see how much they shrank! Now, they are way too short and too tight in the legs and too small all around. I have never had this problem before. I am returning them.

Do everything jean I'll write a review because I know it can be for other ladies on a quest for good jeans. I've probably ordered 10+ pairs on Amazon and these are the winners. My measurements: 28" waist. 37" hip. 30" inseam. About 5'3". 133lbs. Athletic build. 30 yr old mom of a toddler. These jeans stay up without a belt. They are mid rise just below my belly button. I gave up on low rise cut jeans awhile ago because I hate having to yank them up every hour. The Levis website says these are relaxed through the hip and thigh. But if you are an athletic build and occasionally do outdoor chores in these pants, I found that they fit great and still flatter. I dislike skinny jeans because I prefer to wear my pants over my winter/fall boots without looking awkward. These bootcut jeans are a rare find in the skinny jean saturated market. Material is medium weight cotton blend, not paper thin. Decent cold weather jeans. I may wear leggings underneath if the temps drop lower in NH. Hope this helps another lady who needs "do everything" jeans.

SHORT, BIGGER GIRLS—buy these jeans! My body type: 5’ 5” and 242# with short, thick legs and a belly. I’m not familiar with this type of sizing, so I went with a 38 Short (I usually wear a 20 short in “regular” sizes). These jeans feel like they were made just for me! The material is very soft and stretchy, and the bootcut isn’t too flared at the bottom. The front of the pants can be pulled up over my belly without riding too far up in the “front” (if you know what I mean). Also, the back of the jeans doesn’t gap out if you pull them up in the front which I LOVE! I have washed and dried these jeans which made them shrink a bit, but the stretchy fabric allows them to still fit and loosen up after a bit of wearing. Highly recommended for my shorter, bigger girls out there!!!

The material is very soft and stretchy, and the bootcut isn’t too flared at the bottom. The front of the pants can be pulled up over my belly without riding too far up in the “front” (if you know what I mean). Also, the back of the jeans doesn’t gap out if you pull them up in the front which I LOVE! I have washed and dried these jeans which made them shrink a bit, but the stretchy fabric allows them to still fit and loosen up after a bit of wearing. Highly recommended for my shorter, bigger girls out there!!!

Nice jeans, run maybe a tad large, very messy & small buttonhole My sis-in-law recommended Bandolino jeans. I was in need of nice, dark jeans for jean day at work, & although the description said high rise, I could tell by the photos that they weren't high rise -- which is great because I'm short from my waist to straddle. I ordered these in my normal 16. They fit well, almost a tad loose, & a bit loose in the rear (& at 180, 5'3", my rear is not tiny). I just ordered again in a 14 to see how they'll fit, & for when I drop a few pounds. :) They seem to be made well, & the regular length I ordered are good & long, but I'm sure they'll shrink a bit in length, even with the polyester. The buttonhole is very messy looking & tight. It was a struggle to unbutton & button back the jeans. Will trim it myself, then might have to sew over it to reinforce. All in all, they seem like very nice jeans. Will update after I've washed them, on whether or not they shrunk. I'm not expecting a huge amount of shrinkage, since they contain polyester, but will know in a day or two.

Good & Not So Good Of course, these pants would look great on a tall, slender person...on a 5ft tall, 120 lb. gal, they're "ok". I will say, they are comfortable despite the material being a bit stiff and heavy. Since I ordered the white, however, it's nice that they're not see through! But, they're a challenge to iron or steam and they are seriously like a canvas sail! They should definitely hold up to a lot of wearing and washing. I like that they have stretch and the high waist reminds me to hold in my stomach all day! 😬😁 It's an overall cute pant and I do get compliments even though I don't find them wildly flattering. 🤷🏻‍♀️

Sizing Don’t Listen To Them! I was back and forth with seller about the appropriate size for me, I kept changing from L to XL, both were inaccurate reviews and seller. Listen when I say, there is no way a person weighing 200 lbs plus can fit into a XL., you won’t be able to breath or button up the jeans. I know because I used my own common scene and ordered the size I knew would fit at my size. 190lbs and went with the XL and the fit was perfect. The seller indicated I get a size 3X….REALLY…never in my entire life wore nothing higher than a XL. The reviews are insane as we’ll. All I would say if you want a looser fit, size up, other then that use my guide, it’s more then most. They are stretch and if your over 200 lbs don’t buy no XL you will be sending them back for a larger size. 170-180lbs Large. Shipping was guaranteed sooner too, that didn’t happen, wasn’t too long though. Very nice pair of jeans overall. Listen to your body, if you need to squeeze into any pair of jeans then that’s on you.

Comfy and soft I ordered the 447-medium Denim Blue in size 4. I am only 5 feet tall and have a 27" waist. Pants are long on me and need hemming but that is to be expected and normal. The jeans are a loose fit, especially at waist. As described a baggy high waisted jean with wide straight legs. They are very comfortable. Nothing is tight or pulls in a bad way. Material is a softer jean with distressed marks along back and front pockets. I noticed on my figure the back waist rides up higher than slopes down on the front. I like the jeans and believe they will get lots of use. Price is good for the quality.

I can't believe it!!! A pair of jeans that fit with no alterations needed!!! When I find a pair of jeans (or pants in general) that fit, they are usually baggy in the tummy area and fit my butt (or vice-versa) and 9 times out of 10 they are way too long so I end up taking them in to be hemmed. I love wide leg pants and usually I guess they aren't the most flattering on shorter legged people - I am - 36-33-42 and 5'4" - and maybe I'm just kidding myself but I think these jeans look pretty good. And even better, they feel GREAT on. They have a perfect stretch. They fit my butt but aren't too tight - I always have trouble there too. I washed them with darks and tumble dried them and they came out great. The unfinished hem still looks good - no extra fraying or anything like that while being laundered. I think they are fabulous jeans.

Great fitting jeans! The reason I didn’t give these jeans 5 stars is because 1) they decreased the front pocket size and, 2) they understate their inseam by 1.5 inches. I am 5’ 8”, 138 lbs and I have long legs. I need about a 34” inseam if I’m not wearing flats. I originally ordered a 28x34, but the inseam was actually only 32.5”. Today I received the 28x36s and they fit great. I hedged my bets that they, too, would be understated by a full 1.5 inches and I was right. They are actually a 34.5” inseam (not 36”) and perfect with a 1.5” heel. The classic straight leg Wranglers give women a full hand front pocket, which I find respectful. These are only as deep as your fingers.

I am 5’ 8”, 138 lbs and I have long legs. I need about a 34” inseam if I’m not wearing flats. I originally ordered a 28x34, but the inseam was actually only 32.5”. Today I received the 28x36s and they fit great. I hedged my bets that they, too, would be understated by a full 1.5 inches and I was right. They are actually a 34.5” inseam (not 36”) and perfect with a 1.5” heel. The classic straight leg Wranglers give women a full hand front pocket, which I find respectful. These are only as deep as your fingers.

Decent pair of jeans I am 5’3”, 155lbs. This pair is a size 8 (I am always able to wear a size 8 in Levi Signature - as they all run big IMO). The waist measure 15.5 inches flat, the inseam is 27 inches (ankle length, if you are taller than 5’4 these are gonna be short, almost a perfect length for me at 5’3”), 11.5 inch rise, the leg opening is 14 inches. They are a mid-weight cotton blend with plenty of stretch. The rise is long, maybe a little too long, so the crotch hangs a little low if you don’t keep them pulled up. They are a nice slim straight style, that are a pretty classic cut that will likely always be “in-style” They are comfy. I have washed themed and they really didn’t seam to shrink and they have done a decent job of holding their shape throughout the day. Overall a solid pair of not expensive jeans, great for wearing around the house, but definitely don’t do anything to enhance your figure; like you don’t put them on and think “wow, these look great” it is more like “wow, these are a perfectly serviceable pair of jeans”. I mean for $23 you can’t go wrong really.

Durable. Pretty long. Versatile. Very Skinny Legs. Affordable. Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

Strange Fit, Baggy & Long My wife is disappointed in the Lee Women’s Sculpting Slim Fit Slim Leg Pull On Jean. They simply don't live up to the "sculpting slim fit” description. If anything, they do the opposite. My wife thought the stretchy waist would be comfy and slimming, but they feel more like maternity pants. They are loose around the abdomen and crotch and the size 8 is way too long on her 5'5 ½" frame. She has to wear heels to keep the hem from dragging. The back fits okay but it's not overly flattering or special in any way. The sizing seems way off. 8 medium is more like a 12 large. No thank you to vanity sizing in this instance. The material is smooth but not breathable. The stitching is decent in some areas but seems messy in other spots -– definitely not refined like lux jeans, so you're getting what you pay for. The overall style is unflattering and makes my wife feel weighed down and uncomfortable. These are jeans she probably won't wear again — she flat out dislikes them on many levels. I would agree that they do not bring out the best in her form and style and I noticed she had to keep pulling them up while we were shopping. ~ Kort

Very comfortable...but they stink! I think I’m giving these a 3.5, I went back and forth with the star rating. These are very comfortable pants. I wasn’t sure whether to go with the 0 or the 2, and the short or the regular. I’m 5’3.5” and my go-to Jean is a size 1 in Celebrity Pink. I ordered the Size 0 Regular. They seemed a tad long for me (I think I like the shorter length on me in tighter jeans) but they were perfect with rolled cuffs. They are definitely more of a relaxed look than I am used to. The legs were looser. I liked the rise and it was high enough to contain everything but I don’t think it gave me a flat butt the way some high-waist jeans do. Great lighter color. I think I would have preferred the shorter length, but with the looseness of the pants I’m not sure that would’ve looked better. So I’m ultimately glad I went with the 0R. If you are on the smaller side of a size 0, you may find these too big, but that’s what free returns are for. The thing is these pants STINK. They smell so bad. It’s like formaldehyde meets moth balls. I thought after a wash the smell would be gone but nope! These still wreaked after a wash. I’m not going to return them and hope that a few more washes will get the smell out, but if you’re sensitive to that, I’d skip these pants.

I ordered two sizes up and am thrilled with the fit I don't like really tight clothes but want the fitted look. First I ordered my usual size (6 Long) and a size up (8 Long) and found both too tight for my taste. Then I ordered the 10 Long. They are PERFECT. And for us tall girls who have fought the High-Tide look all our lives, the longs truly are long. They hit my ankles, which I love. I started with a white pair and just ordered a blue jean pair the same size. These jeans are currently the best thing going - good price, good quality and good looking. In case it helps, I'm 5'9 and usually wear a 33 inseam.

Satisfaction varies whether you want skin tight or not I like ankle skinny jeans that are not skin tight. In Amazon Essentials Bermuda shorts I wear size 4, generally I wear a six. I do not like my clothes tight. Based on reviews I sized up to an 8. The jeans have lots of stretch and if you want leggings look these pants would be great. For me, they are so so. I did keep them as I wanted some tan jeans and they had been hard to find. I am 5’1” and weigh 100#. My perfect ankle skinny jeans are Calvin Klein Ankle Skinny in size 8, which are a little looser thru the legs and ankle, but I don’t think they make any more. Hope the review helps.

Pleasantly surprised but white has side stripe When I got these, I thought they were for kids, but sure enough, they fit and fit perfectly! I’m 5’8” so I like to have a little added length on the hem and these look great. My only complaint and this is likely just because they’re white, but on the hem of the leg, it looks like a stripe. The pants aren’t see through on the leg (they are somewhat on the top, but that’s mostly cover by shirts), but that hem stands out. It wasn’t bad enough that I wouldn’t wear them though and almost could be considered a styling feature depending on how you look at it.

It's nice to find something casual that doesn't break the bank. I am very happy with this purchase!

Super inconsistent sizing! AWFUL quality fabric. UPDATE: ugh. just look at the new photos. Changing to 1-star. Do NOT recommend. After a few months, I’ve found that these jeans are total garbage. They are droopy and unflattering, and the material quality is extremely poor. Even freshly washed, they never un-bag. The elastic in the fabric buckles out or pulls in weird looking strips in multiple places, and it doesn’t even smooth out after putting them on. TERRIBLE! Wish I never bought them, even at my insanely cheap price. Overall: Meh. I would have returned these if they were at what appears to be the very reasonable sounding full price. - Material is very stretchy, but a bit thin. Nice for summer, but you can catch some VPL even through the dark wash. - Inseam length runs short. I wear flats and almost always have to get short lengths for a 30" inseam, but the short of these hit in an awkward spot above my ankle bone. - The listing didn't specify, but these are pretty high cut. They hit right at my belly button. - Butt pockets are higher than I expected, but not too 90s looking. I bought a pair of these in 2 short. Initial fit seemed just okay, but for the very low price I decided to grab a second identical pair in case I ended up loving them after a few wears. Turns out... They are clearance priced for a reason! In the photos, you can see two pairs of the same jean- same exact size, same exact color, but cut at least a size differently. The waists have about 1-2" difference in diameter! Luckily, the material is stretchy enough that the cut too small jeans are just good enough. And the cut is high enough that cut large ones are also just good enough. Neither are great, but for the price I found them at, I'll keep them while I wait for my normal jeans to go on sale (AE jeggings).

Misleading Color I ordered the “Blue” color version of these jeans in size 12 as recommended. I’m 5’2 & as you can see in my photos, the crotch area is baggy/big, as well as it’s a bit too tall for me. The photo in the advertisement is a much softer blue toned than what I received. The blue denim I received is not only darker but the acid wash effect is also harsh. I think the seller may have sent me the “N Blue” pants because the blue denim advertised, doesn’t have the acid wash design in the back. The placement & harshness of the acid design looks terrible as if someone diy’d it. The only positive thing I can say is that the denim material itself is soft which is comfortable for wear. Overall I’m not sure if this was false advertising or a clerical error of being sent the wrong item but I’ll be returning these pants. I’ve lost faith in opting for an exchange because who’s to say any of the jeans are the correct style/color of what’s advertised here. Not worth $40 gamble.

Baggy fit perfection (edited review) Edited review: Scratch the sizing comment in my original review. It took me a few try-ons and changes to love that I got them so baggy. Coming from skinny jeans it took me some time to figure out why I wasn’t crazy about them. Decided it was the color so I soaked them in a bleach solution to lighten. Then I added more rips and washed and dried these a couple of times and I flipping love them. Definitely going to order the lightest color offered next. They’re so comfy and I get a lot of compliments when I wear them. As a 40-something this style takes me back to being a teenager and I forgot how great it feels to be comfortable in jeans and not dying to peel them off at the end of a day. Original review: Ordered 2 sizes up so they’d be baggier on me and I should have probably just got one size up. Wear a 10 in American eagle and got the 14. The material is soft and on the thinner side which I like. The rips are perfection too. The vertical seam that goes down the backside/butt is crooked and sort of drives me crazy when I look back in the mirror but overall these are nice jeans. The bottoms are very baggy/wide but again I sized up too far so that’s on me. 145lbs 5’4” 40” hip 29” waist Got the size14 but should have got 12.

Comfortable, flattering, true to size These jeans are super cute and great quality for the price. I did not want to spend $200+ on such a trendy style that I wasn’t sure would work for me but glad I took a chance on these Free People dupes, they did not disappoint. I am 5’9” and 155 lbs, age 47. The 10s fit perfectly, however I am extra thick around the middle and if you are more of an hourglass type the 8s might be a better fit. The 8s were great but a little too tight in the waist for me. I will echo some other reviews that these are definitely high rise, not mid. Totally fine by me but be aware if you care. Lastly, there was a huge difference in length between sizes so maybe consider that when you’re selecting a size. The 10s hit my ankle, the 8s were more like a cropped length.

Runs Large, Good Barrel Shape I like the quality and fit but they run large. I’m 5’7” 158# and ordered size 10. My normal size is usually 10/30 or 8/29 depending. Amazon clothes seem to run small but not these! They’re too big - in the waist, legs, and length. I may order a different size. I don’t like the Army Green color. The green part is actually cute but the fading looks cheap. It’s yellowish in real life. The material is a soft, lightweight jean. They don’t stretch but have a soft, looseness to them. I’m happy with the quality. The stitching detail is great and they have a substantial horseshoe/balloon leg shape. I haven’t seen the FP Barrel jeans IRL but these must be a close dupe. They’re a good price for a trendy jean! I’ll likely order a smaller size and different color.

Perfect jeans to elongate legs, but color bleeds 4 stars instead of 5 because it is not colorfast. First pic is after 3-4 rinses in the sink. Second pic is after the 6th rinse. I would not wear this with anything white until it is run through the wash a few times. Otherwise, I love these jeans because they are very flattering to girls with short legs like me. I’m 5’1” and less than 108 lbs but am blessed with ample hips and short legs. If it’s too high waisted I look very bottom heavy. If too low rise my proportions look off. These have just the right amount of high rise. The dark wash, straight leg and positioning of the back pockets all do me favors. I will have to hem these as I do with almost all pants and will not be rolling the ankle because the inner color of fabric is light. Rolling the cuff will create a light band at the bottom that detracts from the elongating effect. Fabric does feel nice and soft yet sturdy. It’s not stiff and there is a slight stretch to accommodate ease of movement. Seams are all well done and pockets seem well reinforced. I expect these to last me many years. Amazon fashion purchases are always hit and miss, but if you are disciplined about returning items you don’t love, true bargains can be had. This is definitely a score despite the color bleeding.

Much Longer cut than 721 (High Rise Skinny) I have a pair of 721's (High Rise Skinny) in Soft Black that I wear all the time, and the fit is perfect, so I figured the same size in 724 (also Soft Black) would be a slam dunk! But they are at least 3-4 inches longer! Why, Levi's? Why? The material is exactly the same, the cut is just waaaay longer. For reference, I'm 5'6 145 lbs and size 30 fits great- snug, but stretchy! In Soft Black at least. I've found every wash is a different amount of stretch so this review is for Soft Black only! Update- Chelsea Hour Dark Indigo had a similar fit to Soft Black, although a tad shorter. Even though they're cropped, they're way too long. Even the 10S hit the top of my foot and bunched slightly. They might shrink a bit after washing. Verdict: returning. Update 2: Chelsea Pier Dark Indigo - pics in the ad are super misleading. These are a thin extra stretchy denim - like super stretchy jegging material - and the 10S/30S brushed the top of my feet. The model for this style must be 12' tall bc they look like crops of her! The color is a very cute blue but only shoe I could wear these with is a flip flops or they'll bunch up

Super soft and stretchy; VERY high-waisted I'm a straight-leg girl, but I liked the look of these online so I thought I'd give them a try. I actually like the way they look and feel overall. The flares balance my broad shoulders nicely...they definitely do away with that top-heavy look that skinny jeans and a blousy top can cause (I'm an inverted triangle shape, so leggings and skinnies can make me look like a chicken if I'm not careful lol). They're incredibly stretchy and soft, and I was shocked to find them long enough for my 33.5" inseam (I NEVER find jeans long enough unless I order tall, and even then they're sometimes only a 32"). The length alone made me celebrate, and I can actually wear them with heels! My only issue with them is that I am short-waisted, even though I'm tall, and these are not made for a short torso. They button at my ribs, which I kind of hate. It feels suffocating. I typically buy mid-rise jeans (I like them under my belly button an inch or so), so getting used to these literally poking the bottom of my breasts when I sit down will be a whole thing. They're ok when standing (no muffin top!), but sitting feels super...awkward. I don't think these will replace my usual Joe's jeans, but I enjoyed wearing them for a night out when we were in Vegas, and they were quite comfy. They'll be nice for occasional wear, and are a great compromise between slacks and jeans when it comes to dressing for those occasions in which you're unsure of the dress code. I ordered a 14 (I usually wear a 31 or 32 jeans) because I have a bigger booty, but these have so much stretch that sizing up was unnecessary. They're quite roomy in the waist...I think a 10 or 12 would probably have been better (if I reordered, I would go with the 10). They work, though, because they're so soft and stretchy. I would recommend these as long as the uber high waist doesn't bother you. If you're used to skinnies, these still have that soft feel, so they're a good transitional option before you dive into a pair of stiff, high waisted Levi's. I washed in cold and dried on medium. They did shrink a bit (which is what I was going for when I put them in the dryer). Now I would probably just hang them to dry.

Never had pants be so thick & high quality material & still this stretchy!!! I have trouble with pants because I have wide set hips, a tummy, and Itty bitty chicken legs with no butt. These are a great solution because they're so stretchy!!! They fit comfortably around waist but aren't super baggy in the butt and legs. Still a bit bigger in those areas than I'd like but in a not quite tight enough way not a baggy way. They also cover my ankles even though they're high wasted and pulled up past my belly button! I'm almost 5'10 so pants always look like high waters on me. The most impressive thing about these was the fabric quality. Normally if I find jeans with amazing stretch like this then they're super super thin or like jegings. It doesn't bother me I like thin pants but then they wear and tear very fast. These are very thick sturdy durable denimaterial but SO stretchy and comfortable!!! An awesome pair of flattering jeans! Definitely a good order!

Buy these! But maybe size up First I love the look and feel of these skinny jeans. I am normally a 18 and ordered the XXL, however the crotch is stretched too tight and so there is a gap there. I am 5’4” and 217#. I am long waisted with a lot of my weight in the belly area. I am going to try sizing up to the 3X but I often have this problem with being between sizes. I will update my review after ordering the larger size. **Update** I have worn the larger 3x size now twice and I can’t rave enough about these jeans. They are really comfortable and you don’t have to constantly pull up the waist. They sit above the belly button on me. I will be buying more of these and I highly recommend them but I would size up if you are tummy and bottom heavy.

Comfy, cute. Great for moms. I'm 32. 5'2 and post baby. Before pregnancy I was 114lbs. After I've ranged from 119 to 130 something. I've stopped weighing myself because it became discouraging. So I've done some exercise (not a lot by any means) and some intermittent fasting/cutting calories. Not sure how much I weigh now and I won't weigh myself as not to be discouraged. Anyway, i have found that jeans are not comfy nor do I like the fit. I've got some curves but also am petite. Now I understand why older people where panst with elastic. These are super cute and you can hardly tell they aren't "jeans". I got the medium . Not sure how they wash yet and hoping they hold up.

Large Navy-67 Large/Navy-67. I am 4"8/140 pounds, thick booty and thighs. Fits perfect waist through leg. I am short so I had to cut about a foot off from length to make above ankle length. Comfortable, stretchy, thinish material. Great for summer.

Comfy! These pants are so comfortable and so affordable! After reading reviews I bought a large. I’m 5’9 so they were a little shorter on me than I like, but I just cuffed them and they were perfect.
Is Beautiful I'm 5'9 202lb first I order a size 2x because of reviews but was very loose I end up ordering again but size large fits perfect looks like real jeans.

Love these!! These fit me perfect and are super comfortable. I’m 5ft7 and 147 pounds. The medium fits perfect. Highly recommend!

SO FLATTERING These fit me perfectly. I’m obsessed. Size 10 or 12 pants, usually. About 170lbs and 5’6” and got the 30 Regular. These Levi’s have such a nice height on the waist. Perfectly covers and shapes the pelvis and tummy and lands right at juuuust above the navel on me. That area of denim is shaping as well as the rear. Kinda cinches at the natural waist and a bit more under the rump than other styles. At the waist, there’s almost a V-shaped waistline and I think that also aids in concealing the love handles area. The denim is very sturdy and while they’re not stretchy like some other cheap brands, they will conform to your body and have just enough give as to not be constricting. I got some kind of light indigo color - it’s pretty and would probably go with any top or shoe style. I love how the ankle isn’t suffocating like traditional skinny jeans! I wanted a tighter leg but tiny bit of give at the ankle (unlike the “straight” styles with is way too loose) and these fit the bill. The regular in a 30 is 30 for length as well and is AMAZING on me. Not too short at all. Front pockets are pretty short and useless but I don’t care the rest is a dream! These can be worn dressed up or down.

Love the flattering cut. But sizing information listing is misleading Got a 24 regular. I checked the size guide for inseam before making the ordering; listed as small, medium, long. So I will assume 24 regular is 30” inseam vs. 24 long’s 32” inseam. But no. I received a 24x32 for my order of 24 regular. So if you are thinking of getting a regular, take note that you will receive a 32 inseam, not 30 inseam. Which leads to the question; what’s the inseam length for “long”? 34??!! Wished I have the legs for 34 inseam.

I’m 4’11” weight about 135lb. I ordered a 12 short. They are a little big in the waist and hips. They fit like high waisted pants. The inseam is fine. They have a funny odor to them. I washed them once but I can still smell a faint odor. I’m going to wash them a second time hopefully the smell will go away.

Inseam is shorter than advertised. I like everything about these jeggings except that the inseam length for normal is advertised as 30.5", but it's shy of 29" which is the short size. Did the wrong tags get attached at the factory? The mistake makes these a bit too short on my 5'6" frame. I will exchange them for the long length. I'd give 5 stars if not for the length error. The fabric is substantial, stretchy, and looks like denim. The waistband hits at my bellybutton and is so thick it creates a little shaping right where it's needed, and it doesn't roll, bunch, and the jeans stay on securely. For a sexy look, I'd get a size 8, but I'm in my 50s and the 10 fits close without being tight, like dress jeans, on my 145-150 lb body. I'm delighted that these are available in different lengths!

Inconsistent Sizing I bought three of these all in size 18: dark, medium, and light washes. The dark and medium fit perfectly. But the light wash is both tighter and shorter than the other two. Luckily they are easy to return. Otherwise, the medium and dark washes fit perfectly and are a great length! I'm 5'4". The material feels durable but I'll see how they hold up after a few washes.

Fabulous! These are the best jagging I have ever had. The waistband sits perfectly and doesn’t rolled. I am almost 6 feet tall and I am thrilled that these come in the long. I am also flabbergasted as such fantastic pricing on these. I am ordering as many pairs as I can.

LOVE these jeggings! I am 5'9" and have longer legs so finding jeans that fit AND are long enough has always been a challenge. These are stretchy, nice waistband that helps with tummy control and are long enough! Plus they are super cute and look great! Never thought I would wear 'jeggings' but here I am loving these! I have already ordered another pair and will stalk...I mean patiently wait for the black version in my size to restock! And you cannot beat this price!

I like to read reviews before buying an item because I am 6' tall which makes it difficult to shop for clothes. To be honest, I forgot what size I got but I think it was 10-long. They are not long to my standards - on the photo you can see that "high waist" is not up to the waist and leg length is barely enough to look ok when walking. It looks too short when sitting (also on the photo). I like the material, so that's a plus. Pants are super hard to put on because they are tight in the waist area and I barely squeeze it through my hips. Also, as a side note - while I am tall, I think I have a very proportionate body, so my legs aren't long compared to the rest of my body. I am keeping them and might even get another pare when this one is done, but there are things that I wish were better :)

Long-Legged Luxury: Perfect White Jeggings for Tall Travelers!" Finding the right fit for jeggings can be a tall order when you are 5'9" with long legs, but these white jeggings in size 6 Long were a fabulous find! I took them on a trip to Italy in March, and they were a dream come true. Not only did they fit my long legs perfectly, but they were also incredibly comfortable and flattering, making them the ideal travel companion. Worn on the stunning Amalfi Coast, they matched the beauty of the surroundings and handled everything from long walks to leisurely dinners. The jeggings maintained their shape and comfort throughout the trip, proving to be both durable and stylish. For anyone looking for great-fitting, chic, and comfortable jeggings that cater to taller frames, I can’t recommend these enough!

Great fit true to size I am 5'7" and weigh 280 lbs. (I know) I am posting some maybe not so flattering pics of my belly pouch but hopefully gives someone with same stats an idea of how these fit. First thing I absolutely loved is the color, exactly as pictured. They material is soft yet sturdy and the stretch is perfect and a nice snug..(I feel cute) fit. The waistband is super confortable.. not like a regular jean and it doesn't cut Into your waist. Important if you have a a belly and pouch like me. The length is perfect all the way down to my ankle. There was no weird smells. When I sit or bend down my backside business stays my business. They feel almost like a second skin like I could wear them all day and not even think about it. I would definitely recommend. Love the price too as I recently had gastric sleeve and am having to buy others more often I definitely will buy these at the next size down. Overall great buy for the money. Very satisfied!!

LOVE THESE I am middle-aged and all my 9/10 Old Navy jeans are too tight. I have extra weight around hips/tummy that I want to loose (hopefully), and I am not ready to give up. So meanwhile, I thought I would try a size 10 Long in these no-zip Amazon jeans. I am 5'7", and as you can see from the full leg picture, maybe I did not need the "Long". But I just now pulled them on and have not washed them yet. I would rather have a little bit of bagginess by the knees then too short at ankles. I am very satisfied with the feel and fit. It is a decent thickness of fabric --not super thin, but not thick and heavy. These are very comfortable everywhere on my crotch and legs, including the waist. The waist band seems midwaist and sits about 1.5 in below navel. Not highrise or lowrise or hipsters, etc. Some of my belly is inside the jeans, some above, but nothing feels constricted. Snug but not too much. Super comfortble when sitting and bending/squatting and my crack does not show. Hope this review helps you. I would try them out at least, since Amazon Prime gives you time to try them within the return window. I only bought one pair for now, but wow! I am excited about these. Have a blessed day.🙏💖

Color Unexpected. Otherwise Nice The color is described initially as Blush Pink. For reference I have photographed the pants on a Blush Pink quilt. Not even close to a Blush Pink. In ordering, the color is described as Coral Pink. Closer, but I would say Salmon Pink is a correct descriptor. It’s okay, Salmon Pink will work into the autumn, so maybe it’s even better. But I did order three tops to go with Blush Pink pants, and thus is disappointing. I took a star off for that reason. I ordered size 12 Long and the fit is perfect (5’7” 163 lb). It’s a thicker denim than in other leggings from Amazon Essentials, more like regular denim. Still has stretch. I think I will get a lot of wear from them.

Perfect for postpartum jeans I really like these right out of the bag. I had two babies back-to-Back and I have DR and lots of skin. The waist band on these are gentle and feel forgiving when I sit and stand. The butt fits great! Now I’m 5’7 and ordered a tall so that I can fold the bottom for cuffs and these actually have a normal length, but it also gave me a longer hip and thigh which I LOVE. It’s been why I order long pants. The demon is a great color. Need to wash before wearing because it smells like manufacturing and it’s mildly irritating to the skin. The denim is thin and has the perfect amount of stretch. I would buy these in every color! 10/10 would recommend

Stretchy yet firm I'm 5'3" 172 lbs and wear a L in most things. I have short legs so I purchased these in a 12 short. They are slightly snug at the waist, but too much looser and I'd worry about them sliding down. So far they work for me. I haven't noticed any sliding yet and the stretchiness is forgiving yet firm, so I'm not too concerned about the knees getting baggy. The back pockets are real, but not the front. I would definitely recommend these. Hopefully I still like these at the end of the day. There was a weird spot on the waist band where the stitching was done over several times. It's pretty itchy right now, maybe I'll just get used to it. Or maybe it rubs my stomach raw and I curse Amazon as I'm putting bandaids on the spot. only time will tell.

I haven't noticed any sliding yet and the stretchiness is forgiving yet firm, so I'm not too concerned about the knees getting baggy. The back pockets are real, but not the front. I would definitely recommend these. Hopefully I still like these at the end of the day. There was a weird spot on the waist band where the stitching was done over several times. It's pretty itchy right now, maybe I'll just get used to it. Or maybe it rubs my stomach raw and I curse Amazon as I'm putting bandaids on the spot. only time will tell.

comfortable & attractive pull-on jeans, good value for the price The fabric these jeggings are made of is thick enough to look like real jeans, but just a soft and stretchy as leggings. Way better quality than most jeggings. I got the black and the dark wash and I really like both of them. I love having no button to cut into my tummy when I sit, or bulky zipper under my shirts and sweaters. I'm 5'2" and 110lb and the 0 fits snugly but isn't too tight to sit cross legged. The 0 is likely long enough to cover your ankles if you are up to 5'6" or so, but for shorter legs it's easy to just tuck the extra fabric under for a hemmed look. These are likely to become my go-to jeans to wear with sneakers or boots. I wish they made them in boot cut, too!

Good fit and comfortable, but created a muffin top I ordered the short size 12 and they are good but not the ideal jegging for me but still worth keeping. I have taken two photos with two different shirts and you can see that with the loose shirt, it camouflages my waist area. With the tighter shirt, it created an unflattering fit to see my fat bulging from the tight waistband. Ideally a looser band would be preferred, but I have a difficult body to fit jeans and pants since I am 5’3” and I have more of a “gut” than butt. So to get pants to fit in the butt, I have to order a size down but then it creates a tight waist and sometimes a muffin top and this is exactly what these jeggings did for me. But other than that, the length is great, the material nice and stretchy while still looking like jeans, they are comfy, and the waist band is still comfortable, despite the muffin top effect. I think I’m going to keep and just wear with looser shirts.

Not the most flattering look for me, but practical I'm always looking for denim leggings, and appreciate new pairs that I can wear out of the house before they get ripped, banged around, paint splattered, etc. I'm not sure these jeans from Dokotoo are that flattering for me, though. I wear an XL (5'4", 170 pounds) and the legs are way tooo long, and the wide flares are also maybe not that slenderizing. The brand-new stitching looks a bit bright and cheap to me. With that said, I may just hem the legs and wear these at home and about town. They are very comfortable and fit well aside from the leg length. They might be perfect to sew kneepads in, for floor work on my various building projects. For someone who is a taller XL, these might be just perfect. Of note is that the slash pockets at the top are real, and the stitching in the area of where cargo pockets would be, is faux. A big plus is that these are elastic cotton -- 98 percent cotton, 2 percent elastane, which may be why they feel comfortable is a way different to the soft comfort of polyester. Just more natural feeling. Review of Dokotoo Women's Casual Mid Waist Cargo Jeans Stretch Wide Leg Denim Pants with Pockets

Wish these were a *tad* longer I wore these all day and I gotta say they are COMFY! I ordered a 4 and I'm a 26-27" waist and they fit really well, I probably could have sized down for a tighter fit on the waist without issue. Obviously the cut of these pants allows for a lot of movement but the fabric stretches and bounces back with ease. I could squat and goblin walk in these and be totally comfy while doing it (we all know that's the true test). I wasn't sure about the aesthetic of these but I got multiple compliments from coworkers and they really grew on me. I did notice that the white threads started to fuzz up by the time I got home. It seems like they may have used cotton thread for these accents which would account for that. My only hesitation is that I wish they were an inch or two longer, but I'm 5'9 and they're definitely long enough. That's why I removed a star, but I think for *most* people the length will be absolutely perfect. These are honestly really great and the quality exceeds the price. Much better than similar pants I've gotten for more $ from popular retailers

Excellent quality. I ordered these Dokotoo Women’s Casual Mid Waist cargo jeans for my daughter and she loved it. Cargo jeans are sewn with high quality and neatly. The seams are even throughout and the threads do not stick out anywhere. The inner seams are carefully finished. The fabric from which the jeans are sewn stretches well and is pleasant to the touch. Cargo jeans look good and complement the look nicely. Cargo jeans have a beautiful gray color. I ordered a size 12 for my daughter and it fit well. My daughter was deciding between 10 and 12 and chose 12 because she doesn't like jeans that are too tight. The daughter is 5.3" tall and weighs 140 lbs. Her hips are 41.5 inches and her waist is 32 inches. There is a size chart in the description, which is very convenient. The cargo jeans are a bit long for her height, but this can be easily fixed with iron-on adhesive tape.

My favorite pants! These are the softest, most comfortable pants I own. And I own a LOT of pants. Based on the size chart, I ordered up but I needn't have, so I'm hoping they shrink a little. But the length is perfect, so hopefully they don't shrink too much! I'd order my usual size but if you're between sizes you might want to order up. For reference, I'm 5'6" about 145 lb and normally wear a size 6 but ordered size 8. They're a bit loose in the waist and with so much stretch and always carrying my phone in my pocket, I'll need a belt! The fabric is amazingly soft - like peach fuzz - with a lot of stretch. They're light but thick enough to have good structure and quite flattering. So glad they're 98% cotton - they'll be breathable for summer comfort. And speaking of pockets - get a load of these bad boys! Nice, big phone pockets - for those of us who don't carry purses, my wallet phone case has no trouble fitting in here! I'd give these pants 10 stars if I could, they're so comfortable. They're my go-to pants for wearing every day and doing every thing! Highly recommended, A+++++ !!

Great Cut & Soft Denim High Waisted Button Up Jeans Very hard for me to find a pair of jeans that fit well, because I'm 5'1" and 138 lbs - they are either way too long (like by a foot+) or so tight I can't bear being in them. These looked funny to me when I opened the package because I had forgotten I had ordered high waist jeans. Been a long time! I love them, they are a perfect fit for me. I like the material because it's soft, not thick and stiff. They are a great length on me, and not skin tight at the bottom. I see other reviews where the buttons fell off, etc. So far my buttons are on just fine. I love the high waist, it prevents love handles and these will look good with just about any top-T-shirt, blouse, turtlenecks, or any kind of shoe or boot. In photos I am wearing low kitten heels with black tights. They arrived nicely packaged and quickly after ordering. Looking forward to loving them a long time.

Tighter Than Expected, Overall Nice Fit and Fabric I'm 5'4" and 38"x33"x39". According to the size chart the Size 12 would fit best at my waist, but would have been baggy at the hips. I decided to purchase a 10 and a 12 to make sure of the fit. The 12 was obviously tight looking while still in the package, so I tried them on first—never even opened the size 10. The 12 fit very tight across the hips and thighs, but are not tight around my waist. Waistband rolls when I sit, but I don't tuck my shirts in as I did for the photo. Thick fabric for a stretch denim. Regular inseam length hits right above the back of my foot. Front pockets are very shallow, 2" deep. Followup note: These pants are difficult to pull all the way up. I have to grab the waistband and hold it while I bend over to pull them upward up to 3 times. Once up they stay pretty well, but I wish the waistband was a little tighter. Basically, every slim-cut stretchy denim pant I have tried has this same tight hips and loose waist fit problem.

I've never had off the rack jeans fit so well! So caveat, I am 5' tall, so I knew that no matter what these were going to be too long. Apparently you can't be short and curvy. I went off of the measurements, and these fit so well around the waist and hips. I love the high waist and the ribbon tie. A little bit of a hem and these are sure to be my favorite jeans.

They have enough stretch that I feel comfy walking and sitting and just living in them, they shape my legs and butt quite nicely and they have FUNCTIONAL pockets! I'm a shortstack at 5'2" so my only concern is that the hem rides up a little too high but I can work with it even if that means the pockets sit too high as well. Just a warning for my fellow shorties out there.

Great pants but some sizing differences. Long review. Long but detailed review: I'm 5'6 250 for reference and I bought a 16 long for both pairs. I got the long because sometimes pants aren't long enough for me but I think I would have been fine with the regular length cause they bunch up at the bottom. The Sea sky (dark blue) color fit like a glove. I love them so much! I decided to get the night sky (black) color and they were really tight. I should have gone a size up for these ones. So be weary of the size differences in the different colors. They were also made in two different countries so IDK if that's why there sizing is different. They have a pretty decent amount of stretch to them. They're not like regular cotton jeans where they is no stretch, but they're also not like leggings where they're super stretchy either.. pretty much in the middle in my opinion. I've also worn and washed them both multiple times and they are holding up really well. So all in all really good jeans!

Stitching is off! I was really looking forward to these jeans and ended up super disappointed!! I previously bought Levi Signature 720 size 14s skinny jeans (for reference I'm 5'0 and 130lbs) and they fit perfect, so I was hoping these would fit just as great. Wrong!! I got the same exact size in these jeans (color is sea and sky) but they fit terrible! I knew right away there was an issue with how the pants were sewn. They were very wide on the thigh areas but very narrow on the calf area. You can see what I mean in the pic where they are hanging up. When I tried them on, they were way too big and too long!! You can see in the pic where I have them on, they are gathering up in several places. The material was soft though and stretchy but just not sewn properly. I will be returning these!

Beyond Flattering Let me lay out my body negatives so you know just how awesome these wide leg jeans are. I'm 5'3 weighing 180lbs, short legs & short torso. I have a solid athletic build with a firm muffin-top and small fupa. I carry most of my weight in my stomach and breast. I'm never without shapewear on; most times two of them on. I purchased a medium in these, the perfect boot cut denim stretch jeans, and boy are they comfortable. At first when I pulled them from the package, the waist looked really small, esp in comparison to the nice wide leg and ample bottom part. So of course I tried them on without shapewear just to see how much stretch was in the waist part. I was able to button them, but not comfortably. I knew they would be perfect with my shapewear on. I ordered the 'wased black'; I love the faded look. The stretch on these jeans is a nice firm stretch. And because they are not really denim material, they are not heavy like denim, but sturdy enough to still feel like a great pair of jeans. The best part, they got my booty looking great. I absolutely love these jeans, so I plan on getting two more pairs.

Cute wide leg style, but runs very big! I picked these up for my daughter in a size small in the fresh bright blue color option. My daughter is 5’4" and 120lbs. These are quite big, especially in the length. She is happy with the high cut waist and the wide leg. These hit her at the belly button, but she needs a belt to keep them up. We washed them in hot water and machine dried them in hopes of shrinking them, but there was not much shrinkage. Made in China from 71% Cotton, 27% Polyester, 2% Elastane they have a metal zipper and button closure and nice deep pockets. Overall they are a cute, and very current style of jean. There is whiskering to the dye at the leg creases and the color stayed fresh. I did wash them inside out to protect the color and prevent fading.

Great Fit, But I Ordered a Size Smaller Than Normal. Long Enough! The Wide Leg High Wasted Stretchy Denim Jeans (ASIN: B0CRHBL7WQ) feel really good on. Other stretchy jean that I have ordered fit fine initially, but after wearing them for a few hours they tend to stretch more and are difficult to keep up. So, I ordered these jeans a size smaller than my standard and I really like the way they fit. The material does still have the stretch, so they are not binding. The legs are wider than I am accustomed, but I am still good with the look. At 5’6” I really like the 38” inseam, as the pants leg breaks as intended.

Never know what you're gonna get It’s hard to find the right fit because I’m 5’2”, 30” inseam. Inverted triangle shape maybe? with a fuller waist. Because of this, when I order online, I get different sizes so I don't have to return and reorder all the time, but.... It’s exhausting. Normally, I wear a 6 or 8/28 in high waist jeans and 4 or 27/28 in lower rises. S-M in leggings. Inseam depending on style 25”-32”. All orders below I ordered 32” inseam and none were that. I included pics of 2 black (can't see1 of them and hard to see difference in color) and one of the ice blue.I ordered:4 (S) Ice Blue Fit was oh k, but afraid it might stretch to be too big because fabric is stretchy.6 (M) Ice Blue Too big all overThe small is more like a size 6. The fabric was decent quality, the color is a very light blueI ordered (XS) Lapis longing blue The fit in the butt was ok but bordering on too tight and uncomfortably tight in the waist. The color is med. dark blue with feathering …fading.. bleached? in the butt and on the front thighs. The fabric felt cheap. Thin, kind of crunchy or stiff but stretchy.I ordered(S) Always Black too big! Bigger than the 6(M) the color was very black, the fabric different from all the others which I kind of expected, but ok and the quality was good for the price. I liked these and was happy I had ordered the XS assuming they would be the winner or so I thought….(XS) Always Black, when I tried these, the fit ok except that the fabric felt thinner and the color had a slightly washed look to them and then the length was shorter than all of them! UGH!!! So frustrating!All sizes were shorter than the stated 32”. They varied between 30 and 31. So bummed because I liked them for the most part. I could accept the lesser quality if I could find one that had the qualities of three put together fabric of the ice blue, the smaller size and color of the Always black and then the right length of 32"! LOLMy favorite fabric and fit were the ice blue. Always black my favorite color, so I will try one more time and order XS 32” inseam Ice Blue and Always black and pray that’s what I get. I’ll try to update.****UPDATE:Ended up order the “Always Black” only in XS/32”So disappointing and frustrating. What I got was extreme faded black looked like they had been washed and again the inseam was not 32”! The last 3 photos are the newest pair! I mean come on!😞

Good fit lightweight denim When my husband and I sold everything we owned & moved to Mexico, I packed on almost 30 lbs. We overindulged in all the delicious food here, and stayed in Airbnbs for months with no access to a gym. As a result, none of my clothes fit anymore, so I needed something I could wear that was at least as flattering as possible given the current state of my body. Since my size 10 jeans were too tight, I decided to get a 12 in these. In the photos, I weighed about 180 lbs, 37 waist , 42.5 hips and they were the tiniest bit snug, but the spandex in the denim allowed me to confirm to my body instead of giving me muffin top. Since I'm 5'7" with long legs, I ordered the 32 inch inseam. The length is perfect for a low wedge heel on me. I love that these jeans are lightweight with some stretch. So much better than the stiff denim from the old days (I was born in the 70s, raised in the 80s). The denim back then was thick and so stiff if you gained an ounce jeans weren't wearable anymore, and even if you could squeeze into them, they dug into your skin and left indentions and red marks. This more modern, stretch denim is so much more comfortable to wear, and the lighter weight fabric is more bearable in hot climates like Mexico.
