Keeping but there are things you should know I read all the reviews and went into this knowing that these are not high quality. What gave me hope was the curvy girls I saw that loved them. For reference I'm 5'5", 36/27/40 measurements, 138lb. The good: I don't have a gap in the back. (this never happens!) The length is perfect. (also never happens) They are a little stiff but mostly comfortable. My thighs and butt have room to breathe! The bad: The distressed look on these is so tacky. I'll try to mute it by using dye. The seams are awful and could need reinforcement in stress areas. Look at the belt loop placement. Couldn't be bothered to make them symmetrical. The hardware is cheap looking and you'll need to rub a wax candle on the zipper to prevent sticking. Overall if you're athletic build/curvy these are so worth it for the cut alone. I very rarely find pants that fit me. I want to cry. When these inevitably fall apart, I'm going to take them apart and sew a higher quality copy of them with fabric that has stretch. This is such a relief after years of skinny jeans and manufacturers that pretended we didn't have bones, muscle, and fat on our bodies. With those very human things, you can also have a small waist! I'm mostly happy and glad I bought them. Took two stars off because they won't last long and I shouldn't have to dye them and resew the belt loops right out of the box.

3 1/2 stars These jeans fall between 3 and 4 stars and I don't think I would buy again. The good: The fit is great. I am 5' 7" 135lbs and normally wear either a 4 or 6 in other brands. The small in these fit nicely. They are a little tighter around my waist than I'd like, but I do have a pretty big waist-to-hip ratio (menopause belly!) However, they are stretchy so are wearable. The length is perfect on me and they are wide in the leg without being super-wide and baggy like some brands I've tried. The bad: The denim is low quality and the fabric is stiff. I have washed and steam-ironed them to soften them up a bit but they are still pretty stiff. Also, on the pair I received, the distressing is uneven. I ordered the deep blue color and there were three parallel white lines that looked like scratch marks on one leg. Luckily, I have a deep blue felt pen so I lightly touched up the white lines and now they aren't visible. I am sure I will wear these but they won't be my go-to jeans.

Cheap-ish Fit me well, nice color, soft, even if somewhat in the thin side. However the seam at the very top is scratchy and I don’t want to always have to wear a shirt tucked in. They’re also just long enough for me (I’m almost 5’9”), and I’m worried they’ll shrink in the wash. I’m afraid to will be going back as I’m at the point where I want things to work for me, or it’s not worth it.

I am disappointed that the second pair I ordered in the Pecan had white lines on the top. I think to make them look worn but it doesn’t look natural. In the ad photo the lines are not noticeable. I will be returning. Would love them if they didn’t have the lines. Very pretty color.

Large waist I am 158lbs @ 5'3", measuring 36DD-32-42, and I normally wear a sz 12 in jeans. I bought a sz 10 in these pants based on other reviews saying to size down, and I loved how comfortable they seemed at first. As someone who has a small belly, I wasn't bothered by the waistband being slightly large, and the waistband didn't roll. They are very soft, not too thick or thin, have good stretch, and fit comfortably everywhere else. I did wish that they had real front pockets. I was ready to keep these, but as I wore them while getting ready to go out I noticed that they just didn't look right with any of my tops, and gave the appearance of being slightly baggy in front. I also had the worry they would loosen more while wearing so I'm returning them. It may be worth it to mention that they didn't come with any brand tag or price tag, so it's possible it was a previously returned item.

Comfortable quality I'm 5'8" and about 180lbs. Most of my weight is in my midsection. I dont have much of a butt or thick thighs. I'm usually a size 12 and that's what I ordered. I did the try before you buy option which is a great benefit for sure. The 12 fit me about right, perhaps a smidge loose in the waist. These jeggings had a nice comfort band and the material was comfortable and a little thicker than I expected. It definitely is not a cheap looking or feeling pant. I sent it back because I didn't like how shirt it was on me. If it had been longer then it would have made a winter staple for sure due to the thickness of the material, which is a little heavier than I'm used to.

Great fit When it comes to clothes, its hard to know exactly what you will get if its not brands that you regularly buy, so I got these jeans with a small amount of hesitation. I was so wrong to worry, they are fantastic. I love the fit, they have some stretch to them, but not the kind that makes them look sloppy or too big, these are very fitted in a good way. The length is perfect for me and the rise is good. I normally only buy Lucky or Banana Republic jeans, so to get these was a risk, but honestly these are great. for reference, I am 5'4", 125lbs and normally wear a small 6 or roomy 4. I got these in a 4 because the description said they are stretchy and they fit perfectly.

Fitted and stretchy I really like the cut of this jacket. I am 5’8/235 and got a size XL. It hits just below the waistline and can button up if needed. It has a snug fit and wouldn’t work with anything thicker and T-shirt material - very tight in the upper arm area. I like the stretchiness of it, and the pockets are deep enough for a phone and keys. The fabric is super soft, but does show every little detail if you spill or get anything on it (including streaking from laundry detergent, which you can see in these photos). I agree that it has a strong smell out of the package, but washing it took care of that. It works well for fall weather, not thick enough to replace a jacket though.

I needed a new denim jacket to fit my current size. I liked this darker wash color and classic look. It is important to measure your bust, waist, and hips to determine your size; don't go off the standard sizes you buy in the stores. I saw some reviews from women who also bought an XL and claimed it was too small; and although their weight was slightly less than mine, they were also much shorter than me. I am an athletic body type with broad shoulders, but still have womanly thickness. In the photos I have on a wireless bra, but I dont think a push up would fill in the chest area too much more (38/40 C). Your true measurements are the key. If you get your correct size, your abdomen will have a straight line silhouette (like in my photo), but if you want to show your curves, a size down may do the trick. If you are hoping to wear thicker shirts or sweaters with the jacket, I suggest sizing up one or two sizes, because the arms are a form fit. The denim is light weight (perfect for TX), but not thin (great for "fall" and "winter" we have in our state). It has enough stretch in the fabric that you can unbutton the sleeve cuffs and roll them up as I did in my photos. My hips are wide, so I can only button the bottom button and the jacket still look decent. But I only want to wear it open anyway. Overall, this is just what I wanted and I am very pleased! I'm excited for the weather to cool off some more so I can wear it. If I find issues with the stitching or quality, or have issues after a wash, I will update my review.

Long enough sleeves for taller women I loved the stretch material this jacket was made of, and the fact that the sleeves were long enough for me at 5'10"! I bought the darker color in Medium and lighter color in Small, and ultimately decided to return them both just because I thought the way they flared right at the lower band was unflattering on my already bottom-heavy figure. It might have flattened out after washing, but I didn't want to take the chance that it didn't. The flare is right at the little flap-and-button seam, not just a crease from being folded in storage. My measurements are 35-29-39, and you can see from the pictures that both S and M fit me, although M would be better for layering over anything long sleeved or thick, while S would be a good fit for layering over a sundress or tank top.

Great! I've been wanting a jean jacket for a while, so ordered this one and the Levi's version ($10 more). I ended up keeping this one and returning the Levi's one. I'm 5'8" and 165, with super broad shoulders and a 38D chest. I'm normally between a medium and a large. I ordered a large in each jacket, and each fit, but this one is much more comfortable. The Levi's jacket is cut narrow in the shoulders, and has a more boxy shape. This one is broader in the shoulders and has a more tapered shape. The fabric is decently stretchy, and the jacket just molds to your body like a good-fitting pair of jeans. Looks super cute with jeans or a skirt and boots. The only negative I can think of is that the front pockets are seriously tiny, so small that my keys barely fit in there. (There are 2 inner phone pockets that are great. Can't live without a phone pocket!) But for $29 I'm thrilled.

Jean Jegging With Strech I bought the black Jean jeggings in 1X . Not the size that states , fits most . I went to the next size. My weight is around 165 - 170 lbs . My height is 5'3 . If you are slightly overweight, size 14 - 16 go up to the next size . People who are size 18 and above I would recommend the last size . The stretch is amazing. They are very comfortable .

Shorter than I expected I have a lot of pairs of leggings and joggers from this brand and I've always been impressed that they come all the way down to my ankles, because I'm 5'10" and have trouble finding pants that are long enough. So I assumed these would fit similar to the same brand and size of leggings and joggers, but they're a full 2 inches shorter, as you can see in the photo. If you're average height or shorter they'll probably be fine, but I will not be purchasing this style again. Other than the length I like them. I'm keeping them because I got them for a Halloween costume and don't have time to replace them.

the olive ones are reversible! (pics) I got the olive ones mainly because in the ad photo they looked like they had a subtle vertical stripe texture. In person, they do have that texture and it's very pretty, enhancing a long silhouette. But guess what? As I removed them I saw that they have more pronounced striping of varying widths inside. So I turned them inside out and put them on, AND THEY ARE SO CUTE! Yes, I'm totally going to wear them this way. The details from the outside of the pants (stitching around real back and faux front pockets, waistband, fly, rivets) all show on the inside as well, I honestly don't think anyone could tell they aren't supposed to be worn inside out. OMG, are other colors of this legging striped inside? I have been looking everywhere for striped leggings. Most designers seem to think that legging stripes should be horizontal--you know, so that you look as much like a chubby toddler as possible. OK, so, standard features that other people who aren't as stripe-obsessed as I am might care about: 75% cotton very long-- I got the One Size; I'm 5'8", curvy muscular 140lbs, but there is plenty of length, scrunching at my ankles beautiful shading so that it's not just a bland olive smooth- and sleek-looking super-soft and comfortable stylish design great value for the price did I mention that they're striped on the inside and reversible?!

Cute and Great Fit For sizing reference: I'm 5'5, 135lbs, 28in. waist, 40in. hips, approx size 6 pants and I ordered the OneSize. I didn't know what to expect for this cheap price, but so far I'd say I'm pleasantly surprised! I have a booty and I often have the "waist gap" problem with pants, but since these have the elastic waistband and are stretchy overall, they fit perfectly everywhere, including the waist. The material is neither too thin or thick and the pants seem flattering on me. I can definitely see myself getting more colors in the future, in both capri and legging lengths. I do agree with another reviewer that there can be a tendency to create a "camel toe", however, so that is an area for improvement.

Makes your booty look great! Awesome leggings! I loved the first pair I bought so much, I ordered 4 more pairs afterwards! The pocket placement, (as we all know is a big deal on women's pants) is very booty flattering and the material is thick enough that it kind of smooths out those chubby chunks. The waistband is nice and high to cover up my post-baby gut, and no camel toe either so that's a definite plus. The length is just perfect too, not ridiculously highwatered or too long like some other leggings. Time will tell if the material holds up thru a few washes but honestly for like $12 a pair? Who cares? I'll just buy more! I am 5' 7" and 157 lbs, so I was a bit concerned with the leggings making me look like a fat ars but they are actually quite figure flattering. 2 enthusiastic thumbs up!

Good for the price, not great. LIKES: These are very soft, very comfortable, and very stretchy, all of which are what they advertise and you want to see. I am 5’5” and have a much smaller waist than my hips so I also appreciate the higher rise on these a lot. Also customer service is great. DISLIKES: These aren’t the thinnest leggings I’ve ever owned, but I would describe them as delicate. In fact, if you are looking for something durable I would probably pass on these. My first pair came with a small hole in them already and I returned them for a new pair. I have had them for about 4 months now and they are pilling after some semi-regular use. All in all I like them enough to buy again for the price, but be aware that they are not built to last in my opinion.

It was a big mistake… What I got was awful. The same model, the same size. The black pair was from Mexica, the blue one was from Egypt. Sizing inconsistent! I purchased these in Ellis, Black, and Seattle, all size 6 Medium. First off, they're not very high rise- more like a mid-rise. My Levi's all have a much higher rise, so if you're looking for a true high rise, look elsewhere. Secondly, you're going to want to order a few different sizes bc they're pretty inconsistent. The Black pair were so tight I could barely get them over my hips! The Seattle were slightly looser, but still tight, and Ellis was just right! All were a tad long (I'm 5'6 145lbs). Going to return for Ellis size 6S and Seattle Size 8 Short. The Black pair had a cheaper fabric quality, more like a jegging but the Seattle and Ellis washes are ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ quality. Will definitely try those two washes in a Short!

Bought 18 Long. I'm 5'10", 210lbs, 39" waist, 32" inseam. They fit perfectly. Thought I would try out these jeans. I'm a guy but they fit very well. The longs have an inseam of 34" but the hem comes right across the top of my sneaker shoe laces where you would want them for a normal guy fit. Only complaint is the shorter front pockets. My phone sticks out a bit when I put it in them. Is it really that hard to put a normal pocket into women's jeans? lol Other than that I can easily wear these day to day. I'll probably get more.

Cute jeans I ordered these as a loose-fitting pair of jeans so I could wear to work comfortably. They fit the bill. They’re nice and loose in the thighs. The waist is a bit too big, but I usually wear a 0-2 so I expected this, I just need to wear a belt. Otherwise these are very comfy and I like the fit. They’re definitely a denim material, not a jegging- which I like. The rise comes to a little under my navel. They hold their fit throughout the day and don’t loosen up. I’m only 5’3 and they’re still a little long, even with the short length but not too bad. Overall, great for the price.

Any Gen Xs out there may understand these are "old school" jeans -- TO ME this means-- they hug your curves, they don't get baggy (like jeans with "too much" stretch nowadays) and the fabric is thick, soft denim. I put these jeans on as soon as I received them and I've had them on all day. They feel like a favourite pair of jeans I've had for years! I'm 5'10, size 14 (with curves) and these fit perfectly!

Cobalt Overboard goes all the way to ankle unlike description photo Im 5’9 169 postpartum and these are my first pair of postpartum pants/jeans and after just trying them on Im SO HAPPY (size 31 regular). Even though I ordered Cobalt Overboard the packaging says Marine Overboard but it’s fine because they fit beautifully and go all the way to my ankle which was a pleasant surprise because the photo makes it look like they’re shorter and have more of a capri look. I prefer the longer look actually and can just roll them if I need/want to.

I finally found some black levis that fit. These are the blackest black levis I have ever seen. They are soft and stretchy and they fit great (My butt is lifted up). As I mentioned in another review (Levis 311 skinny plus, kabekona camo), I currently wear an 18 in Womens Levis 505 which run larger than most levis so I sized up to a size 22 to be safe. I probably could have gotten away with a size 20 with these but they are so comfortable (I don't want to take them off). However, I did not have to hold my breath to button them or wiggle into them, they just slid on, so I'm happy. I will also have to wear a belt with these which is always the case for me. For reference: I am 5"5 with thick hips, butt, and thighs and currently weigh around 260 and my stomach is not flat but it is smaller in proportion to the rest of my body.

True to size, stretchy, size down if you are between sizes. long leg friendly I have been looking for some cropped wide leg jeans for a while and haven’t liked any of them. This is the first pair that I think works on someone with long legs, I typically wear a 34 inch inseam so some of the other cropped jeans look like an odd length on me… These hit in a better place. The fit is true to size, obviously I was hesitant to buy jeans that are not a numbered size, but size small S works for me. The denim does have a good amount of stretch though so if you are in between sizes, I would recommend sizing down because these have a good amount of give. The material of these jeans is a medium weight denim not too heavy, but not too thin, with stretch. This is good for summertime when you are on the go, comfy enough to squat down in. I would definitely recommend these, See photos for an idea of the fit. hope this helps you!

True Low-Rise fit I cannot describe in words how much I love these jeans! I have been searching high and low for a low rise jean with no luck. As someone with a short waist and very long legs, the high waisted styles that have taken over literally everything from underwear to yoga pants, have been preventing people like me from getting a desired fit for a long time.Yes, the waist is low, but not so low that the pants are falling down. The fit definitely elongates my torso, complements my hips, and still keeps my lung legginess intact. I love the double button at the waist, the boot cut fit, and the position of the pockets is just perfect in helping me to have a more visible behind. You can’t be the value, the material is a nice medium weight denim, and has the perfect amount of stretch to it.My only complaint would be the smell of them, which is similar to mothballs, but not worthy of removing a star for. I had the same issue with some lucky brand jeans I bought on Amazon as well. I imagine just to prevent moths from feasting on their inventory 😁 And a couple of washes, they should be good to go! I am 5‘5“, 150 pounds, and 55 years of age.

Not 100% cotton I'm 5'3", 152 lbs, 32" waist, 39" hips, and wearing a size 31. The waistband is about an inch below my belly button; to me, this is on the lower end of “mid rise”. The widest part of my calf is 15", and I found it a bit snug in that area for a bootleg. These were my least favorite of the six I ordered under Prime Try Before You Buy. All jeans I selected were (supposed to be) 98%-100% cotton, and these were described as 100%. However, they are actually 93% cotton, 6% polyester, and 1% elastane, which makes them an automatic fail on that front alone. After losing 125 lbs, I’ve found stretchy jeans highlight, rather than conceal, the loose skin on my thighs. While they aren’t as bad as those with a higher poly content, they can’t compare to the 98%-100% cotton jeans, and thus, are going back. The jeans I tried were: Levi’s Women’s Premium 501 Original Fit Levi’s Women’s 501 Original Fit Gap Women’s High Rise Straight Fit Wrangler Women’s Cowboy Cut High Rise Slim Fit Tapered Leg Wrangler Women’s Real Wrangler Classic Fit Mid Rise Boot Cut Silver Jeans Co. Women’s Suki Mid Rise Curvy Fit Slim Bootcut (Amazon won't allow me to post with hyperlinks to each item)

LOVE the look, HATE the butt seam These jeans are super cute on. I'm 5'1 115lb. Normally wear a 2 or a 26, but based on reviews and Amazon's recommendation, sized up to 27 which was the right call. Length is perfect with a small platform sneaker, would probably need about 1/2 inch shorter normally - the inseam is 31" and I'm usually a 30. Knee holes hit at the perfect spot. For all these reasons I wanted to give five stars, BUTTTTTT.... My gripe - the butt seam is HORRIBLE. It's fine when I'm standing and looks cute but it is so uncomfortable when I sit. Digs up my butt crack and toward the front, not sure how I am going to fix this, or if sizing up would help, but OMG, sitting in these is the worst.

Sizing is weird Update - I hot the 27 and they are literally perfect. I bought both colors but the one in the photo is the black. Comfortable and seems to elongate the legs Wife is 165cm or about 5ft 5 inches and weighs 66.70kg or 147lbs. camera mode is normal (not .5). Fabric and style: my wife likes it, as it can be trendy and formal depends on your top and shoes. The fabric is comfortable, lightweight, and doesnt need ironing (we just sprayed it with water and lay it on the bed for a few hours and hang it nicely). The thickness is just right too, not too thick and not the one that will get thin in just a few washes. stretchyness is not too much as a denim jean should. Size: the sizing is right when it comes to lenght and just a little small on waist. Wife's upper waist size is 36 and the demin holds her tummy nicely (with a little tuck or tummy in 😝) Price: we got a deal and with this brand, it's worth it as I use my shorts and pants from this brand for years.

Flattering cut. Here’s why I gave these jeans five stars: * the cut and stitching are very flattering to my pear-shaped body. * fabric has a nice weight to it but still maintains a lot of stretch. * they are very comfortable and hit me right at my waist. * the length hits right below my ankle bone. * I like the barrel style legs. * there is interesting stitching detail at the knees. BOTTOM LINE: These jeans fit true to size that are definitely worth the investment. I highly recommend these. - For reference, I am 5 foot two, approximately 110 pounds. These are a size Small.

GREAT Fit for my "APPLE" shape, Holding Up Well, Comfortable I'm hard to fit - I have the dreaded "apple" shape instead of the more common "pear" body shape. These Lee Plus-Size Relaxed Fit Straight Leg jeans fit. Being almost 6ft I get the tall variation. The fabric is not as heavy as the Lands End relaxed jeans I loved but are now discontinued (darn!). They feel a little skimpy by comparison and did not keep me as comfortable/warm in this exceptional New England winter as those heavier ones. These have elastic built into to fabric itself. The Lands End jeans had a sliding elastic panel. Different solutions and each worked well for me. One thing I like about these Lee Jeans is the pockets are deep enough to use quite useful. My doctor told me to stop carrying a shoulder bag (and my shoulder problems stopped!) so now I have come to rely on my pockets so getting pants with useful and deep pockets is a big plus for me. Besides fitting my waist they seem to fit everywhere else too. Maybe I should get the legs shortened just a little. If I get the regular length they are too short and as my mother used to kid me - I can't cut it longer! I've been wearing these jeans for about a year and I'm very happy. They seem to be wearing quite well with many laundry cycles still look quite good. They keep getting softer and more comfortable with each wash. They have been color-fast so I can just throw them into any load. No worries. I'm really pleased with these Lee Plus-Size Relaxed fit jeans. I don't have a down side for this review so I think they are a solid 5 star item.

They have been color-fast so I can just throw them into any load. No worries. I'm really pleased with these Lee Plus-Size Relaxed fit jeans. I don't have a down side for this review so I think they are a solid 5 star item.

Very comfortable and soft. Just a bit too big. It’s hard to know what size to order when it comes to Lee jeans and my normal size 18 is always way too small in this brand so I ordered 2 sizes bigger (22). They are comfy but I am going to order another pair in a size smaller (20) because there is quite a bit of stretch in them (see pic). Also, I am just over 5’7” and the long are too long. When I re order I will not choose long.

Love these jeans - I'm just too short! 5'1 160 bought a size 32 31 1/2" waist 44" hips I bought these jeans in two variants: A New York Moment and Ringing Bells, both equally beautiful! *pictured is Ringing Bells* Unfortunately, these jeans are just too long for me (even with boots on), but I love them so much I'm having them altered! They fit so nicely and give you curves in all the right places. They've also got a nice stretch, so I may have even been able to go down a size! They are also very high-waisted which is a plus. Definitely recommend, but if you're shorter, just keep in mind that these pants are VERY long.

Great jeans I bought the 29 in "Bells & Whistles". I am 5'6. My inseam measurement is 29. These jeans say the inseam is 32, but they do not touch the floor when I am wearing flat shoes. My waist measures 30 and these fit nicely. I weigh 143 with most of my weight at my stomach. Overall I am very pleased with this purchase.

LOVE!! I am absolutely obsessed with this jeans, they have a stretch to it and lifts your butt up a little bit! For reference i’m 5”3 120 pounds. I got a size 25 and they are perfect!!! I would get a 26 if you like them a little bit looser on you!

LOVE!! I am absolutely obsessed with this jeans, they have a stretch to it and lifts your butt up a little bit! For reference i’m 5”3 120 pounds. I got a size 25 and they are perfect!!! I would get a 26 if you like them a little bit looser on you!
Love them Love these jeans. Comfortable, cute, and high waisted like advertised. For reference, I’m 5’7” and 172 lbs, and bought a size 32.

Soft & Stretchy Jeans These jeans are super soft, comfortable and flattering! They have a lot of stretch all over, making these jeans ideal for any occasion. They hug my curves, accentuate my butt, prevent love handles, and have a slim fit all throughout. The waist sits right below/at my belly button, which is good because I prefer a mid-rise (super high rise jeans on someone my height/with a short torso isn't great). Because the waist is extra stretchy, I can tuck a shirt in without it feeling tight. I can be bloated as hell and still feel comfortable! The one main negative is the crotch area. When I sit down, the fabric gathers, hardens and points outward in an awkward way (see photo). I absolutely despise the way it looks, but these jeans are so awesome in every other way that I can overlook it. The length is just about perfect on me, which is rare! The shorter inseam is ideal for those 5'-5'3". Because these jeans can expand so much, I can most likely afford to size down. I do have to keep pulling the back up after sitting/lying down. There's plenty of extra room in the waist area so maybe I should've sized down. I usually wear a size 2 Petite in pants and these feel roomier. If you're not sure between two sizes, I would size down, unless you want a roomy waist. Overall, for under $35, these are really nice jeans, and I would get them in other colors/styles. The fabric is lightweight so they're not ideal for wearing in really cold weather. But they work for the hot weather here. They look good, they feel good, and they don't break the bank. What more could you ask for?

Best fitting jean I've found in a long time! I was a little worried about the sizing of these jeans but read through reviews and was told to order 1 size bigger than your usual since it's junior sizing. I'm 5'3" about 155lbs and typically buy size 8-10 so I ordered size 11. They fit very well, have extra room with stretch. I hope they don't get too loose with all the stretch they have. The length was spot on. They are extremely soft feeling also! The only downside so far is the front color is slightly lighter than the back. I feel like the picture portrays a darker color. Over all I'm super happy with the fit and light feel of these jeans. Not sure of the quality yet since they're brand new. I would guess because they're made of stretch and light weight material they won't last super long with frequent use.

Not long enough I ordered the 9, or “9 regular” like it says on the tag. These jeans would be great but they are too short. I am 5’6 with long legs and a shorter torso and they barely reach my ankle bone. If these came in long they would be perfect. They’re also not tight enough around the ankle. Soft and great fit This is my first time ordering from this brand. I’m 5’6” and 127 lbs. I usually like my jeans a little lengthy so that they bunch at the ankles. I normally buy a 30 inch inseam jean to get that look, but since regulars were 29 inches in this brand, I went with regulars, not long, fearing they might be too long. These come just below my ankle, so I will most likely wear them with booties versus tennis shoes. I will have to see if a pair in long is too long or not. The fit is great, the fabric is soft, and I like the rise on the jeans, as I have a very small belly button to crotch length. I don’t like my jeans to bunch up in the crotch when I sit down, and these do not - yay! The lengths were all consistent between the three pairs. I lined them up by the back of the waistband, and the lengths were exactly the same as you can see in my picture. May need to use a belt with them, as I had a little extra room in the waist — again that’s my narrow hip issue. The fabric is very soft and comfortable, maybe just a tad thinner than other stretch jeans I have owned. I really love them, so hopefully they hold up during regular washing and use. I am either a women’s 2 or 4 in other brands, depending on my winter/summer weight. I could see going down to a size 3 in these if I lose my usual 7-10 lbs come summer. I love these and overall I am a happy camper!

Difference between pure and non-pure versions I ordered these jeans in a size 9 in both Amal and Amal Pure colors. I already ordered the jeans in a size 9 in the color Lake and loved how soft the material is. I wanted a lighter color and chose the two Amal colors to compare which one I like better. Both of them have too much fading for my liking so I returned both. Also, the stitching is a bit off and is more noticeable on these jeans than on the Lake pair. The Amal color has the wallflower logo on the back and stitched within while the Amal Pure does not. The Amal Pure has a plain black tag within and less decorative back pockets. Also, the Amal Pure has tiny front pockets while the Amal has more normal sized front pockets that you can actually fit your hands deeper inside. The Amal color jeans are slightly lighter than the Amal Pure. In my pictures with them side by side, Amal is on the left and Amal Pure is on the right. In terms of fit, I am 5'4", 140ish lbs, 38" hips, 29" waist. I usually wear a size 7 in juniors and a 4 or 6 in women's and the size 7 in these jeans was a tad too tight so I kept the 9 in Lake. I love how soft these are but they do slide down when walking around extensively or when getting up from sitting. They are more like leggings than jeans. UPDATE: I wrote my original review in Fall 2018. It is now July 2020 and Wallflower instasoft jeans are the only jeans I wear since they are soft and stretchy and don't bother my sensitive skin. I still have my original Lake and they are a little less dark but still in great shape. I also have and wear regularly Shannon and Hayden all in a size 9. The Lake seems a little tighter than the other two but all are softer than normal jeans. I always wear these with a belt since they are super stretchy. But overall, they have held up to constant wear and look great which is surprising since the material is thin and my thighs rub.

So comfy I normally can wear a 12 or 13 in pants. I just had baby number 3 seven months ago and haven't lost the baby weight. I have to have mid rise jeans to kinda hid the mom pouch and these are perfect. They sit at the perfect spot without being too high or too low. I ordered a size 15 because it says it recommended a 13 or 15 based on reviews. I am about 5'5" and ordered 15 long. I am not super tall but I can't stand pants that don't cover my ankles so I wanted the length. I do cuff them once but they look fine that way to me. I just don't like short skinny jeans. These have stretch but not so much that I am constantly stuck pulling them up. They are very comfortable and look great one. I just ordered a second color in the same size and length. Will update review once I receive those.

Super cute. A little Tight The style is super cute! I’m 5’3” 127-130lbs. Normally a size 4, but it is semi tight in the waist. I would advise sizing up 1-2 sizes. I bought the black pair. It’s a soft black/greyish.

Love these - a little stretchy and very cute and comfortable My husband gave me the thumbs up on these -- so that is a pretty strong recommendation in my book. But I am loving these jeans!!!⭐They are a little stretchy so they are super comfortable. They are denim (not legging material) but they have a little bit of 4-way stretch to them. I don't feel that they are squeezing or squishing me and even when I squat down, they give a little. The waist isn't cutting in and when I sit down the jeans move with me (and the strings of the cut out don't cut into my legs or cause weird fat rolls).⭐These are mid to high waist. They aren't mommy jeans, but they do come up to my belly button so they fully cover my tummy. When I sit, they don't slide down. As a fluffy mom, I hate when belly rolls pop out over the top of jeans -- no such issues with these.⭐SIZING: I am 5'5" and usually wear an 8/10 (L) in pants. Because these are jeans and I have a little muffin top, I decided to size up one to be sure I would not feel squeezed in the waist. I went with a 12 and they are 100% perfect. I can wear these all day. They aren't slipping, but nor are they uncomfortable or tight in the waist. Love them!! For me, they are standard length jeans, going to right below my ankle. No adjustments necessary.⭐Zipper w/ single button front; Pockets - one in front at each hip and one in back over each bum cheek.I love jeans, but my fluffy body doesn't always agree with the fit. These are great -- comfortable, forgiving, a little stretchy, higher waist, and pretty flattering in my opinion. I 100% would recommend!!

Real jeans; no stretch material (spandex) thin or light weight I have been looking for REAL jeans since 2006 when everyone started adding spandex so they stretch out and fall down after an hour of wearing. These are thin or light weight so they are not stiff like mens jeans tend to be. They sit on the hip, waist line so they are great with a short sweater or t shirt. They are "stove pipe" legs, which I love and Express used to make. 100% great jeans! The medium blue (true blue jeans color) is what I ordered and I just ordered 2 more in case they stop making them. I am 135lbs and 5'3" and I bought medium. MAYBE...an inch too long but I wear heals and when I put them on the jeans just brush the floor at my heal. I saw a shitty review for these but bought them anyway because it said they were thin and I was looking for thin (I HATE thick, stiff jeans) I will probably order a few more and put away for safe keeping in case I never find good jeans again

Surprisingly cute but not exactly like the Amazon photo These are the second pair of jeans that Amazon has surprised me with, they’re a great value for the money. In this case the size chart seems correct. I ordered small, but perhaps the x-small would fit, but these are not baggy pants, so much as barrel leg, or full leg pants.These are fun jeans. I love the look of the jeans both with the legs worn down, or cuffed for a more casual beach comber look. These jeans fit well, due to the stretch. I ordered another pair that were genuine denim, with no stretch and ordered my regular size, I was hard to even pull. them up, let alone button them. These are comfortable, and the stretch makes them fit well with some size flexibility.I am 5'8" and 130lbs. I usually wear a 4-6 or size small in online sizes, I wear a size 27 or 28 in jeans, and usually a 4 in women's and a 5 in juniors all US sizes. With the chinese clothing it is highly recommended that you look at their measurements and size guides and if there are reviews, read them for info on whether to size up or how to interpret the size chart. There shouldn't be a size problem any longer, since US sizes for clothing and shoes is standardized as are EU sizes, have been reliable and specific for decades for clothing and shoes.In this case I ordered according to the measurements chart and chose size small. These jeans are baggy in the leg and fit in the waist and butt. They are stretchy enough that they pull up over my hips however I do not have much of a butt. My measurements are Hips 38, waist 30, right now. I gained about 10 bls recently, usually I am a bit smaller.The length almost reaches my ankles. I like the cuffed look, but like the option for leaving the legs down. I am okay with the length. I like the mid rise style better than high waist, so I may tailor these to be mid rise, that will make the legs a more desirable longer length. Inseam is 28.5" My inseam is 30 to my ankle, 31 to top of foot.4 stars.

Comfortable, accurately sized I’m petite and usually prefer form fitting jeans. I ordered these on the whim of trying something new. I’m happy I did. These jeans fit true to size. I ordered them in XS being that I’m 5ft 108lbs. The length just grazed my heels and the waist fits comfortably with the option of wearing a belt to fit higher in the waist. The fabric is thin and slightly stretchy. It’s durable as there won’t be a lot of stretching to these jeans due to the bagginess that gives plenty of room for movement. They’re not exactly flattering on the derrière as it gives a flattering appearance but it can emphasize a narrow waist by the way the pants flair over the hips onward. It can be chic if accessorized properly. But it also has the versatility to look relaxed and youthful depending on what one pairs it with. The price seems accurate when comparing to other Jean brands. I would be up for ordering in another shade.

Stretchy soft comfortable denim, okay to size down from the size chart due to the stretch I’m 5’, 115 lbs with zero curves (waist 32 1/2”, hips 36”). I ordered a size 12 for the 30 1/2” waist knowing that the jeans would be baggy in the legs and rear and long but didn’t mind since they’re a baggy style. The denim is so stretchy that the waist fit comfortably without showing any muffin tops and the length was fine with tennis shoes. No hemming needed. I machine washed them (cold water) and machine dried on low and the jeans didn't shrink in length or width. If in doubt or between sizes on the waist measurement you’re safe to size down because the denim will stretch. Highly recommend these high rise comfy slouchy jeans.

I ordered a size 12 for the 30 1/2” waist knowing that the jeans would be baggy in the legs and rear and long but didn’t mind since they’re a baggy style. The denim is so stretchy that the waist fit comfortably without showing any muffin tops and the length was fine with tennis shoes. No hemming needed. I machine washed them (cold water) and machine dried on low and the jeans didn't shrink in length or width. If in doubt or between sizes on the waist measurement you’re safe to size down because the denim will stretch. Highly recommend these high rise comfy slouchy jeans.

Didn’t love the hem 5’6” 136lbs, normally wear a size 4 or 6. Ordered a size 4. I like these jeans in most ways except the hem and the somewhat unflattering backside. It just didn’t do much for me. They’re not the cutest jeans I own, but I can put them on to run to the store and feel like if I run into a neighbor I look ok.

You may consider sizing up due to waist Update: I originally wrote my review saying I didn't like how these look but I have changed my mind! I have them on with a cute summery top and some sandals and I have to say I like the way they look! Cute, casual, something I'd wear to coffee or for errands--which, I am about to run errands now and feel pulled together but effortless, because it was effortless. So glad I sized up because the waist is nice and loose in case I eat something makes me feel really full :D ha. In the photo I took to show you how it looks, it doesn't really show it as flattering as these are when you pair them with the right top and shoes--and again, that's when I want a comfy and casual look for errands or coffee, maybe lunch. ------- I usually wear a size 4 but I sized up to a 6 since I wanted a more looser comfortable fit for around the house and maybe errands or possibly coffee out with a friend. I hope the pics will help you to have an idea of fit and style of these jeans. I will note here that I don't find them to be 'stretchy' jeans but likely they will loosen up even more after wearing a while. Fit: For reference, I am 5'2" about 116-117 - my body type is 'curvy/hour-glass' but fit - I'm often called 'a tiny woman' though I see myself as curvy and powerful. Take in to consideration your body type. I have a small waist but if you have a different body type where your waist isn't small in relation to your hips, these may feel constricting at your waist. They come to just right under my belly button, where I like high-waisted pants as I don't like super high waisted. I am glad I did size up since while the legs are wide leg, the waist is 'nipped in' quite a bit so that it fits tighter--the size 6 allows it to be looser and comfortable around my waist area, while the size 4 would have felt too constricting to be loose and comfortable. Would the size 4 have fit me? Yes, but, again, not in a nice loose comfortable way that I wanted. Actually, I laughed when I unrolled them as they looked a bit comical with the wide legs and tiny looking waist (they come rolled up in plastic, which I hate that - it's why I am not always keen on ordering clothes from Amazon - no tags, in stinky plastic, ugh). Length: These come to just below my ankles. My legs are relatively longer than my upper body but I am still short so if you are short like I am, then these aren't going to look cropped. I didn't mind the length. Material: The material is soft and pliable. It's thick but not in a stiff way. It does have 55% cotton but I washed and dried mine on normal cycle so the water was warm and the dryer was very warm, before trying on, as I do everything because I'm just that way. I don't know if they shrank, but if so I believe it was minimal. These did have a chemical odor to them. Not overpowering but it was there, even somewhat after washing so I'll need to wash them twice. Another reason I hesitate ordering clothes from Amazon. Details: The front pockets are 'slit' pockets and I was surprised at how deep they are. The back pockets are the small 'patch' pockets. Other than that they are minimal in design, in a good way. Button and zipper fly that seem sturdy and well made. Stitching looks good. These are sort of style of 'mom jeans' but not really ...? I absolutely do not like the mom jean style and wish that had not come back but things will come and go - I am in my 60s so I've seen a lot of styles come and go! I do miss lower rise jeans as they fit with my shorter torso! But these in the size 6 don't constrict and I achieved that loose comfortable fit I wanted and they aren't too mom jean looking. Color: I almost ordered the darker pair but these light blue are nice too. A little retro. All in all they will be a good pair of jeans to wear when I want to feel comfortable. I wouldn't order another pair though.

Super cute pants! These jeans are so nice! I love that they are hemmed but they still fit me well, which is an accomplishment considering I am very petite and have a hard time finding that combo. I am 5'0", ~125lbs, 29" waist, 41" hips, typically size 4-6. I got these in the color black in the size 8 - I chose to size up to be safe and I hate clothing that squeezes me. These are nice and stretchy but they do have good structure. The pocket detailing is unique and fun. The length is perfect if you are petite like me but I imagine they would be shorter on taller people. Overall, love these pants and will be buying more colors!

Stylish and Comfortable These are exactly what I was looking for. Most come in flare but these are wide straight leg which is what I wanted. I’m 5’6” 125 lbs and usually wear 2-4. Cute jeans I really like the fit of these jeans however I wish they were one size smaller they feel a little loose around the waist… A size 4 was the smallest, but an easy fix if I wear a belt. The length was short on me so I got a little creative and undid, the 2 1/2 inch stitching at the bottom to make them longer! I am 5’7” tall so length is important, especially when I’m Wearing shoes with a higher heel other than that they’re really really cute. The material feels really nice.

Fun front pockets! These ASTYLISH jeans have some fun front pockets - so in style right now. I love the wide stitched hem on the legs too. As far as being high waisted, on me they were high in the back and fit lower in the front, not like my usual high waisted jeans, that fit around my true waist. I do have a long torso, so maybe that had something to do with the fit. For reference, I'm 5'6" 120# with 30" waist and 35.5" hips. So a size 4 hips, size 10 waist - buying jeans can be a challenge!! Since these had the stretch, from experience, I went with size 8 - the fit was perfect! However, had they went around my true waist it would have been a tight fit. I took a gamble and it worked out. I also found the inseam to be 28.5" after washing in cold and drip drying. I usually prefer a longer inseam, however for the rest of summer I'll be wearing flat slide sandals or low sneakers, so they'll be fine. Make sure to scroll down in the product description for size chart, not the one on top. I'll enjoy wearing these through the end of summer, early fall. Cute!

Flattering and comfortable Comfortable, flattering stretch jeans. Form fitting but not tight. The fabric is deep black with no weird chemical smell. They appear to be well-made, and the fabric feels like jean material. I have washed them in cold water in the washer with other dark jeans with no problems. I also dried them for 10 minutes and then hang to dry. They look and fit exactly as they did before wash. The fabric smoothed out after washing and the seams stayed in place with no twisting of the leg. They are a bit long and I need to wear heels. I usually buy petite or short jeans. I am 5’3” tall, 150 pounds, 41-31-41 and the size 14 fit nicely. The legs are a bit tight near the knee but not uncomfortable. It is the style of the jean with the flare legs. The flare is not too big. I love them.

Perfect inseam for those under 5'3 The taper in this Amanda jean means that for curvy ones, you'll need to go up a size. With an hourglass figure, ample bottom, hips and thighs means I'd need to go up a size. If you're figure is straight, this will fit. Pear-shapes, depending on thighs may need to size up, and so will apples. However, the one thing that I did learn and appreciate was finding the perfect inseam length. Approximately 25" give or take. See the pics. The inseam is nowhere near 29". ShortPetite is the length for me. The jeans, however, are too tight and going back.

85% Love It I really like this jacket….mostly. I love the color, and it does look and feel like real denim. I like the length of it as it fully covers the booty. Now what I don't like is that it has a drop shoulder, and it feels really big and loose. I read a lot of reviews saying to size up if you want a boyfriend-jacket type feel and size down if you want it to be a little more snug or form-fitting. I just went with my regular size (2XL) as I didn’t want either of those things. I just wanted it to fit like a regular jacket would. But it still seems to be really huge on me and the with the drop shoulder, it can look like I’m swimming in it. I had to roll up the sleeves by a lot so that the sleeve isn’t hanging off my hands. BUT - it really is a cute jacket and goes with just about everything, so I’m keeping it. For reference I am 5’4”, 220lbs and regularly wear a size 14/16, depending on the brand. I can wear an XL or 2XL but generally wear a 2XL.

Comfortable, Roomy Jeans with Some Stretch I ordered these jeans in size Medium in the color Medium Blue. They have a recycled look to them. They are cut roomy in the thighs and legs, which I find comfortable. They are made of 76% Cotton/26% Polyester/2% Elastane so they have some give to them. After laundering them three times, they haven't noticeably shrunk. For reference, I'm a long-waisted/short-inseamed 5' 4", 124 lbs, 29" waist and 38" hips. The length of these on me is at the longest side of OK. I think I could have ordered these in a size small, as they are a little baggy and the waist slips down a bit if I don't wear a belt. They have a strange smell that hasn't completely washed out after 3 washings, but I suspect it will eventually. Overall, I like these comfy jeans and I think they look kind of boho stylish.

Perfect Jeans for Texas cold weather Was afraid the fleece would be too much and make the jeans fill stiff and hard to move in. I’ve worn them all day chasing around a one year old and they have been amazing. I bought “Yehopere Women's Winter Fleece Lined Jeans Slim Fit Warm Skinny High Waist Denim Jean” size M. I normally wear size 6-8 in jeans. I’m 5’7, weigh 150-165 pounds. I’m not skin and bones. I wouldn’t call myself curvy but as I said I did have a baby a year ago and I’m surprised how by how much I really do love these jeans. For the price, they’re a must for Friday night football games sitting on cold metal. The back pockets are also fleece lined and the pair I bought has all working pockets, none are. Fabric really does appear and feel like denim on the outside. There is a good bit of stretch to them as well.

Great quality, runs small These jeans look great and are really cozy -- I love them! The lining is soft and very warm, and the fabric has good stretch. The fit is medium-high waisted and surprisingly skinny for jeans with a thick lining. Judging from other reviews, I'm guessing sizing varies depending on color and style. In light blue, they definitely run small. I'm 5'3" 135 lbs and usually take a medium, but I ordered large because I wanted them to be comfortable and to fit over a base layer in extreme cold. Good thing I did -- the large fits perfectly, with no room for a base layer (fortunately, I won't need one, because the lining is plenty warm). The bottom hits my foot, so if you're any shorter than me, you'll need to cuff them. These are going to be my favorite winter jeans for spending time outdoors.

Wish the material held up more for washing I've been searching for ages for a pair of high waisted jeggings, and I finally found them! These are just the right blend of fabrics to make it not too stiff (like pants), and not too thin (like leggings). I was worried because the reviews said these are fleece-lined, but the ones I got definitely aren't. (They were referring to the longer ones possibly?) I'm not a huge fan of the buttons down the front, but hey, I'm not going to complain considering these are the only pair of high-waisted jeggings I could find on the market (with pockets in the back AND front no less!) Regardless, this is probably one of the best purchases I have ever made. Will certainly be buying again! (For reference, I am 5'0 and 110 pounds and I bought a small.) Edit: I've had these for a couple weeks now and the material is beginning to pill. Love the look, but wondering if I should return these due to the fabric not being able to hold after washing.

Love them but size up Item received 2 days after order. These jeans are soft and stretchy and super comfy. The fleece lining is warm but not bulky at all. The back pockets are lined with fleece but the front pockets are not. The quality is excellent. I am 5' 4”, 120 pounds and normally wear anywhere between size 6 to 10 in jeans depending on the brand. I ordered a size 10 in these and they fit perfect, until I washed them and dried on medium heat. They shrunk about half a size, 1 inch in length and in the waist. Since they are stretchy I can still wear them, but they were more comfortable before washing. It is hard to say if the next size up would fit better or be a bit too big. I am including (terrible) pics of before and after washing and drying. They are not as dark in color as the pics look. Excuse my poor editing program and skills. Just trying to show the difference in length. The price at under $40 is amazing. I paid more than that for flannel lined jeans over 10 years ago. Highly recommended, but you may want to size up.

I am amazed with how comfortable and such great quality craftsmanship!! First and foremost, these are true to size with a little extra stretch for those who adjust weights now and then. These have a looser fit on me, but not in a saggy way, but not hip huggers. No issues with bunching or pinching, making it uncomfortable, or any friction when trying to rush around. I have full-sized pockets, I can fit my huge cell phone in any pocket I want, and sit down!! The fabric holds its shape without slipping down throughout the day, I wear these even with the looser fit without a belt. Even on the second-day wear!! The legs are nice and long, so I can cuff the bottoms to have a stylish cuff on the bottom, or let them stay long with strong bottom hems. The length is perfect for me too, barefoot they hit the ground with ¼ inch to spare. That is the perfect length for me, I am 5’7, about 165. The fabric is nice and thick in denim, the lining is secure the whole way down the legs without any separation. The stitch-work is solid without any loose threads and the button holds tight so I don't have concerns about it slipping out like some other stretch jeans.

Wonderful Jeans! I was shocked at how much I liked these jeans. Ordering clothes online is always gamble. These jeans are awesome. They are soft, comfortable, stretchable.... The jeans are high waisted, which my aging body appreciates. The pockets are easily accessible, both front and back. I am between sizes at the moment and went with the larger size. I probably could have gotten into the smaller size since they stretch, but these are so comfortable. The length is long for those of you that are short. I am 5'8" and the jeans puddle on the floor. I can't remember the last pair of pants or jeans I bought that did that. Since they have a fringe bottom, the extra length can easily be remedied if needed. There is a slight flare to them, but they are definitely not "bell bottoms". I would highly recommend these jeans.

Not a Fan 🙁 For reference I, 5’8” tall and 135lbs. I ordered a size 8. I’m Not a fan for several reasons: 1. Too much polyester - 23% and 2% spandex makes these way too stretchy and no real form. If you are looking for jeans with structure that will tame lumps and bumps and smooth your thighs, these are NOT the jeans. 2. Odd length for me at 5’8”. They aren’t long enough to be long, and not short enough to be wide leg crops. If you hem them then you lose the frayed raw hem. I do like the light blue color, and like the frayed hem, but that’s all I can really l8ke about them.

Very cute For sizing reference I am 5’2” and 138lbs, I purchased these in a small. All clothing is washed and dried according to manufacturers instructions prior to reviewing. These jeggings are so cozy. I was a bit worried they would be overly warm, but that has not been the case at all. The inside is so soft and going to be perfect for fall. They are a true button fly and I love that! It’s such a great fashion detail. They have nice stretch to them, but due to this, they aren’t the most accentuating of the backside. They do have great length to them for those who are likely taller then I am. I gave each leg a double cuff to have them sit at my preferred length. I was a bit surprised to find the waist a bit lose. It’s not overly large, but if you are much smaller then my stats, these might not work for you. Overall, these are great and I can’t wait to wear them in the fall and winter seasons!

You guys!!??!! Come on!! You shouldn't have...now everyone is gonna hate me. Listen, I didn't ask them to, I didn't know about it in advance, heck, I didn't even know they were aware of my existence. But obviously, Gap did & does. They went ahead and designed a pair of custom jean leggings made to my exact measurements. Now, even though I plan to buy every single pair of size 6/28, I decided it would only be fair to share my stats with you as well as the stats of MY custom made, comfortable, flattering & 100% perfect fitting jean leggings. Me: 140-145# 5'3" 33.5" waist (measured at the bottom of my bellybutton because that's where MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS sit) 40" hips (measured at the widest point, mid-bum) MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS (Measured flat) 9" rise 14" waist 17" hips They have a REAL zipper fly, not one of those "just for show" kind commonly found on jean leggings. Every pair I've tried in that past with a "fake fly" always had extra fabric that bunched up at the crotch & made me look like I was smuggling oranges whenever I sat down (& they didn't look any better when standing up, either). The traditional 5 pockets are fully functional (real) as well. The stitching/color are absolute perfection & the plain, proportionately sized back pockets are what my denim dreams are made of. If you have time pls check out the pic I attached to see another one of the reasons why these fit so well. I hope my humble opinion helps you in some way. Thank you for reading.

First wash improves fit. Lots of waistband stretch, a belt may be useful. The first wash really helps the fit on these jeans - GAP Mid Rise Legging Pant - Dark Willoughby - 31 Regular. I tried them on after delivery and the legs were super tight (never had that problem) and the waist stretched to huge. Fortunately, after the first wash and dry (warm and medium), the fit was back to normal. I wear a 31 in all jeans and these are to size. However, there continues to be lots of stretch at the waistband, I notice significant droop as the day wears on. An initial high-rise becomes a sinking mid-rise, a belt may be useful. As a positive, they can handle generous weight fluctuations. I like the Dark Willoughby. I’m 5”5” 135lbs.

On trend, fits well First, let me state that I have a tiny mirror so my pictures aren’t great. I am also 65, and you can judge for yourself whether I’m too old for this trend. My philosophy is you are almost never too old, but you do you. I purchased the Frida in a 26, which is my normal size and they fit perfectly. I’m only 5’3” and 100 lbs, but have longer legs, so while these are a bit long I like the length. They don’t drag on the ground with casual shoes but can still be worn with heels or boots. I like the front seam detail and the pockets are roomy for women’s denim. I’m loving this baggier style, although I think since I’m small I’ll wear something tighter on top rather than a blousy top and baggy bottom. Live and learn no matter your age. They are super comfortable. I’m usually ready to put sweats on when I get home-COVID ruined me for real clothes, but I can wear these relaxing. I am, in fact, resting on the couch wearing these as I type. Another good buy from Amazon the Drop. I would say this is my new favorite place to get denim.

Comfortable, Attractive & Edgy! I ordered these IVIR Women's Wide Leg High Waisted Cargo Pants in size Medium and color Khaki. For reference, I normally wear about a size 8 in jeans, and these pants fit me fine with some roominess to allow for freedom of motion and for shrinkage. The fabric is medium lightweight. Its cotton-soft hand-feel and slight stretchiness make for a very comfortable wearing experience. I like the wide-legged cut of these pants and all of the totally useable, actually deep-enough pockets! These pants are very high waisted (above my belly button prior to laundering). I am 5'4", long-waisted and short inseamed. Before laundering these trousers, I wore them with cowboy boots to absorb the excess leg length. They are extremely comfortable to move in and have an edgy-stylish vibe. I even received unsolicited compliments on how these trousers look. I recommend them!

Good quality, good fit, good purchase for busy mom of 6 I like these jeans quite a bit. I broke my foot and wanted something that would fit over my orthopedic boot, and these did great. But I also found them to be very flattering. I'm 5'7" and about 200 lbs, usually a size 14-16, with a slight pear-shaped body and I carry most of my weight in my belly (Mother of six, any surprise?) The 16 fit me nicely, and I don't think I would have been comfortable in a 14. The jeans are fitted through my thigh and knee and open up below that. They just skim the floor when I wear low sneakers or flats, so they can easily be worn with some heels and boots as well. The waist is the definition of mid-rise, coming just above my hips and about to my belly button. After a couple days of wear, they loosened just the tiniest bit, which added to the comfort but didn't have to be hitched up constantly. (That's important to me as a mom!) I got a darker wash, which I like but they kind of turned my hands blue before I washed them. I definitely recommend washing these separately the first time. These aren't "Oh my gosh! Best jeans ever!" But they are very good jeans, and I don't regret the purchase at all, especially at this price, and with the option of Amazon wardrobe. I also tried the Amazon Essentialls mid-rise authentic bootcut jean and preferred these because of the fitted upper leg, which the "authentic" style lacked.

Close but not quite right I liked the color of the faded black and the length was good for me at almost 5'8". What I didn't like: The fabric was not comfortable at all. Flattering pants but sizing chart is incorrect I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

Very disappointed that this didn't fit me how i'd like, they're so comfortable! Okay, so I bought these pants because I needed them for a work/volunteer gig. I had high expectations because I have had other Lee pants and I really want them to fit properly. I'm 5'3 and 110-113lbs, not exactly the curviest person around. I ordered a 4-Short. So...they do fit at the waist. Perfectly, I might add. Not tight, but definitely not loose. Very comfortably, exactly like how I think a pants should fit. However, they are both VERY baggy on my relatively skinny legs and a little bit long even though I bought the short version. When I put it on, I loved how comfortable they were, but disappointed how baggy and unkept they looked on me. I would get a 2, but I doubt the waist would fit. For my short queens (those sub 5'3), you might want to look elsewhere. I have a feeling they'd look rather long on y'all. They are otherwise brilliant pants and great Lee quality. Such a bummer.

Great buy. I bought two pairs of these a size 8short and a 10short. I am 5’3” and a half and the length of the short size is perfect. I’m not telling you my weight, sorry. I normally buy size 10 jeans at Old navy and in Levi’s. While both sizes fit me (8 and 10), the size 10 is much better for work. The waist is a bit tight on both sizes, but this loosens during the day. The pockets are functional: the two pockets in the front and the pockets in the back. This is a big deal because way too many women’s pants assume we don’t need pockets which is so annoying. I bought the size 8 in dark gray and the 10 in tan. I’m pleased because while I can wear jeans to work, it is always safer to wear actual pants. These are not too stuffy, cheap enough to buy multiple pairs and I can just wash them with everything else. The material isn’t too thin. These are pretty nice. I wouldn’t Hesitate to buy more when I stain these.

Fantastic pants. Let me start with stats: I'm 5'6" 150lbs (athletic build, or pretty straight up n down build but spreading with age 😭so maybe I'm an apple now, lol. Anyway...), a 36DD Bust (usually M top,large in button ups typically) , a 29"waist (usually size 6 in jeans/pants or 4, brand makes a big difference there), and a 31" inseam. I got these in a 6 and if say they for well. There's plenty of stretch to the very soft fabric making these comfortable and flexible. They definitely just fit me comfortably in the waist line (no belt needed) any tighter they'd be uncomfortable. these also are definitely midrise pants which at my age I prefer. The length is perfect for flat or low heel shoe. I washed and dried these and did not notice any shrinkage. They came out softer and ready to wear. My use for these is for camping. Great value. These seem like great everyday pants as well. Breen color is pretty. Brownish green. True to product picture. I would also say these are a they're straight leg pant. These for over my ugh boots snugly, over a hiking boot nicely and with athletic shoes without dragging. I highly recommend these at this price.

Nice Leopard Print The leopard jeans are a nice variation to the common plain regular jeans. The leopard print is what I like the most about the jeans. The shape of the jeans is different from my usual skinny jeans and especially the legs and the hip feel a little boxy. I am 5'2" petite, 140lbs, 31" waist and 43" hips, and size 12 fits just right. If I gain a little bit more weight, I won't be able to fit. The jeans feel fine from my waist but there are some creases that form in the front, also the pants are too long but for somebody taller this won't be an issue. The fabric feels a little bit stiff and rough compared to regular jeans but after wearing them for a while they get softer and adapt better to the shape of the body. I wash the jeans in a cold regular cycle and dry them in the permanent press cycle in the dryer. I steam them to remove wrinkles. The pants didn't shrink and preserved the colors after washing.

I got a size 12, but at first I thought there was no way I could zip them up. I forced them closed and was able to button and zip them, but these are not a true 12. user_comment: Need smaller size Update: purchased size small and they fit great. They wash and wear well. I own multiple pairs and wear them all the time. They hold up nicely. They are very easy to move in and I enjoy wearing them to the park to play with my toddler. They are also nicely made and good quality. I think ladies with a curvy bottom half (like myself) will enjoy these! The accommodate your butt and thighs in a flattering way. The waist doesn’t dig into me or cause muffin top. They don’t bag out or sag during the day in size small. Original review: I’m 5’4” 130-135lbs (pear shaped) and normally wear a 4-6. The size medium was too big. They felt like they where going to fall down during the day and the crotch was baggy. Overall just not a snug fit. They look ok in pics, but def are not snug enough. Like the color and style. They came up to cover my belly button. Def a true high rise! Will need a size small! They have a generous amount of stretch. If between sizes I would size down. I’m sometimes a small and sometimes a medium in leggings. More often than not a medium in leggings. They are what I would call a jegging. They aren’t as thick as a true Jean material, but not so thin they show all your lumps and bumps. I like that they have actual pockets on the back. You can wear them on their own with any shirt or also as a traditional legging with a tunic or longer top. They don’t look like leggings so I believe the black or dark wash (or whatever conservative colors) would be appropriate to wear with a nice blouse and dresser shoes for work or a nice restaurant. In my opinion at least, for most occasions.

A lovely pair of nice, thick, warm leggings! Based on the sizing charts I bought these leggings in a L. For reference I'm 5'6 approx. 145lbs, generally a M in tops and bottoms, a 6/8 in jeans. The waist measurements provided made me think I should prob err on the side of caution and go up one. These "work" but I may have been slightly better off with a medium as the crotch is a little long and baggy if I don't pull them absolutely up as far as they will go. But otherwise, these leggings are nice and thick and warm and the fleece lining is divine in these colder months. I do maybe wish they were slightly thinner so I could wear them as a base layer to some jeans or athletic pants for hiking, sledding etc, but as-is they will just be a nice pair of leggings to pair with boots, sweaters, and tunics!

Order 1 size up if in between sizes I ordered these because I needed a black work pant and I am so glad I did! I am between a 31 and a 30 size wise, and ordered the 31. They fit PERFECTLY but there is no waistband stretch so if you are comfortably a size 31, then order a size 32. I loved the fit, the cut, and the pant length. Also, it goes up right to my belly button, so this is ideal for a business appropriate pant and can accommodate tucking in a blouse. See (unflattering) pictures for front and side perspectives. These hit right at my ankles (for reference, I am 5'8) so I didn't feel like they were high waters like the other pair of dockers I've owned. Material is soft and smooth. 98% cotton, 2% elastane based on the tag. Value on these? Awesome! They retail for $58 and the price was $39 on Amazon.

A little big I ordered my usual size. I should have gone down a size but I do like the cut. These jeans have quite a bit of stretch. I wore them around the house for an hour and they did loosen up with wear unfortunately. What I like best though, is how the waist band is cut. It cuves in the back so it sits flat on the waist, no gap. The sides curve up a bit so there is no muffin top (can see in the pic) and the front sits about 1 inch below the belly button. I am 5'3 and ordered the short length which is listed as a 31in. inseam. They are how I like but some might still find the short too long for them. To wear with tennis shoes I will have to cuff up the back or they will drag but should be perfect with a platform. Update: I decided to return as they were just too baggy. I tried the Elyse in the same cut in a size 20, too big, then a size 18, too big. I switched to the infinity cut and those fit how I wanted.

Inconsistent colors, sizes weird, fabric issues For context, I’m a 41 year old female, 5’4”, 175 lbs, recently lost 45 lbs and somewhat athletic build. I’ve had 4 c-sections and have a bit of a belly and the ol’ caboose is a little big (not overly though). Originally, I ordered the 13/14 (waist) x 30 (inseam) in the Dark Indigo. I’m short, but the 30 inch inseam was about 2 inches off the ground - higher if I bent or sat down. Too short for my taste. Legs were too tight for my liking throughout the thighs, calf and especially the knees. The waist was too tight as well. The first time I tried them on, the waist was okay-ish, but when I went to try them on again for a double check, I had eaten a small lunch and they were extremely uncomfortable/restrictive. Amazon allowed me to exchange them for the same pair in a different size, so I ordered and just received 15 (33 inch waist) x 32 (inseam) which luckily, was right before I was leaving to return the first pair (cuz these ones are going back with them). Fabric is that thinner, saggy-type stretch where if you wore them for an hour or two they’d start drooping. The first ones I ordered were a sturdier, stiffer jean material which I prefer over super stretchy droopy jean material. The waist line and lower abdomen or ‘Fupa’ area is too poochy. Length is better with the 32 inseam. A little longer than I’d prefer, but something I’d be fine with if the rest of the jeans were decent. One of the biggest issues is the color. The 15 x 32 are waaaay too light to be ‘Dark Indigo’. In the photos I provided, they are the pair on the left. I wouldn’t have kept them even if they fit because I don’t dig light pants on me, hence why ordered Dark Indigo. Definitely inaccurate description (photo on Amazon doesn’t match either). The 13/14 x 30 on the right, I wouldn’t necessarily call Dark Indigo but the wash is really flattering on my frame. Hope this helps.

Comfy jeans I'm often hesitant to try jeans online but the sizing on these was thankfully alright. For reference, I am 5'5" and about 155 lbs right now. I got these in a size 8 which is what I typically try, but these days I might be a 6 to a 10 depending on the company selling the jeans. It's hard to trust jean companies anymore even when the give waist/length sizes, because pretty much nobody fits each parameter perfectly. Anyway these are very comfy and all pockets are open and functional. Gotta say I like the two forward pockets especially as most front jeans pockets you'd be hard pressed to actually put anything in. These actually fit my small phone in a pinch and do fit a wallet or other small object. The blue is nice and the pre wear on the pocket doesn't look like it will rip out too fast. It's tolerable unlike most pre wear/shredded types that you know are going to give you issues in cold weather or become functionally an issue. The flared bottoms aren't my typical style but they're nice. I've already gotten a few compliments on them. One potential addition for the company would be either a double button or a snap plus button. I'm a bit concerned the main button could eventually come out as its placement seems a bit tight on the waist. Otherwise though, I really like the feel of these.

Very Comfortable Jeans! These are a really comfortable pair of jeans. They are in the more current style, being on the shorter side and flared. I have to say I really like the fit. They have a good bit of stretch to them, so the size 18 fit me fine, and that's my usual size for most jeans. I like the color, which is a bit lighter, but it is still what I would consider a standard jean color. They are relatively high-waisted, but due to all the stretch, they are not going to "hold" you in the way jeans with stiff material will. I like the length on me, I'm 5'6 for reference, and they hit just at my ankle. My only pet peeve is that, basically, the entire pair of jeans is standard with no distressing, except for a random tiny spot on one of the back pockets. I think because this is the only spot for any distressing, it looks more like an actual tear or rip than intentional distressing. It's not a big deal, but that's the only thing I don't like about these jeans.

Stretchy, thin material, great price point. Just got these, so this is based off of unwashed straight outta the bag. I bought an XL. I am 5'9" and between 170-180 lbs bottom heavy. Big tummy, hips, thighs and butt. They are comfy and stretchy. They fit just above my ankles. I would definitely order more if they had Jean or black color in my size. The material is fairly thin. I have a lot of cellulite dimples and they definitely show more in these white pair than a denim colored pair I received through a different retailer. The denim pair are also heavier material than these white ones I purchased on Amazon. I go commando and I feel like you can almost see through them. I certainly wouldn't wear any type of colored panties underneath. I think some nylons underneath would help smooth some of the dimples. Over all I think these are pretty awesome for a $13 pair of pants. I've played more for pants I've liked less. I think the top you pair them with is gonna be what makes the difference if you are curvy.

A cheap petite’s dream? These are $10 pants, but are still cute, while they last. I am 5’3” and typically a size 6. If you are petite, you will be thrilled. It appears a lot of women didn’t notice the inseam is only 27” long. So, if you are over 5’4”, these will be short on you, and you may like that or style them by rolling them up. I like the placement of the pockets on my flat butt. The front pockets are fake. The waistband is nice and stretchy and I can pull on over my pooch. I wore them for about 7 hours today and they’re not uncomfortable, however, look at photos so you can see how the rips wear. The strings coming off are nylon, not denim so they ball up. I will just trim them. I shared photos of the stringy balls, the slits, and my outfit. My daughter thought I looked pretty cute. I paired these with booties, but would look cute with flats too. I would order more, but they’re sold out now. I do wonder how see through they would be in white... these black are pretty thick though :) Will wear them until they look like $10 pants.
