Wonderful quality! Order one size up I chose to try this Levi's Women's Round Buckle Belt in Wheat Loop as a new accessory for my high-waist jeans and skirts. I love that it is 100% leather, as it will last me a long time. I chose size 1X as I normally wear a size XL in pants and wanted to ensure I had extra length on the belt (as they tend to run a bit small). I should have gone with a size XXL, as I am using the 6th and 7th belt notches. I would order one size up when ordering this belt, but not two sizes like I did. For reference, I'm 5' 5" and 197 pounds and wear a size 16 in bottoms.

Enthusiastic Approval. Triumphant! I am so incredibly happy with this product, I had to take pictures and do a review. I don't do this for every purchase (I couldn't possibly) but when I have an experience this positive, I have to acknowledge it so I give credit where credit is due and also provide other shoppers with information to help them with a decision. I bought these pants because I was searching everywhere for pants to match this bright purple blazer I had bought and it is just impossible. I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

More legging than jegging I'm 5'2 and 160 lb (which i carry all in front, not in my hips). I bought a large. As you can see, they are a little long on me so they bunch up a bit at the ankles. They are also really more legging than jean - clingy, thin cloth (but not see through for me). My husband said he didnt like the shape of these pants and I was like "Uh, thats the shape of my legs!"But I do love the choices of colors. I love the way this pair coordinates with the tunic I'm wearing and they arent too warm for warmer weather. I do wear this outfit to work, so I may consider getting more to coordinate with other tunics, but I'm not sure.

So comfortable!! I bought these for work, where I am required to wear khaki pants/shorts. The fact that they have functional front and back pockets was a HUGE selling point for me. I am 5'3 and between 125-130lbs. My waist was 30" at my time of purchase so I could not decide between small or medium because according to the size chart, I am at the upper spectrum of small (25 - 32) and at the lower spectrum for medium (28 - 38) and I really do fluctuate between those sizes depending on the brand. I ultimately went with small and they fit nicely with absolutely no squeezing of my body. I am so comfortable at work and because of the texture of the fabric, they look like form fitting pants rather than leggings. As you can see in the photo, the pants are too long for me, so I will stick to the capri style. I've worn them once a week for the past month and although I have followed the directions for washing and drying, the fabric is starting to look like its going to pill so I am not sure how much longer they will look nice. If I order more to wear outside of work, maybe I'll try hanging them to dry and see if that helps.

Love them! I'm 5'7, 155lbs., I got an XL, based on the size chart. They fit great, look great, very flattering, I would not wear them with my shirt tucked in, they still have a leggings crotch look! I read the reviews and they mentioned the opacity of the fabric, I can see what they are talking about, it doesn't bother me with the dark blue denim, it's because they have spandex in them. If you want that solid color, these do have that lighter color that shows through a little! They sit above my belly button, which I love! They are very comfortable, feel like jeans, nice deep pockets, love the jean look! I can roll the legs to make a cuff for more casual look, or leave them down to give a more dressed up look! I really like the fit and comfort of them! I have added photos to show how they look on, and close ups of the fabric for you to see that it's not a solid color, and can see what others have said about that! For the dark denim blue, doesn't bother me!

Don’t buy these, even if you have no pants at all. Being naked is better than these. Fit is really really REALLY bad! Don’t buy these unless you have tried a friends on and they look fabulous on you (even then probably don’t). This fabric should never be made into pants, or any clothing really. It is soft when you first pull them out but after trying them on and walking around the house the fabric already had pills where my thighs touched. Normal wear and tear on these will destroy them. I’m only going to be able to wear these in public with a long tunic or something to cover my waist. Need a belt because the fabric gets so loose. Good luck with these! Seriously save your money for a nice pair of actual denim pants. These should not be sold to anyone else! Believe all the other reviews, and mine, about the poor quality. I would have returned them but missed my window because I went out of town. So now they get a poor review. I’m 5’5 150 and medium/long was the right length but no way were these going to stay up on their own around my waist. Gaping and frumpy front. NEVER BUY THESE PANTS!

Looks like a Gucci This AUROLRAN belt feels top notch - reminds me of a Gucci. It came with a nice pull string pouch to store it in, love those for keeping my belts looking nice. The gold buckle is not a bright yellow gold, but rather it has a darker richer hue. The buckle slides off, so if you do have another belt this width and style you can change it up a bit. I love the punch buckles for this reason. The belt has plenty of holes for sizing ~ I got the 28-31 for my 30" waist and it fits great, plenty of room if I gain an inch or two. Lol Super happy with this belt. It's going to look good with jeans, dresses, sweaters, wherever & however I decide to wear my belts!

So soft and comfortable First, I was really surprised at how soft these are. I tried them on and then kept them on (I usually wash everything before I wear). They also didn’t have the weird chemical smell that a lot of clothing has. I really love the fit, and the long pockets are flattering. The pants have a baggy barrel cut which is generally not flattering on me. However, you can see in my third photo that you only notice the barrel cut when I pull the denim out to the side on one leg, but the other leg lays flat. They also fit true to size. I am 5’6, 140 pounds and size 6 fits perfectly. I got these with a 20% off coupon and a flash sale, so the value for the price is fantastic.

Super Cute But SUPER Stretchy I’m 5’5,” 165lbs but carry excess weight in my tummy. My waist is currently 36/37” and per the brand’s size guide provided I went with the sz 18. I’ve never been a sz 18 - I’m typically sz 14 or 32/33 waist in Free People jeans, but I love these jeans and wanted them to work. Turns out they fit! But due to how incredibly stretchy these are I definitely could’ve sized down to a 16. I still love the way these look and they’re a great dupe for the FP barrel jeans I’ve purchased in the past (and a lot less expensive). They did shrink some in the washer/dryer, but after a few hours they tend to stretch out again. I received these through Amazon Vine, but I love them so much I’m purchasing on my own a pair in a smaller size. Plus, at the time of writing this review (8/23/24) they’re on sale. Will update my review when I receive the smaller size. I do recommend these jeans, but keep in mind the stretch factor. The quality is excellent and I especially love how lightweight these are. It’s hot and humid here in South Florida, but these are jeans I could sport year round.

The Angel's Forever Young 360 Bootcut Jeans are perfect for my everyday lifestyle. I purchased these jeans as I have gained weight and all my other jeans cut into my waist and had less give than these jeans. The waistband and denim are soft and thick with enough stretch for comfort without losing its shape. After all we all need a little support when you are as curvy as I am. These do not change your shape like butt lifting jeans do. I will be doing more squats for that...lol. I would buy these again in a different color. I bought a size 6. My measurements are waist 37.5", booty 44", hips 43.5" and inseam 28.5". I could hem them, but I like that my ankles stay warm when I squat.

PERFECT length for my height My info: I am 5'9", 215 lbs, long legs, short torso, large tummy, normally a size 20 OR 2X. I had originally ordered the 20 Plus Long but they were baggy on me under the tummy and lower butt areas and the waist & legs areas were really loose, so I return those and ordered the 18 Plus Long. They are still a little baggy under the areas I mentioned above, which I would have to wear a long tunic to cover these areas. The waist & legs are more fitted but the length is PERFECT!! These are a true LONG as they reach exactly even with the bottom of my ankles. The fabric, IMHO, can be worn in ALL seasons; it's thick but not heavy thick, similar to jeans. The only negatives I have are, I wish there wasn't a zipper & buttons AND I wish they had belt loops. So in regards to belt loops, I will take these pants to a fabric store to match or closely match these fabrics so that I can sew belt loops on these pants.Edited to say: As you can see, these do not lift the butt!

💗 Love. Love. Love 💗 I love these pants! I recently lost 141 pounds! At 50 years old that's very hard to do. So because I have a budget for everything in our household I had to purchase 5 new pairs of pants and 5 New tops or shirts. Like I said I have a budget, I don't receive deeply discounted or free anything for writing a supposedly honest review. No I am the old-fashioned type that spends her husband's hard earned money to get what our beautiful family needs.😀 They do run small so I did need to research every review board and website that sell these very specific pants or the specific type of pant that I wanted. I wanted a triple stack three-button tapered soft Jean or pant. These are so wonderful! I get compliments on them everywhere and because I have a finite amount of funding for my clothing budget I plan on purchasing two more pairs that I can wear to any event we're going to attend. And I received compliments from my husband I haven't heard in a couple decades! He will probably not like that last sentence LOL. 😁 But very truthfully, these are great pants ....you just have to look at the size chart and go off the inches and not the size. But purchases, they are worth the money they do exactly what they promise to do, and you feel great when you wear them because they are so soft. I wouldn't hesitate in purchasing these for a second, however, if you do, please read the size chart , measure your waist and go off their size chart not what the US size chart is. Well done Hybrid pants!! So HAPPY!!!

Finally Long Enough!! Says the 6 ft woman. Having packed on the Covid 15, I was scared to get my actual size. I went with a 22, but now wish I had gone for the 20. At 6 foot, 265 pounds, it’s nearly impossible to find jeans long enough. I’ve been looking for the perfect pair of jegging‘s for over a year. These pants are thicker than leggings, but terrific. They don’t lift my bum, but at 55 I don’t care. They are super stretchy, Maybe if I had gone a size smaller, they would hold my stomach in more, but they don’t feel super strong. They are very comfortable. AND LONG ENOUGH! I will be keeping these for my fatty days, and will order another one in size 20, hoping for more tummy support. I love that they are truly high waisted (over my belly button), have four real sized pockets, and come below my ankles. I highly recommend. Don’t judge me on my pics, LOL.

They are very comfortable. AND LONG ENOUGH! I will be keeping these for my fatty days, and will order another one in size 20, hoping for more tummy support. I love that they are truly high waisted (over my belly button), have four real sized pockets, and come below my ankles. I highly recommend. Don’t judge me on my pics, LOL.

Highly recommend. Stop flip flopping and just buy these! I LOVE these pants! Enough to deserve a thoughtful review. That said, let's start with the fit! The pants are advertised as butt lifting. I cannot personally confirm this, as i do not have a problem in that area, but i CAN confirm that the pants almost contour to your body. There's no bunching or baggy spots. The stretch is enough to provide a perfect fit but not too much that they become loose. I love the rise - I'm a sucker for high waisted pants 😍 These come right up about up to your belly button (which is my favorite height for high waisted pants). Super cute rise! Just make sure to measure yourself properly with this type of rise - there isn't gonna be a lot of wiggle room. I know my measurements well but i also try to keep in mind that a lot of women dont so i just want to put that reminder. Measure your waist at your belly button. Measure your hips at your thickest part. As for the length: I'm a little short - not quite 5'3 - so the pants are a tad long on me. However, if you're average height or like me and always wear heels, this wont be a problem. Plus, they're easily rolled at the bottom if needed. Over all these are very cute, comfortable and everything i waned. Black skinny jeans are a staple for me so I think I will be ordering another pair. I got a lot of complements on these (usually you don't get compliments on plain black pants lol).

Insanely inconsistent sizing. 3 stars for the pair that actually fit. I first reached out to the company on the 6th of January, to see if this could be rectified before I left a review using the email they had on the happy/unhappy card with my purchase. No reply. I bought 3 pairs, all the same style in a size 1. So, the very first pair I received are Amazing! The fit was excellent, the length, love love loved it. Those were the medium blues. Was so stoked to get the other two being delivered the following day. Thought I had finally found “the jeans” you just love to wear. Wow, just completely shocked at the inconsistency in sizing. The dark wash was fitting like a 0, a bit tight on my hips but I could still put them on. The light blue acid ones. Omg. I couldn’t even get them past my hips. It was like trying to put 00’s on and I was a bad can of busted biscuits. I’m 5’9, and 114lbs. You can see each measurement just getting smaller and smaller. Each stating they are a size 1. No, honey they are not. I now believe the other reviews. It’s just incredibly frustrating and annoying that even though they are the exact same style, just different colors, none of them are the same. I’ll update my review once they finally contact me.

I’m glad I didn’t size up. UPDATED: I added pictures I purchased the V3/ (high waisted with the 3 buttons) size 9/ in Dark Wash thinking they might end up being a little too small, but they’re actually a little loose (and very stretchy). So maybe the manufacturer updated sizing to be more true to size? (I see in the comments that a lot of people have had to size up one or two sizes.) Summary: Sizing? True to size for me. I’m 5’7” around 140lbs waist 29” hips 39-1/2” inseem about 32” and this size 9 will work even though it is a little loose fitting. High-Waisted? Yes. The pants sit above my belly button Butt-Lifting? Yes and no... They’re not fitted enough for me to notice any actual structural butt lifting, but I do believe the cut/ style around the butt is flattering (Sorry the picture isn’t better. Worth the purchase This is the 3rd color I've purchased from Hybrid and I'm very pleased with all of them (olive, wine & now khaki). They are high waisted, long in length (which is great since I'm 5'10" and it's hard to find long lengths) and they are stretchy. I'm usually a 10, maybe 11 is size depending on the maker bit I got these in a size 9 and they fit amazing so I would say they run a little big. I feel like they hold your tummy in and lift your bum to look amazing at the same time. The only reason I gave it 4 stars is because the khaki color in the listing is not what I received. The color in their khaki description is more of a taupe and the color I received is closer to their "tobacco" color. However, they fit great and I'm planning to keep them.

Stoked about my new post-baby jeans I am really happy with these pants. They look different in person but ultimately that doesn’t matter bc they work so well. I am 5’4” and about 160 pounds but what’s more important is that I just had a baby (1month ago) and had a C-section. I needed jeans that fit well and looked good but had enough stretch and went over my belly in order to not irritate my C-scar. These pants work well now and should still work for awhile as I loose weight. They are comfortable and make me feel like I look really good for just having a baby! So I am very pleased with this product and consider the quality and value for $ to be great.

BEST JEANS! EVER! Over the years, I've gone from living stretchy jeans, to essentially trying to LIVE in super stretchy, jegging type jeans. I don't have a lot but I want more. I'd love to look good while feeling extremely comfy, so I took a chance on these. I am 5'3 and have a 32" inseam but these are almost perfect as they are. They have a cute fit. I'm not crazy about the little stitches on the back pockets (seems unnecessary) but the fit is just cute and flattering! Style wise, I love that these look like regular jeans as they have real zipper and button closure, real pockets, but have the comfort of Jennings or pajama denim. I want these in a million colors!

I don't love the fit First off, use the size chart. I NEVER take a size 12-14 (waist around 29.5") and I'm usually about an 8. 5'4" and 145 lb. I trusted the size chart and ordered a Large (12-14), and they are a good fit at the waist and tight through the thighs. They seem to be the right size, although I find it really annoying how the jeans keep slipping down and I end up needing to hike them back up again. I think maybe it's because they're so tight at the thighs; it may be pulling them down at the waist. I'm not super crazy about these, and because of the falling-down problem, I probably need to wear a belt (ugh) and I won't be reaching for these first. They're so-so for me.

I live for the comfort! This is are a trendy choice for 2024. As someone who usually wears a size 9 and is 5'2" with a short torso, I found these jeans to be flattering and stylish. The mid-high waist provides a comfortable fit that helps elongate the legs, while the wide-leg cut and raw hem add a modern, casual touch. Given some of the pictures, I wasn't quite prepared for how weathered these jeans are, but for super casual wear they are comfortable and stylish. The shape is a nipped high waist with baggy "horseshoe" legs (they have a couple extra darts of fabric to round out the legs... as if you were a cowboy wearing chaps). I'm 5'8" and they're still long enough to not look like capris, so most women should have ample inseam to play with. I just got them and I've already worn them to 2 events! They look great with a belt + cropped tee, with either sneakers or ballet flats. Highly recommend.

Long jeans that don't work for most body types. I'll start with I'm 5'4 170 with curves. I first purchased size 32 as recommended by Amazon and the sizing chart. They were large enough I could pull them off without unbuttoning them. Sized down to the 31 which is the size in the pics. The fabric and the top part of the jeans is the only reason these jeans get 2 stars. The waist sits nice and when I sit it doesn't do the stupid gap in the back. I think the cut and sizing all works well until you get to the calves and the inseam. These are long. So much that I had on 4in heels and they still were on the floor if I dared wear them with the heels. I was nervous my heels would get caught in all the excess fabric of the calf area. I get straight leg but at 9in wide (when the thighs are 10 there's some taper) I felt like I was in the oh so popular JNCO jeans. So I tried with tennis shoes. Again my shoe could get lost in the leg causing a trip hazard. So I arolled them up twice which only caused more bulk. If you're not statuesque or planning on cutting these into shorts I wouldn't recommend. But if you're going for that emo hot topic 90's baggy look go for these, though Levi's does have a 94 throwback that may be better for that.

I recently purchased the Kensie Ella High-Rise Straight Leg jeans in a size 14, which is the largest size offered. For reference, I am 5'3" and weigh 195 lbs. I usually wear a size 16, but these jeans have a lot of stretch, so the size 14 fit me quite well. The fabric is soft and stretchy, which made them comfortable to wear. While I'm getting used to the straight-leg style, I do find the jeans to be flattering overall. However, I noticed some minor issues with the front, where the fit isn't as smooth as I would like. Additionally, the price is a bit on the higher side for what you get, which is something to keep in mind. All in all, these jeans are a nice addition to my wardrobe, especially if you're looking for a comfortable and stretchy option. Just be aware of the sizing and the fit in the front before purchasing.

Bad material, too short, poorly made. These jeans looked so well in the picture, I assumed they were thin and light, baggy, long (judging by how they looked on the model, who is tall). In reality, the fabric is unpleasant to touch, rough, has some stretch, too thick. The legs are way too short (I am 5’9” and have long legs, but this is 4-5 inches too short compared to pictures. I got size M based on the chart, where the waist was indicated as 28”, in reality it was 26”, because whoever made it did not realize that the distance needs to be form the button to the buttonhole, not form the edge to the edge, which was about 27.5 inches. Fortunately, the pants are high-waisted, and the band was at the high part of the waist, but it is not comfortable, that one inch would make all the difference. The zipper is sewn in in a weird way (see picture), there are loose threads everywhere, some of the seams are uneven, the pockets are badly made etc. I cannot recommend these, won’t even donate them.

Comfortable, a few issues I ordered these jeans in size medium in the black, grey color. I'm 5'8" 125lbs with a fairly straight figure, and they fit very comfortably. The material has a nice stretch to it and fits snugly but not too tight like some high-rise jeans do. I measured the rise at 10inches. The inseam was 29inches which is a little short for my height, so I'll probably wear them mostly with boots in the future. The cut is wide leg but has kind of a slight flare and the leg opening is 10inches. A few small issues are that the jean material doesn't go down into the pocket at all so when I stand up after sitting, I have to re-tuck the pockets. these are regular sized pockets, so that's good. Also, two of the three buttonholes are a little small and I have to kind of force the buttons in. Still, overall, these are good jeans and a cute and comfortable style.

I gave them three stars for being long Good points: Very deep black (and they come with a warning that the colour might rub off on lighter textiles!) VERY stretchy if that's what you want Nice and long in the legs. I'm 5'11" and could wear boots or heels with these Modern boot cut (more on that later) Drawbacks: The FIT: I always wear a 12 in Lee jeans and these were a bit tight. I could wear them if I didn't mind the muffin top due to the fact they are smaller than any of my other jeans and the "midrise" is very low (to me, at least). If you are looking for mid-to-high, really, like me, do not buy these. The STRETCH: VERY stretchy. The kind of stretch that snaps in to hug your body no matter what. And, I don't know why, but that sort of pants always seems to get folds in the backs of the thighs (the front, too, if they are loose, but these are not). They are also very stifling in the heat. If, like me, you live somewhere where it is 30 degrees Celsius much of the year, do not order these. You're welcome! (Looked it up, that's 86 in Fahrenheit.) The CUT: As I said, these are modern boot cut. Maybe I am really looking for flares. 20 years ago, what we called boot cut flared out right below the knees and looked great. These grab me right down to mid-calf (so everyone can clearly see how knock-kneed I am - if my legs were straight, I'd be even taller!). Then they flare waaaaaay out. I find that pretty ugly on me. I guess they could work with boots if you have small calves (I do not). They are indeed huge through the ankles, but I think they would be pretty snug over boots in the lower calf. Also, they are on the low side of midrise, which is another huge negative for high-waisted me. Hope this helps!

Better than expected I saw these jeans on TikTok and was worried about sizing as well as the jeans being barrel jeans (unsure how they’d fit). I am pleasantly surprised by the fit, comfort, and overall quality of these jeans so far! I am 5’3” and they are barely cropped if at all, and the size works well for me (I range anywhere from a 6 at old navy to a 10 at h&m). I ordered a size medium of these and in the Blue color. One thing I’ll say is the smell! These hearts are painted on with what smells like acrylic paint. It’s a VERY strong smell and honestly I’m a bit afraid to wash them as I’m wondering how the hearts will hold up! I will update after I’ve washed a few times on the smell and how the hearts maintain :) if they do hold up though these are a surprisingly cute purchase that exceeded my expectations!

Stylish & Comfy Wide-Leg Jeans. I got these jeans for my daughter & they look adorable on her. She especially loves how stretchy & super comfy they are. Sizing might run a bit small. She’s a size 6, which the description equates to a "small," but I decided to order up 1 size as a precaution & opted for the "medium." Thankfully, that was the right decision. They fit her perfectly. The high waist is flattering. They’re fitted on the hips & backside without being too snug. Although they’re wide-leg, they don’t look baggy, maintaining a stylish shape. These jeans have a sturdy zipper, metal button, & back pockets, giving them that classic denim jeans look. However, the material is thinner than your average denim & even thinner than other stretchy denim I’m used to. Despite the thinner fabric, you can’t see through them in the white color, so it’s not a major concern. My daughter is 5'7", & the bottom hem lands just beneath her heel, which makes them look long with flat sandals. However, they pair well with shoes that have a raised sole or heel, like boots & wedges. She did mention they’re a bit long below the zipper, causing a slight bunching when she sits. Fortunately, this improved after washing, as I expected since stretchy denim tends to draw up a bit. Although the care instructions advise against tumble drying, I did toss them in the dryer on a low, wrinkle-free setting & they held up just fine. Overall, these jeans are stylish & fit great. I’d recommend getting precise measurements & comparing them to the size chart, as you may need to size up. The denim is decent quality, though I’m a bit concerned about how long they’ll last given the thinner material. But mostly, she’s thrilled with how comfortable they are.

Comfy & cute I wanted some black barrel jeans without a high price. I’m pleased with these. They are very comfortable and look pretty good. I’m tallish (5’8”) so I wish they were about an inch longer, but they are good for this early fall time of year . I like the extra high rise and the big utility pockets on the front. The fading looks a little wacky on the butt but it’s not very noticeable. The denim is very lightweight but not stretchy at all. And I appreciated that there was no strange odor. I ordered my normal size, 8, and they fit perfectly on my waist. They are loose in the hip and leg as intended. I’ll get a lot of wear out if these! Hopefully they will wash well.

Heavy denim stretch waist look at model closely 5’7” 195- 200lbs Size 18 is too big because I thought the regular size 16 would be too small. Should have bought regular size. I liked: * Heavy weight denim 70% cotton. * Stretch 28.5% polyester and 1.5 % elastic-elastine * Loose fit in belly and legs * Not tight on ankles * Real pocket hold a cellphone iPhone 13 Pro * Not too hot - not too cold * Very comfortable on the skin after washing. I did not like the length. I know they are supposed to be at the ankle. They can be rolled up to look stylish. This is great for business casual days at work and for nurses who wear regular clothes but still need to do patient care. Nice to not burst out of pants while bending. I purchased mine too large. I was worried that the belly would be too tight. The elastine lets the waist stretch without being granny style elastic. I recommend offering your actual size. I would buy again. I will keep these because they are not jeggings and can still be worn baggy style. I recommend a figure defining top to give you a shape because the pants are baggy. I will keep these size 18 and order another pair sized to my regular size 16 and then style like the model. The first pair will become lounge jeans and work around the house jeans because they really are comfortable without being jeggings. I recommend them but - let the model photos be your guide to styling.

Fit and comfortable Purchased these cargo jeans because they reminded me of the ones I had in the 70’s, which I wore to death! I’m a 73 years young woman who is still young at heart 💜 For reference I am 5’3 @ 144lbs. I purchased the size 8(normal size)light blue color. These jeans are very comfortable with just enough stretch at the waist to allow me to breathe and not “suck in my pouch “. I love the fit and the higher waist. The back pockets are placed just right on the rear, the front pockets are nice and deep. As far as the length I feel they are just right for me. I don’t wear high heels just Sketchers and the hem falls just enough that they don’t hit the ground. The weight of the jeans are just right, not thin fabric. I am very pleased with my purchase. Haven’t washed them yet but I am sure they will be fine.Just a side note, IF you are young and don’t have a “pouch”, fit and trim, you might get by with one size down from your normal size. There’s nice stretch with these jeans VERY comfortable.

Keep slumping down! A bit mad I so hastily took off the tag on these, but my main issue is that they keep sliding down! I’m 25 weeks, 5’6, 190 lbs pre-pregnancy and usually a size 10-12 with 41-42 inch hip, so I ordered a large. Having worn under-the-bump jeans and finding that they were hitting in an uncomfortable spot, I wanted a pair of pants that go over the bump with front pockets so I can hook my work badge. Maternity pants with front pockets appear to be a rarity. These pants keep slipping down! The legs feel fine, although they run long so I need to roll/cuff them, but the waist is not staying in place. I feel like the bottom of the panel is constantly falling down to my crotch instead of staying above it. There’s no belt loops either, so it’s awkward to try to “hike up” the pants constantly. I’m not sure if sizing down would work, given that the legs are perfectly fine for me in the large, but these pants were not the 10/10 I was hoping for. Maybe they’re best for mid to late third trimester. Included a pic when they are first hiked up and another once I’ve been walking around to show the slump!

Not relaxed/loose enough to be called “boyfriend jeans” but decent for the price. I am 5’5” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 or 27 in jeans, medium bikini bottoms, and small in shirts. I was really torn which size to go with and decided on the medium recommended by the size chart. Let’s just go ahead and get the negative out of the way. When I think of “boyfriend jeans” I expect straight and loose legs but fits at the waist. The legs of these jeans are not baggy enough to be considered true boyfriend jeans. This pair in particular is quite a bit more fitted than the model photo. I would classify this style as distressed relaxed leg. Now let’s move on to judging the material. These are soooo stretchy. I wore them for a full day of running around town and they never lost shape or loosened up. They look tight in my photos but in real life the waist line does have some gapping at the spine and is barely touching my abdomen. Even though the size chart recommended a small, I am extremely glad I went with the medium. I think these jeans are stretchy enough that the small would have fit but they would have been super tight skinny jeans. The material of the light blue is slightly scratchy before a run through the washer. I don’t trust them to survive many rounds through the dryer but they did survive a tumble on the low heat/delicate cycle because I was trying to soften them up a bit. I will probably hand dry in the future to keep the fraying in tact. The paint splatters on the jeans are white and very much paint (not bleach) so they could flake off over time. Recommendation: Cheap enough to be worth a shot. The material is thin, stretchy, and ripped so these will make great summer jeans. Don’t expect them to be really loose unless you are willing to size up but keep in mind that the waist may be too big for you if you do size up. This pair of jeans just became my go-to for weekend road trips because they are stretchy enough for the car but also cute. I was comfortable and cool enough wearing them for a full day in the Texas heat.

Great stretch and so comfortable!! I love these splatter painted jeans. They were very big when I was a child and I love that they are back in style. These jeans come stiff and over-starched but one quick wash and they are good to go so don’t give up on them. It’s crazy that we pay for ripped jeans that look like they were in a paintball tournament but these are so worth it. These have a nice stretch to them and are very forgiving. They are a tiny bit big for my waist but they were the smallest size so I have to wear a belt with them. They come with the bottom legs rolled up twice but you can unroll them. I didn’t because I am 5’3 so the length was perfect when rolled for me. I am wearing a size small in the pictures and I am 5’3, 135 lbs. My waist is 27 in and my hips are 36. I would definitely recommend them.

soooo cute for the short girls! but they do have some faults… Please excuse my dirty mirror, but these pants are soooo cute and perfect in person! I really love them and they are embroidered and not printed on so they look high quality although the quality could be better for 50 dollars. Now while I like how they look, I didn’t give 5 stars because they do SHRINK quite a bit like some of the reviews suggest. I suggest sizing up one or two sizes for this reason. I am 5ft tall exactly and I am usually a size 14-16 in jeans. I usually would have gone with the XL but due to the reviews about shrinkage I went with the XXL. When I tried it on they were long and little loose on the waist so I threw them in the wash. They shrunk to the most perfect size! Unfortunately these pants are not for the tall girlies, again I am very short and you can see after shrinking they hit right above my ankles which is perfect for me, but not for taller girls. I am worried that washing them again may shrink them even more which would suck but hopefully this is all they will shrink. Definitely not pants you will want to wash often haha.

Best big-booty-big-thigh jeans eva! Fantastic fit. I’m 6’ tall 201 lbs w/30” inseam 34” waist 44” hips 28” thighs!!👀👀👀 I am ALWAYS in two sizes up to fit my thighs and butt. This leaves me so much gap and nothing to hold up the pants on my giant thighs. Add large calves, and you basically just walk around holding your pants up or wear a belt and try to tuck and conceal all the extra waistband. These jeans are for curvy girls with a waist—OR NOT. My friend tried them on. She carries her weight through the middle and is maybe one size up from me, was able to put them on and zip them up. I was wearing a 16 and ordered a 16. They went on like butter and fit every curve of me. Very comfortable and easy to bend, sit and move. They loosen up quick, so grab a belt after first day wear. They’ll need a hot-water tightening often. I went ahead and ordered a 14 in the light shade. Both fit so well, I’m debating the12 now. 🤔 Stylish? So many compliments already. As for stamina—how well will they hold up over time? 🤷‍♀️ I’ll try to update in a few months.

Had to exchange for a size up, then had to wash jeans for a perfect fit! I normally wear a size 5 in Juniors and a size 4 in women. I ordered a size 4 and the fit and length were fine but the waist was a little too tight. This was before I washed/dried them so I decided to return the size 4 and purchase a size 6. The size 6 arrived and I tried them on, the waist was fine but now the legs were a little big compared to how I normally like them. I decided to wash and dry the jeans so they can shrink and after that they fit perfect. They go right to the bottom of my ankle/top of my foot and they tight but comfortable and easy to get on. The go up to the bottom of my belly button like a good high waisted jean. The rips are perfect and the color is great. So all around attempt number 2 was great and i am happy with these jeans. For reference I am 5’7” and about 150lbs
